item.title: 基于语义过渡的互补推荐框架CAST
item.content:encoded:
CAST通过语义过渡捕捉物品互补关系，提升推荐效果。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为CAST的互补感知语义过渡框架，用于解决传统推荐模型因依赖稀疏共购买统计而误判虚假相关性的问题。CAST通过在离散语义代码空间中建模动态语义过渡，捕捉细粒度的语义依赖关系，并引入经过LLM验证的互补先验信息，优先考虑互补模式而非共现统计。实验表明，CAST在多个电商数据集上显著优于现有方法，Recall和NDCG分别提升17.6%和16.0%，且训练效率提高65倍。

item.title: AdaLeZO：提升零阶优化效率的自适应层感知框架
item.content:encoded:
AdaLeZO通过自适应层选择提升零阶优化效率。



摘要： 本文提出AdaLeZO，一种基于自适应层感知的零阶优化框架，旨在解决现有零阶优化方法在训练大型语言模型时存在的收敛慢和方差高的问题。通过将层选择建模为非平稳多臂老虎机问题，AdaLeZO动态分配扰动预算至敏感参数，同时引入逆概率加权机制以减少方差。实验表明，该方法在LLaMA和OPT模型上实现了1.7倍到3倍的加速，且无需额外内存开销。

item.title: 生物启发的色恒常性计算框架研究
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种生物启发的色恒常性计算框架，验证灰像素检测的有效性。



摘要： 色恒常性是生物视觉系统和计算机成像系统的重要能力。本文构建了一个整合生物学机制、计算理论和算法实现的生物启发色恒常性技术框架。通过重新审视生物色恒常性的计算理论，发现光照估计可归结为早期视觉中的灰锚点检测任务。文章在统一理论框架下重新解释了典型灰像素检测方法，并提出一种结合反射模型约束与特征学习的简单学习方法，实验验证了灰像素检测在色恒常性中的有效性及生物启发方法的潜力。

item.title: 算法催化热力学理论及其信息-热力学约束
item.content:encoded:
算法催化理论揭示了计算结构的可重用性与能耗关系。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于瓦特每智能框架的算法催化热力学理论，识别出可重复使用的计算结构，以减少任务类别的不可逆操作。研究证明，任何特定类别的加速上限由底物与类别描述符之间的算法互信息决定，并通过兰道尔擦除确定最小热力学成本。结合这些结果得出耦合定理，下限定义了催化剂在能量上有利的部署范围。该框架应用于仿射SAT类，并将现代学习系统置于统一的信息-热力学约束下。

item.content:encoded:
REGLU提升大语言模型去记忆效果，兼顾遗忘与保留。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为REGLU的新方法，用于解决大语言模型在去记忆过程中面临的遗忘与保留平衡问题。该方法利用表征空间的几何特性，通过优化LoRA的初始化和引入正则化损失，有效减少对保留数据的影响，同时提高去记忆质量。实验表明，REGLU在多个基准测试中优于现有方法，具有更高的模型实用性。

item.title: 基于加权损失函数的医学报告生成模型训练优化
item.content:encoded:
加权损失提升医学报告生成数据效率。



摘要： 本文研究了在医学报告生成任务中，使用加权损失函数来提高视觉-语言模型的数据效率。与传统的交叉熵损失不同，该方法更关注具有重要临床意义的语义关键标记，从而在眼科报告生成实验中表现出色。结果表明，在多个数据规模下，该方法能以十倍少的训练数据达到相似的报告质量，有效缓解高质量标注数据稀缺的问题。

item.title: 基于交互检索增强的性能需求量化方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出IRAP方法提升性能需求量化效率与准确性。



摘要： 本文针对自然语言描述的软件性能需求在量化过程中存在的模糊性和不确定性问题，提出了一种名为IRAP的方法。该方法通过交互式检索增强的偏好获取方式，将性能需求转化为数学函数。IRAP利用特定领域的知识进行偏好推理和交互，减少了认知负担，并在四个真实数据集上的实验中表现出色，相较于其他10种先进方法，在最少五轮交互下实现了最高40倍的性能提升。

item.title: 解决知识图谱嵌入中的实体干扰问题
item.content:encoded:
研究提出修正的CKGE评估协议以应对实体干扰问题。



摘要： 本文探讨了持续知识图谱嵌入（CKGE）中因新实体引入而产生的实体干扰现象，这种现象会导致模型预测错误，使灾难性遗忘评估失真。作者提出了一种修正的评估协议，并通过实验表明忽略该现象可能导致性能高估达25%。同时，研究分析了不同CKGE方法和KGE模型对遗忘的不同影响，并引入了针对CKGE的灾难性遗忘度量标准。

item.title: 人工智能价值对齐的治理视角
item.content:encoded:
AI价值对齐是治理问题，需关注目标、信息与委托人三方面。



摘要： 本文指出人工智能的价值对齐问题不应仅被视为技术或规范挑战，而应从治理结构角度理解。作者提出一个三轴框架——目标、信息和委托人，用于分析现实系统中对齐失败的原因。该框架表明，对齐不是单一技术属性，而是由目标设定、信息分配及利益相关者影响共同决定的治理结果。文章强调，对齐本质上是多元且依赖情境的，解决对齐问题需要在不同价值观间进行权衡，并通过持续的制度过程来管理，而非仅依靠技术设计。

item.title: URoPE：跨视角与跨维度的通用相对位置嵌入方法
item.content:encoded:
提出URoPE解决Transformer在跨视角和跨维度任务中的位置编码问题。



摘要： 本文提出URoPE，一种适用于跨视角或跨维度几何空间的通用相对位置嵌入方法。该方法通过在查询图像平面上投影3D点，使标准2D RoPE可用于不同视角或维度的任务。URoPE无需参数且对全局坐标系不变，兼容现有RoPE优化的注意力核。实验表明，URoPE在多种任务中均有效提升模型性能，包括新视角合成、3D目标检测等，展示了其在几何推理中的广泛适用性。

item.title: 基于受控自组织的能源系统架构设计研究
item.content:encoded:
受控自组织提升能源系统鲁棒性，考虑隐私与限制。



摘要： 本文探讨了在基于代理的网络物理能源系统（CPES）中应用受控自组织的概念，以增强系统的鲁棒性。通过引入观察者/控制器架构，系统可以在受到干扰时进行自我调整并允许干预，从而有效应对网络攻击等威胁。文章重点分析了在实施观察者和控制器时需考虑的隐私、监管约束及数据交换限制，并提出了相应的架构变体。同时评估了不同架构下的控制器行为，强调了在实际应用中设计合理架构的重要性。

item.title: Transformer模型压缩的系统性实证研究
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示了Transformer压缩的关键结构特性与限制。



摘要： 本文通过40多个实验对GPT-2和Mistral 7B模型进行系统性压缩研究，分析了谱压缩、块级功能替换、基于旋转的量化、激活几何和自适应提前退出等方法。研究发现五个关键结构特性：高方差激活方向与预测方向无关，变压器块在特定分布下近似线性，权重分解会引入误差，深度增加使线性增强，约30%的token计算较简单。实验表明单块线性替换可实现34倍压缩，但多块替换因误差累积和分布偏移失败，提示静态压缩存在局限，需采用自适应方法。

item.title: 基于概率框架的深度学习突变体质量评估与选择方法
item.content:encoded:
提出概率框架评估突变体质量，提升测试效率。



摘要： 本文提出一种概率框架，用于量化深度学习中突变体的质量，从抗性与真实两个维度进行评估。该框架通过统计杀灭概率衡量抗性，利用广义Jaccard相似度衡量真实性，从而实现对低质量突变算子配置的筛选和排序。研究在四个真实深度学习故障数据集上进行了验证，结果表明，基于质量的选择方法可减少55.6%的突变体生成量，同时保持与基线相当的抗性和真实性水平，有效降低了突变体生成和执行的成本，而不影响其在测试和调试中的实用性。

item.title: SAKE框架提升多模态命名实体识别性能
item.content:encoded:
SAKE框架通过自适应搜索提升多模态命名实体识别效果。



摘要： 本文提出SAKE框架，旨在解决开放世界社交媒体中多模态命名实体识别（GMNER）的挑战。传统方法依赖外部知识检索或内部知识优化，但存在噪声干扰或知识边界限制的问题。SAKE通过自适应推理和搜索工具调用，结合两阶段训练策略：第一阶段生成难度感知的搜索标签以量化不确定性，构建具备自我意识和工具使用能力的SAKE-SeCoT数据集；第二阶段采用混合奖励函数的代理强化学习，使模型从被动搜索转向主动决策。实验表明SAKE在两个主流社交平台基准上表现优异。

提出一种新型多编码器U-Net架构，提升前列腺病灶分割精度。

item.title: 基于极端空间模型的多输出响应控制系统研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种极端空间模型用于预测复杂生产系统中的极端事件。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于极端空间模型的多输出响应控制系统，通过在两个空间域上使用双线性函数来高效捕捉动态变化。研究探讨了边缘参数建模和极端依赖关系，并开发了高效的图辅助复合似然估计方法，以应对高维输出。该模型在复合飞机制造中的应用表明，其在极端事件预测方面优于传统方法，有助于提升复杂生产系统中的质量管理和运营安全。

item.title: 多校准方法在流行率估计中的应用与优势
item.content:encoded:
多校准方法能有效减少因协变量偏移带来的流行率估计偏差。



摘要： 本文探讨了在使用不完美的测量设备（如诊断测试、分类器或大语言模型）估计群体中某一类别的流行率时，传统方法在协变量偏移下失效的问题。研究指出，多校准方法通过在输入特征上进行校准，而非仅基于平均值，能够保证在协变量偏移下的无偏估计。实验表明，标准方法在偏移增大时偏差显著增加，而多校准方法则保持接近零的偏差。研究还通过两个实际应用案例验证了多校准的有效性，并强调校准数据应覆盖目标群体可能差异的关键特征维度。

item.title: 基于解释方差的强化学习策略优化方法
item.content:encoded:
EVPO通过动态选择基线降低策略优化方差，提升训练效果。



摘要： 本文研究了在大型语言模型后训练中使用学习到的评估器作为基线的问题。传统理论认为PPO等基于评估器的方法能有效减少方差，但GRPO等无评估器方法因简单且表现良好而被广泛采用。研究发现，在稀疏奖励环境下，学习到的评估器可能引入噪声，反而增加优势估计的方差。作者将基线选择建模为卡尔曼滤波问题，提出了一种基于解释方差（EV）的策略优化方法EVPO，该方法在每一步监控批次级EV，并自适应切换基于评估器和批次均值的优势估计，确保每一步的方差不超过两者中的更优者。实验表明，EVPO在多个任务中优于PPO和GRPO。

item.title: 基于大语言模型的Reddit抑郁症风险评估研究
item.content:encoded:
利用LLM分析Reddit帖子，评估抑郁症风险。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于大语言模型（LLMs）的系统，用于评估Reddit帖子中的抑郁症风险。该方法通过多标签分类识别八种与抑郁症相关的情绪，并计算加权严重程度指数。在包含约6,000条标注帖子的DepressionEmo数据集上进行零样本测试，并应用于2024-2025年间从四个子版块收集的469,692条评论。最佳模型gemma3:27b在微F1和宏F1指标上分别达到0.75和0.70，表现与专门微调的模型（如BART）相当。分析显示不同社区的风险特征稳定且存在显著差异，证明了大规模心理监测的可行性。

item.title: 决策导向的联邦学习框架及其优化性能分析
item.content:encoded:
DFFL框架通过预测-优化方法提升决策质量，适用于不同约束和目标的客户端。



摘要： 本文提出一种称为决策导向联邦学习（DFFL）的框架，采用预测-优化方法，每个代理的预测模型作为下游线性优化问题的输入，且不允许直接交换原始数据。客户端在目标函数和可行性约束上可能存在差异。研究基于SPO+方法，构建了SPO+代理损失的异质性边界，通过支持函数表示可行区域，分离目标函数变化与可行集变化的影响。在强凸可行区域情况下，由于优化器稳定性，可获得更紧的边界。基于此，定义了一种启发式的本地与联邦风险决策规则，并指出当异质性惩罚小于数据聚合的统计优势时，联邦学习能提升决策质量。实验在多面体和强凸问题中进行，结果显示联邦学习在强凸情况下表现稳健，而多面体情况下的性能下降主要由约束异质性引起，尤其是样本量大的客户端。

item.title: 基于 Claude Code 的交互式学习课程设计
item.content:encoded:
提出一种模块化 AI 编程学习框架，提升开发者技能。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种名为 cc-self-train 的交互式学习课程，用于教授 Claude Code 这一智能编程工具。该课程包含五个核心贡献：包括渐进式角色模型、自适应学习系统、跨领域统一课程、步骤节奏控制机制以及自动更新课程设计。通过参数化测试套件确保结构一致性，并在27名参与者中进行了初步评估，结果显示学习者在10个技能领域均有显著提升，尤其在高级功能如钩子和自定义技能方面效果明显。研究为自动化教育系统的设计提供了重要启示。

item.title: 基于多任务强化学习的水下导航策略分析
item.content:encoded:
研究多任务RL在水下导航中的可解释性与任务区分机制。



摘要： 本文探讨了在动态、不确定环境下，自主水下机器人如何通过多任务强化学习实现高效且可解释的控制策略。研究发现，在相关任务的多任务RL框架中，网络仅用约1.5%的权重来区分不同任务，其中85%的权重连接输入层的上下文变量到下一层隐藏层，表明上下文变量在任务区分中起关键作用。该研究为提升水下监测系统的可解释性、模型编辑和迁移学习提供了理论支持。

item.title: ShadowPEFT：一种高效的层空间微调框架
item.content:encoded:
ShadowPEFT通过共享层空间优化提升大模型微调效率。



摘要： ShadowPEFT是一种参数高效的微调框架，通过在每个Transformer层中维护并演化并行的阴影状态，实现层级别的精调。与传统的低秩微调方法不同，它将适应过程从分布式权重扰动转变为集中式的层空间优化，提升了参数利用效率和计算灵活性。实验表明，在生成和理解任务上，ShadowPEFT在参数预算相近的情况下表现优于或等于LoRA和DoRA。此外，其可复用、可独立预训练的特性也使其适用于边缘计算场景。

item.title: 基于LSTM的低成本空气质量传感器校准方法研究
item.content:encoded:
利用LSTM网络提升低功耗空气质量传感器的校准精度。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于长短期记忆（LSTM）网络的深度学习框架，用于校准低成本空气质量传感器（LCS）对PM₂.₅、PM₁₀和NO₂的测量数据。该方法通过时间序列学习捕捉环境因素的动态变化，相比随机森林模型，在所有三种污染物的训练、验证和测试集上均取得了更高的R²值。研究构建了包含时滞参数、谐波编码和交互项的特征集，以提高模型在未见时间窗口上的泛化能力。校准结果经Equivalence Spreadsheet Tool 3.1验证，符合监管要求，NO₂、PM₁₀和PM₂.₅的扩展不确定度分别为22.11%、12.42%和9.1%。

item.title: VLAA-GUI: Knowing When to Stop, Recover, and Search, A Modular Framework for GUI Automation
item.content:encoded: Autonomous GUI agents face two fundamental challenges: early stopping, where agents prematurely declare success without verifiable evidence, and repetitive loops, where agents cycle through the same failing actions without recovery. We present VLAA-GUI, a modular GUI agentic framework built around three integrated components that guide the system on when to Stop, Recover, and Search. First, a mandatory Completeness Verifier enforces UI-observable success criteria and verification at every finish step -- with an agent-level verifier that cross-examines completion claims with decision rules, rejecting those lacking direct visual evidence. Second, a mandatory Loop Breaker provides multi-tier filtering: switching interaction mode after repeated failures, forcing strategy changes after persistent screen-state recurrence, and binding reflection signals to strategy shifts. Third, an on-demand Search Agent searches online for unfamiliar workflows by directly querying a capable LLM with search ability, returning results as plain text. We additionally integrate a Coding Agent for code-intensive actions and a Grounding Agent for precise action grounding, both invoked on demand when required. We evaluate VLAA-GUI across five top-tier backbones, including Opus 4.5, 4.6 and Gemini 3.1 Pro, on two benchmarks with Linux and Windows tasks, achieving top performance on both (77.5% on OSWorld and 61.0% on WindowsAgentArena). Notably, three of the five backbones surpass human performance (72.4%) on OSWorld in a single pass. Ablation studies show that all three proposed components consistently improve a strong backbone, while a weaker backbone benefits more from these tools when the step budget is sufficient. Further analysis also shows that the Loop Breaker nearly halves wasted steps for loop-prone models.

item.title: A Controlled Benchmark of Visual State-Space Backbones with Domain-Shift and Boundary Analysis for Remote-Sensing Segmentation
item.content:encoded: Visual state-space models (SSMs) are increasingly promoted as efficient alternatives to Vision Transformers, yet their practical advantages remain unclear under fair comparison because existing studies rarely isolate encoder effects from decoder and training choices. We present a strictly controlled benchmark of representative visual SSM families, including VMamba, MambaVision, and Spatial-Mamba, for remote-sensing semantic segmentation, in which only the encoder varies across experiments. Evaluated on LoveDA and ISPRS Potsdam under a unified 4-stage feature interface and a fixed lightweight decoder, the benchmark reveals three main findings, intra-family scaling yields only modest gains, cross-domain generalization is strongly asymmetric, and boundary delineation is the dominant failure mode under distribution shift. Although visual SSMs achieve favorable accuracy-efficiency trade-offs relative to the controlled CNN and Transformer baselines considered here, the results suggest that future improvements are more likely to come from robustness-oriented design and boundary-aware decoding than from encoder scaling alone. By isolating encoder behavior under a unified and reproducible protocol, this study establishes a practical reference benchmark for the design and evaluation of future Mamba-based segmentation backbones

item.title: Evaluation-driven Scaling for Scientific Discovery
item.content:encoded: Language models are increasingly used in scientific discovery to generate hypotheses, propose candidate solutions, implement systems, and iteratively refine them. At the core of these trial-and-error loops lies evaluation: the process of obtaining feedback on candidate solutions via verifiers, simulators, or task-specific scoring functions. While prior work has highlighted the importance of evaluation, it has not explicitly formulated the problem of how evaluation-driven discovery loops can be scaled up in a principled and effective manner to push the boundaries of scientific discovery, a problem this paper seeks to address. We introduce Simple Test-time Evaluation-driven Scaling (SimpleTES), a general framework that strategically combines parallel exploration, feedback-driven refinement, and local selection, revealing substantial gains unlocked by scaling evaluation-driven discovery loops along the right dimensions. Across 21 scientific problems spanning six domains, SimpleTES discovers state-of-the-art solutions using gpt-oss models, consistently outperforming both frontier-model baselines and sophisticated optimization pipelines. Particularly, we sped up the widely used LASSO algorithm by over 2x, designed quantum circuit routing policies that reduce gate overhead by 24.5%, and discovered new Erdos minimum overlap constructions that surpass the best-known results. Beyond novel discoveries, SimpleTES produces trajectory-level histories that naturally supervise feedback-driven learning. When post-trained on successful trajectories, models not only improve efficiency on seen problems but also generalize to unseen problems, discovering solutions that base models fail to uncover. Together, our results establish effective evaluation-driven loop scaling as a central axis for advancing LLM-driven scientific discovery, and provide a simple yet practical framework for realizing these gains.

item.title: SketchFaceGS: Real-Time Sketch-Driven Face Editing and Generation with Gaussian Splatting
item.content:encoded: 3D Gaussian representations have emerged as a powerful paradigm for digital head modeling, achieving photorealistic quality with real-time rendering. However, intuitive and interactive creation or editing of 3D Gaussian head models remains challenging. Although 2D sketches provide an ideal interaction modality for fast, intuitive conceptual design, they are sparse, depth-ambiguous, and lack high-frequency appearance cues, making it difficult to infer dense, geometrically consistent 3D Gaussian structures from strokes - especially under real-time constraints. To address these challenges, we propose SketchFaceGS, the first sketch-driven framework for real-time generation and editing of photorealistic 3D Gaussian head models from 2D sketches. Our method uses a feed-forward, coarse-to-fine architecture. A Transformer-based UV feature-prediction module first reconstructs a coarse but geometrically consistent UV feature map from the input sketch, and then a 3D UV feature enhancement module refines it with high-frequency, photorealistic detail to produce a high-fidelity 3D head. For editing, we introduce a UV Mask Fusion technique combined with a layer-by-layer feature-fusion strategy, enabling precise, real-time, free-viewpoint modifications. Extensive experiments show that SketchFaceGS outperforms existing methods in both generation fidelity and editing flexibility, producing high-quality, editable 3D heads from sketches in a single forward pass.

item.title: Dual-Anchoring: Addressing State Drift in Vision-Language Navigation
item.content:encoded: Vision-Language Navigation(VLN) requires an agent to navigate through 3D environments by following natural language instructions. While recent Video Large Language Models(Video-LLMs) have largely advanced VLN, they remain highly susceptible to State Drift in long scenarios. In these cases, the agent's internal state drifts away from the true task execution state, leading to aimless wandering and failure to execute essential maneuvers in the instruction. We attribute this failure to two distinct cognitive deficits: Progress Drift, where the agent fails to distinguish completed sub-goals from remaining ones, and Memory Drift, where the agent's history representations degrade, making it lose track of visited landmarks. In this paper, we propose a Dual-Anchoring Framework that explicitly anchors the instruction progress and history representations. First, to address progress drift, we introduce Instruction Progress Anchoring, which supervises the agent to generate structured text tokens that delineate completed versus remaining sub-goals. Second, to mitigate memory drift, we propose Memory Landmark Anchoring, which utilizes a Landmark-Centric World Model to retrospectively predict object-centric embeddings extracted by the Segment Anything Model, compelling the agent to explicitly verify past observations and preserve distinct representations of visited landmarks. Facilitating this framework, we curate two extensive datasets: 3.6 million samples with explicit progress descriptions, and 937k grounded landmark data for retrospective verification. Extensive experiments in both simulation and real-world environments demonstrate the superiority of our method, achieving a 15.2% improvement in Success Rate and a remarkable 24.7% gain on long-horizon trajectories. To facilitate further research, we will release our code, data generation pipelines, and the collected datasets.

item.title: Air-Know: Arbiter-Calibrated Knowledge-Internalizing Robust Network for Composed Image Retrieval
item.content:encoded: Composed Image Retrieval (CIR) has attracted significant attention due to its flexible multimodal query method, yet its development is severely constrained by the Noisy Triplet Correspondence (NTC) problem. Most existing robust learning methods rely on the "small loss hypothesis", but the unique semantic ambiguity in NTC, such as "partial matching", invalidates this assumption, leading to unreliable noise identification. This entraps the model in a self dependent vicious cycle where the learner is intertwined with the arbiter, ultimately causing catastrophic "representation pollution". To address this critical challenge, we propose a novel "Expert-Proxy-Diversion" decoupling paradigm, named Air-Know (ArbIteR calibrated Knowledge iNternalizing rObust netWork). Air-Know incorporates three core modules: (1) External Prior Arbitration (EPA), which utilizes Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) as an offline expert to construct a high precision anchor dataset; (2) Expert Knowledge Internalization (EKI), which efficiently guides a lightweight proxy "arbiter" to internalize the expert's discriminative logic; (3) Dual Stream Reconciliation (DSR), which leverages the EKI's matching confidence to divert the training data, achieving a clean alignment stream and a representation feedback reconciliation stream. Extensive experiments on multiple CIR benchmark datasets demonstrate that Air-Know significantly outperforms existing SOTA methods under the NTC setting, while also showing strong competitiveness in traditional CIR.

item.title: AnyLift: Scaling Motion Reconstruction from Internet Videos via 2D Diffusion
item.content:encoded: Reconstructing 3D human motion and human-object interactions (HOI) from Internet videos is a fundamental step toward building large-scale datasets of human behavior. Existing methods struggle to recover globally consistent 3D motion under dynamic cameras, especially for motion types underrepresented in current motion-capture datasets, and face additional difficulty recovering coherent human-object interactions in 3D. We introduce a two-stage framework leveraging 2D diffusion that reconstructs 3D human motion and HOI from Internet videos. In the first stage, we synthesize multi-view 2D motion data for each domain, leveraging 2D keypoints extracted from Internet videos to incorporate human motions that rarely appear in existing MoCap datasets. In the second stage, a camera-conditioned multi-view 2D motion diffusion model is trained on the domain-specific synthetic data to recover 3D human motion and 3D HOI in the world space. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our method on Internet videos featuring challenging motions such as gymnastics, as well as in-the-wild HOI videos, and show that it outperforms prior work in producing realistic human motion and human-object interaction.

item.title: MARCO: Navigating the Unseen Space of Semantic Correspondence
item.content:encoded: Recent advances in semantic correspondence rely on dual-encoder architectures, combining DINOv2 with diffusion backbones. While accurate, these billion-parameter models generalize poorly beyond training keypoints, revealing a gap between benchmark performance and real-world usability, where queried points rarely match those seen during training. Building upon DINOv2, we introduce MARCO, a unified model for generalizable correspondence driven by a novel training framework that enhances both fine-grained localization and semantic generalization. By coupling a coarse-to-fine objective that refines spatial precision with a self-distillation framework, which expands sparse supervision beyond annotated regions, our approach transforms a handful of keypoints into dense, semantically coherent correspondences. MARCO sets a new state of the art on SPair-71k, AP-10K, and PF-PASCAL, with gains that amplify at fine-grained localization thresholds (+8.9 PCK@0.01), strongest generalization to unseen keypoints (+5.1, SPair-U) and categories (+4.7, MP-100), while remaining 3x smaller and 10x faster than diffusion-based approaches. Code is available at https://github.com/visinf/MARCO .

item.title: 基于局部补丁的3D点云边缘检测网络EdgeFormer
item.content:encoded:
EdgeFormer通过局部补丁分类实现高效3D点云边缘检测。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为EdgeFormer的学习型边缘检测网络，用于解决3D点云中细粒度边缘特征检测困难的问题。该方法分为两个阶段：首先构建每个点周围局部区域的特征描述符，然后基于这些描述符对每个点进行分类。由于将点云转换为局部补丁进行处理，EdgeFormer能够有效提取更精细的边缘细节。实验结果表明，该模型在多个基线方法中表现优异，具有较高的检测精度和实用性。

item.title: 基于图神经网络的流计算与图像分割加速方法
item.content:encoded:
融合GNN与Ford-Fulkerson算法，提升流计算与图像分割效率。



摘要： 本文提出一种学习增强框架，通过将图神经网络（GNN）与Ford-Fulkerson算法结合，加速最大流计算和图像分割。该方法不直接预测初始流，而是学习边的重要性概率以指导增广路径选择。引入消息传递GNN（MPGNN）联合学习节点和边嵌入，捕捉全局结构和局部流动态信息。针对输入图像，构建网格流网络并提取特征，通过一次GNN推理分配边概率，用于引导改进的Ford-Fulkerson过程。该方法避免重复推理，同时保持最大流/最小割最优性，并减少增广次数。还提出了基于高概率边的双向路径构建策略，建立了预测质量与效率之间的理论联系。

item.title: 语言模型表示与人类阅读时间的关系研究
item.content:encoded:
语言模型早期层能有效预测人类阅读时间。



摘要： 本研究通过分析语言模型的表示与人类阅读时间之间的关系，发现早期模型层在预测首次注视时间和注视持续时间方面优于传统指标如意外性（surprisal）。这表明语言模型的低层结构可能捕捉到了类似人类处理的特征。然而，在预测总阅读时间等晚期指标时，意外性仍表现更优。研究还发现结合意外性和早期层表示可提升性能，且最佳预测器因语言和眼动指标而异。

item.title: Sparse Network Inference under Imperfect Detection and its Application to Ecological Networks
item.content:encoded: Recovering latent structure from count data has received considerable attention in network inference, particularly when one seeks both cross-group interactions and within-group similarity patterns in bipartite networks, which is widely used in ecology research. Such networks are often sparse and inherently imperfect in their detection. Existing models mainly focus on interaction recovery, while the induced similarity graphs are much less studied. Moreover, sparsity is often not controlled, and scale is unbalanced, leading to oversparse or poorly rescaled estimates with degrading structural recovery. To address these issues, we propose a framework for structured sparse nonnegative low-rank factorization with detection probability estimation. We impose nonconvex $\ell_{1/2}$ regularization on the latent similarity and connectivity structures to promote sparsity within-group similarity and cross-group connectivity with better relative scale. The resulting optimization problem is nonconvex and nonsmooth. To solve it, we develop an ADMM-based algorithm with adaptive penalization and scale-aware initialization and establish its asymptotic feasibility and KKT stationarity of cluster points under mild regularity conditions. Experiments on synthetic and real-world ecological datasets demonstrate improved recovery of latent factors and similarity/connectivity structure relative to existing baselines.

item.title: 基于强化学习的粒子加速器束流线优化框架RLABC
item.content:encoded:
RLABC实现加速器束流线的自动优化，提升粒子传输效率。



摘要： 本文介绍了RLABC（Reinforcement Learning for Accelerator Beamline Control），一个用于粒子加速器束流线优化的开源Python框架。该框架将标准的Elegant束流线配置转换为强化学习环境，支持多种RL算法，并通过SDDS接口与Elegant模拟代码集成。RLABC将束流调优问题建模为马尔可夫决策过程，提供57维状态表示和可配置奖励函数，以优化粒子传输效率。在VEPP-5注入系统测试中，DDPG智能体实现了70.3%的粒子传输率，性能与传统方法相当。框架还支持分阶段学习策略，提高训练效率。所有配置文件和示例代码均已开源。

item.title: 基于神经算子的相场模型加速模拟方法
item.content:encoded:
利用神经算子提升相场模型计算效率。



摘要： 本文提出一种新的神经算子方法，结合经典凸凹分裂方案与物理信息学习，用于加速相场模型的模拟。该方法采用深度Ritz方法，通过训练神经算子来近似相场模型的变分形式，并在能量分裂变分公式下训练以保证能量耗散特性。研究还引入了专门设计的反应扩散神经算子（RDNO）架构，成功应用于各向同性Allen-Cahn方程和各向异性枝晶生长模拟，结果显示其在分布外评估中具有更好的泛化能力，且推理速度优于传统傅里叶谱方法。

item.title: 持续学习中微调策略对方法评估的影响研究
item.content:encoded:
微调策略影响持续学习方法的评估结果。



摘要： 本文研究了持续学习（CL）中微调策略对方法评估的影响。作者指出，微调策略定义的可训练参数子空间是关键的评估变量。通过分析不同可训练深度下的优化过程，发现改变可训练深度会影响当前任务拟合和知识保留的有效更新信号。实验在多个数据集上进行，结果显示不同方法的相对排名会因微调策略而变化，且更深的微调策略会导致更大的更新幅度、更高的遗忘率，并增强两者之间的关联性。研究强调了在CL评估中应将可训练深度作为显式实验因素。

item.title: 高校学生AI使用披露的心理机制研究
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示AI在教育中的使用披露受心理机制影响。



摘要： 本研究探讨了大学生在使用人工智能（AI）后是否愿意披露的心理机制，采用认知-情感-行为（CAC）框架进行分析。通过546名学生的问卷调查和22名学生的深度访谈，发现心理安全感显著提升学生披露意愿，而评估焦虑则会降低披露意愿。研究还指出，感知公平、教师支持和组织支持有助于增强心理安全感，而污名感、不确定性和隐私担忧则会加剧评估焦虑。此外，制度清晰度和支持性的教学实践鼓励学生更加开放，而政策模糊和负面评价恐惧则导致学生采取谨慎或策略性的披露方式。研究强调了支持性制度环境和明确指导在促进负责任的AI透明度中的重要性。

item.title: 小模型通过数据增强显著提升语言能力
item.content:encoded:
小模型通过注入合成数据提升语言任务表现。



摘要： 本文研究了大语言模型在语言能力上的差异性表现，发现部分语言现象的失败可能并非源于架构限制，而是数据稀缺所致。通过在GPT-2 Small模型中注入少量合成数据，实验结果显示在9个语言任务中的8个表现显著提升，其中特定任务准确率从20.9%提升至69.4%。尽管仍有部分现象难以改善，但研究证明小模型在数据充足的情况下仍能显著提升语言能力，提示数据组成对语言建模的重要性。

item.title: Condorcet获胜集的理论边界研究与自动化推理方法
item.content:encoded:
研究证明3人委员会可确保存在Condorcet获胜集。



摘要： 本文探讨了在选举中，当有n个选民和m个候选人时，寻找一个k人委员会作为Condorcet获胜集的问题。研究表明，当k=1或k=2时，某些选举可能不存在这样的集合，而k=5时总能保证存在。本文通过设计混合整数线性规划模型，尝试寻找反例以缩小理论边界。尽管未找到需要超过3人委员会的选举实例，但实验结果支持了一个新猜想：4人委员会总是存在。研究还提出了一种通用框架用于分析排名投票中的选举，并通过对偶分析提供了一条新的证明路径。

item.title: 基于潜变量推理的强化学习框架LEPO
item.content:encoded:
LEPO通过引入随机性提升大语言模型的推理能力。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为LEPO的新框架，将强化学习直接应用于连续潜变量表示，以增强大语言模型的探索能力。该框架在推理阶段保持随机性以实现多样化的轨迹采样，在优化阶段构建统一的梯度估计方法，同时处理潜变量和离散标记。实验表明，LEPO在离散和潜变量推理任务中均优于现有强化学习方法。

item.title: 基于运动与情绪特征解耦的微表情识别方法
item.content:encoded:
提出MEDN网络，解耦运动与情绪特征以提升微表情识别性能。



摘要： 本文针对微表情识别中运动特征与情绪信息耦合的问题，提出一种运动情绪特征解耦网络（MEDN）。该网络采用双分支结构，分别提取运动和情绪特征。运动分支通过AU检测任务和正交损失减少特征耦合，情绪分支则引入稀疏情感视觉Transformer（SEVit）以增强局部时间变化的表征。进一步设计协同融合模块（CoFM）自适应融合解耦后的特征。实验表明，MEDN在三个基准数据集上表现优异，有效提升了识别准确率和泛化能力。

item.title: 小语言模型的代理范式研究：性能与成本的平衡
item.content:encoded:
小语言模型通过代理范式提升性能，单代理系统表现最佳。



摘要： 尽管大语言模型具有强大能力，但其高计算成本、延迟和隐私风险限制了实际应用。小语言模型（参数少于100亿）作为替代方案，但在知识和推理方面存在局限。现有研究多关注模型扩展或微调，忽视了代理范式如工具使用和多代理协作的潜力。本文首次对<10B开源模型在三种范式下的表现进行了大规模研究：基础模型、单代理系统和多代理协作系统。结果表明，单代理系统在性能与成本之间取得最佳平衡，而多代理系统则增加开销但收益有限。研究强调了代理导向设计在资源受限环境中的重要性。

item.title: AI-native软件生态系统作为复杂适应系统的研究
item.content:encoded:
AI系统交互导致的不可解释问题需用复杂适应系统理论分析。



摘要： 本文探讨了多智能体AI系统在交互中产生的不可解释问题，指出传统软件理论无法充分解释这些现象。作者提出应将AI-native软件生态系统视为复杂适应系统（CAS），其特性如架构熵、级联故障和理解债务源于组件间的相互作用。文章将Holland的CAS属性映射到实际系统动态，并定义了测量因果涌现的方法。通过七个可验证的假设，挑战或扩展了Lehman定律，强调需要从系统层面进行监控以适应自主代理时代。无论结论是否被证实，该研究都促使我们重新审视软件工程的核心假设。

item.title: UniT：通过视觉锚定实现人类到人形机器人的统一物理语言
item.content:encoded:
UniT框架解决人形机器人数据稀缺问题，实现跨身体动作迁移。



摘要： 文章提出UniT框架，通过视觉锚定建立人类与人形机器人之间的统一物理语言。该框架利用三分支交叉重构机制，将动作与视觉信息对齐，生成共享的离散潜在空间，从而实现跨身体动作的有效迁移。在策略学习和世界建模两个范式中验证了其有效性，展示了在仿真和真实场景中的卓越性能，包括零样本任务迁移和动作可控性提升。实验表明，UniT能够将大量人类数据转化为通用的人形机器人能力。

item.title: 基于深度学习的脑肿瘤分类系统研究
item.content:encoded:
深度学习用于脑肿瘤四类分类，准确率高达99.29%



摘要： 本文提出一种基于深度学习的自动化脑肿瘤分类框架，能够区分胶质瘤、脑膜瘤、垂体瘤和健康脑组织。该系统使用预训练的ViT-B/16模型，并结合临床优化的预处理和训练流程，包括CLAHE增强、两阶段微调、MixUp/CutMix数据增强以及EMA和TTA技术。实验结果显示，该模型在7023张MRI图像上取得了99.29%的测试准确率和99.25%的宏F1分数，且在健康和脑膜瘤类别中实现完美召回，优于所有基于CNN的基线模型。

item.title: 基于视觉Transformer与扩散模型的3D人体网格恢复方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种结合视觉Transformer和扩散模型的3D人体网格恢复框架。



摘要： 本文针对单目RGB图像中3D人体网格恢复的挑战，提出一种融合视觉Transformer与条件扩散模型的协同框架。该方法通过视觉Transformer提取可见区域的确定性视觉线索，同时利用扩散模型生成结构一致的人体表示。设计了多样一致性特征学习模块和跨注意力多级融合机制，以实现两个路径间的有效对齐与交互。实验表明，该方法在标准基准上表现优异，具有较强的鲁棒性。

item.title: RF-HiT：一种高效医学图像分割模型
item.content:encoded:
RF-HiT通过线性复杂度实现高效且精确的医学图像分割。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为RF-HiT的高效医学图像分割模型，结合了小时钟结构的Transformer主干和多尺度层次编码器，以实现解剖引导的特征条件化。与传统基于扩散的方法不同，RF-HiT利用修正流和高效的Transformer块，实现了线性计算复杂度，并仅需少量离散步骤即可完成推理。该模型通过可学习插值在不同分辨率间融合条件特征，从而在最小计算开销下获得有效的多尺度表示。实验结果显示，RF-HiT在ACDC和BraTS 2021数据集上分别达到91.27%和87.40%的Dice系数，性能优于或接近更复杂的模型，展现出其在实时临床分割中的强大潜力。

item.title: EAST框架提升早期动作预测性能
item.content:encoded:
EAST框架通过随机训练策略提升早期动作预测效果。



摘要： 本文提出EAST框架，用于早期动作预测任务，该框架通过随机采样时间步来分离观察到和未观察到的视频帧，使模型能有效处理不完整视觉证据。研究还发现联合学习观察到和未来（理想）表示可显著提升性能，甚至使仅编码器模型表现优异。为提高可扩展性，作者引入了token masking方法，减少内存使用并加快训练速度。结合预测解码器，EAST在NTU60、SSv2和UCF101数据集上取得最佳成绩，分别超越之前最佳模型10.1%、7.7%和3.9%。

item.title: 语言模型中周期性数字特征的层级结构与可分性研究
item.content:encoded:
不同模型学习到周期性数字特征，部分具有几何可分性。



摘要： 本文研究了语言模型如何通过自然文本学习到周期性数字特征，发现Transformer、线性RNN、LSTM和传统词嵌入等模型均能学习到周期为T的特征，但只有部分模型能实现模T的几何可分性。研究证明傅里叶域稀疏性虽必要但不充分，进一步分析表明数据、架构、优化器和分词器对几何可分性有关键影响。模型可通过两种路径获得可分特征：从语言数据中的共现信号或从多标记加法问题中学习。结果揭示了不同模型在特征学习上的趋同演化现象。

item.title: 神经细胞自动机自发生成语法结构表示
item.content:encoded:
神经细胞自动机通过局部交互自发生成语法结构。



摘要： 本文研究了神经细胞自动机是否能通过局部交互自发产生语法处理能力。实验中使用了一个18,658参数的二维神经细胞自动机，仅通过1位边界信号进行训练，学习算术表达式文法的成员问题。训练后，其内部L×L网格自发形成名为Proto-CKY的空间结构，该结构满足语法处理的三个标准：超越正则语言的表达能力、超出训练分布的结构泛化能力，以及与语法结构高度相关的内部组织（皮尔逊相关系数约0.71）。Proto-CKY在四个上下文无关文法中独立出现，并在扰动后可自发恢复。它在功能上与CKY算法一致，但形式上不同，是一种物理原型，代表数学理想在物理载体上的具体实现。

item.title: 大语言模型在文化相关问题中的区域偏好研究
item.content:encoded:
研究发现大语言模型对日本等国家有明显偏好，且偏好在微调阶段显现。



摘要： 本文探讨了大语言模型在处理文化相关问题时的区域偏好现象。研究提出一个新的数据集CROQ，用于分析模型在不同文化背景下的表现。结果显示，与以往文化偏见研究不同，LLMs更倾向于日本等非西方国家。此外，当使用英语等高资源语言进行提示时，模型输出更加多样化，对输入语言所属国家的倾向性降低。研究还发现，这种文化偏见主要在监督微调阶段出现，而非预训练阶段。

item.title: SCURank：基于摘要内容单元的高效摘要蒸馏框架
item.content:encoded:
SCURank通过内容单元提升摘要质量，优于传统方法。



摘要： 本文提出SCURank框架，利用摘要内容单元（SCUs）提升摘要质量。与传统指标和LLM排名策略相比，SCURank通过评估信息丰富度和语义重要性，实现了更稳定、更高质量的摘要排序。实验表明，SCURank在多个数据集上表现优异，并验证了多LLM摘要融合对模型抽象能力和整体性能的提升作用。代码已开源。

item.title: 基于CLIP的GR4CIL框架提升增量学习性能
item.content:encoded:
GR4CIL通过任务区分与知识路由提升CLIP模型的增量学习效果。



摘要： 本文针对类增量学习（CIL）中知识遗忘和跨任务响应不准确的问题，提出GR4CIL框架。该框架结合任务区分与知识路由机制，保留任务特定的视觉知识，同时维持共享文本语义空间的稳定性，减少任务间干扰。此外，引入正交补偿机制以缓解模态差异带来的偏差，增强任务内区分能力，并扩大真实任务与竞争任务之间的得分差距。实验表明，GR4CIL在多个基准数据集上优于现有方法，同时保持零样本泛化能力。

item.title: 基于分布鲁棒的信号估计方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种分布鲁棒方法用于风险敏感信号估计。



摘要： 本文提出了一种分布鲁棒方法，用于从观测信号中估计未知信号。该方法假设未知信号和观测信号的联合分布属于一个给定的类型-2 Wasserstein 球内的分布集合。通过最小化在该不确定性集中的条件风险价值（CVaR）来评估估计器性能，并证明当中心分布为有限支持时，可通过半定规划精确计算最优线性估计器。实验表明，该方法在实际电力市场价格预测任务中表现优于现有方法。

item.title: SAHM: A Benchmark for Arabic Financial and Shari'ah-Compliant Reasoning
item.content:encoded: English financial NLP has progressed rapidly through benchmarks for sentiment, document understanding, and financial question answering, while Arabic financial NLP remains comparatively under-explored despite strong practical demand for trustworthy finance and Islamic-finance assistants. We introduce SAHM, a document-grounded benchmark and instruction-tuning dataset for Arabic financial NLP and Shari'ah-compliant reasoning. SAHM contains 14,380 expert-verified instances spanning seven tasks: AAOIFI standards QA, fatwa-based QA/MCQ, accounting and business exams, financial sentiment analysis, extractive summarization, and event-cause reasoning, curated from authentic regulatory, juristic, and corporate sources. We evaluate 19 strong open and proprietary LLMs using task-specific metrics and rubric-based scoring for open-ended outputs, and find that Arabic fluency does not reliably translate to evidence-grounded financial reasoning: models are substantially stronger on recognition-style tasks than on generation and causal reasoning, with the largest gaps on event-cause reasoning. We release the benchmark, evaluation framework, and an instruction-tuned model to support future research on trustworthy Arabic financial NLP.

item.title: 视觉语言模型的字面优先偏差与抽象表示研究
item.content:encoded:
高真实感图像可能干扰模型对习语的抽象理解。



摘要： 本文研究了视觉语言模型（VLMs）在处理抽象意义时的表现，特别是高真实感图像是否会影响习语的语义理解。作者提出了DIVA基准测试，通过生成字面和习语意义的图标化图像来评估模型的抽象能力。同时引入了Semantic Alignment Gap ($Δ$) 和方向性偏差 $b(t)$ 来量化模型在字面与习语语义之间的差异和偏好。实验结果显示，模型规模并不能消除字面优先偏差，且更高的视觉保真度反而导致符号对齐减弱，表明超现实图像可能干扰模型的组合理解能力。研究建议通过图标化抽象和意义锚定来提升模型的组合理解能力。

item.title: 基于闭环仿真的自动驾驶车辆3D资产生成系统
item.content:encoded:
提出Asset Harvester系统，实现自动驾驶数据到3D资产的高效转换。



摘要： 本文介绍了Asset Harvester，一个将真实驾驶日志中的稀疏对象观测转化为完整、可模拟3D资产的端到端系统。该系统结合了大规模对象中心训练对的整理、跨异构传感器的几何感知预处理以及耦合稀疏视角条件多视角生成与3D高斯提升的稳健训练方法。通过SparseViewDiT等模块，系统有效应对了有限视角等现实数据挑战，并支持大规模可重复使用的3D资产生成，为自动驾驶系统的仿真测试和安全验证提供了关键技术支持。

item.title: 面向长期交互的个性化智能体记忆评估基准Memora
item.content:encoded:
Memora评估智能体长期记忆能力，揭示其在知识更新中的不足。



摘要： 本文提出Memora，一个用于评估个性化智能体长期记忆能力的基准，覆盖数周至数月的用户对话。该基准包含记忆、推理和推荐三项任务，并引入FAMA指标以衡量智能体对过时信息的依赖程度。实验发现，现有模型频繁使用无效记忆，且难以处理知识更新，表明当前智能体在长期记忆管理方面存在明显缺陷。

item.title: 大语言模型在对话修复中的行为研究
item.content:encoded:
研究大语言模型在对话修复中的表现差异。



摘要： 本文研究了大语言模型在解决可解与不可解数学问题的多轮对话中如何进行对话修复。研究分析了模型是否主动发起修复以及如何回应用户发起的修复请求，发现不同模型在修复行为上存在显著差异，有的模型对修复几乎无反应，有的则容易被引导。研究还表明，随着对话轮次增加，模型行为变得更加独特且难以预测，反映出各模型在修复情境下的不同可靠性特征。

item.title: Temporal Prototyping and Hierarchical Alignment for Unsupervised Video-based Visible-Infrared Person Re-Identification
item.content:encoded: Visible-infrared person re-identification (VI-ReID) enables cross-modality identity matching for all-day surveillance, yet existing methods predominantly focus on the image level or rely heavily on costly identity annotations. While video-based VI-ReID has recently emerged to exploit temporal dynamics for improved robustness, existing studies remain limited to supervised settings. Crucially, the unsupervised video VI-ReID problem, where models must learn from RGB and infrared tracklets without identity labels, remains largely unexplored despite its practical importance in real-world deployment. To bridge this gap, we propose HiTPro (Hierarchical Temporal Prototyping), a prototype-driven framework without explicit hard pseudo-label assignment for unsupervised video-based VI-ReID. HiTPro begins with an efficient Temporal-aware Feature Encoder that first extracts discriminative frame-level features and then aggregates them into a robust tracklet-level representation. Building upon these features, HiTPro first constructs reliable intra-camera prototypes via Intra-Camera Tracklet Prototyping by aggregating features from temporally partitioned sub-tracklets. Through Hierarchical Cross-Prototype Alignment, we perform a two-stage positive mining process: progressing from within-modality associations to cross-modality matching, enhanced by Dynamic Threshold Strategy and Soft Weight Assignment. Finally, {Hierarchical Contrastive Learning} progressively optimizes feature-prototype alignment across three levels: intra-camera discrimination, cross-camera same-modality consistency, and cross-modality invariance. Extensive experiments on HITSZ-VCM and BUPTCampus demonstrate that HiTPro achieves state-of-the-art performance under fully unsupervised settings, significantly outperforming adapted baselines and establishes a strong baseline for future research.

item.title: Physics-Informed Causal MDPs for Sequential Constraint Repair in Engineering Simulation Pipelines
item.content:encoded: Off-policy learning in constrained MDPs with large binary state spaces faces a fundamental tension: causal identification of transition dynamics requires structural assumptions, while sample-efficient policy learning requires state-space compression. We introduce PI-CMDP, a framework for CMDPs whose constraint dependencies form a layered DAG under a Lifecycle Ordering Assumption (LOA). We propose an Identify-Compress-Estimate pipeline: (i) Identify: LOA enables backdoor identification of causal edge weights for cross-layer pairs, with formal partial-identification bounds when LOA is violated; (ii) Compress: a Markov abstraction compresses state cardinality from 2^(WL) to (W+1)^L under layer-priority regularity and exchangeability; and (iii) Estimate: a physics-guided doubly-robust estimator remains unbiased and reduces the variance constant when the physics prior outperforms a learned model. We instantiate PI-CMDP on constraint repair in engineering simulation pipelines. On the TPS benchmark (4,206 episodes), PI-CMDP achieves 76.2% repair success rate with only 300 training episodes versus 70.8% for the strongest baseline (+5.4 pp), narrowing to +2.8 pp (83.4% vs. 80.6%) in the full-data regime, while substantially reducing cascade failure rates. All improvements are consistent across 5 independent seeds (paired t-test p < 0.02).

item.title: Learning Lifted Action Models from Unsupervised Visual Traces
item.content:encoded: Efficient construction of models capturing the preconditions and effects of actions is essential for applying AI planning in real-world domains. Extensive prior work has explored learning such models from high-level descriptions of state and/or action sequences. In this paper, we tackle a more challenging setting: learning lifted action models from sequences of state images, without action observation. We propose a deep learning framework that jointly learns state prediction, action prediction, and a lifted action model. We also introduce a mixed-integer linear program (MILP) to prevent prediction collapse and self-reinforcing errors among predictions. The MILP takes the predicted states, actions, and action model over a subset of traces and solves for logically consistent states, actions, and action model that are as close as possible to the original predictions. Pseudo-labels extracted from the MILP solution are then used to guide further training. Experiments across multiple domains show that integrating MILP-based correction helps the model escape local optima and converge toward globally consistent solutions.

item.title: DART: Mitigating Harm Drift in Difference-Aware LLMs via Distill-Audit-Repair Training
item.content:encoded: Large language models (LLMs) tuned for safety often avoid acknowledging demographic differences, even when such acknowledgment is factually correct (e.g., ancestry-based disease incidence) or contextually justified (e.g., religious hiring preferences). This identity-blindness yields incorrect responses, unnecessary refusals, or generic "equal-treatment" defaults. We study this via difference-awareness classification: given a question involving demographic groups, the task is not to answer directly, but to classify whether a correct answer requires recognizing group differences (yes) or whether groups should be treated identically (no). Crucially, fine-tuning for accuracy triggers harm drift: model-generated explanations become increasingly harmful as decision accuracy improves, whether by elaborating harmful content, introducing problematic assumptions, or failing to flag harms the baseline identified. To mitigate this, we introduce DART (Distill--Audit--Repair Training), which distills label-conditioned reasoning from a teacher, audits outputs for harm drift cases relative to baseline, and repairs problematic cases via severity-weighted fine-tuning. On eight benchmarks, DART improves Llama-3-8B-Instruct accuracy from 39.0% to 68.8%, with largest gains on equal-treatment prompts (11.3% -> 72.6%), while reducing harm drift cases by 72.6%. It also transfers to 280 open-ended real-world queries across medical, legal, policy, and educational domains, improving difference-appropriate responses from 39.8% to 77.5% while reducing refusals from 34.3% to 3.0%. Our results demonstrate that accuracy and safety need not conflict when explicit detection and repair mechanisms are in place.

item.title: Implicit neural representations as a coordinate-based framework for continuous environmental field reconstruction from sparse ecological observations
item.content:encoded: Reconstructing continuous environmental fields from sparse and irregular observations remains a central challenge in environmental modelling and biodiversity informatics. Many ecological datasets are heterogeneous in space and time, making grid-based approaches difficult to scale or generalise across domains. Here, we evaluate implicit neural representations (INRs) as a coordinate-based modelling framework for learning continuous spatial and spatio-temporal fields directly from coordinate inputs. We analyse their behaviour across three representative modelling scenarios: species distribution reconstruction, phenological dynamics, and morphological segmentation derived from open biodiversity data. Beyond predictive performance, we examine interpolation behaviour, spatial coherence, and computational characteristics relevant for environmental modelling workflows, including scalability, resolution-independent querying, and architectural inductive bias. Results show that neural fields provide stable continuous representations with predictable computational cost, complementing classical smoothers and tree-based approaches. These findings position coordinate-based neural fields as a flexible representation layer that can be integrated into environmental modelling pipelines and exploratory analysis frameworks for large, irregularly sampled datasets.

item.title: OPSDL: On-Policy Self-Distillation for Long-Context Language Models
item.content:encoded: Extending the effective context length of large language models (LLMs) remains a central challenge for real-world applications. While recent post-training methods have made progress in long-context scaling, they either rely on high-quality supervision data or sparse sequence-level rewards, leading to unstable and inefficient optimization. We propose OPSDL, an On-Policy Self-Distillation method for enhancing the Long-context capabilities of LLMs. Unlike other recent self-distillation methods that inject privileged information and rely on the model's in-context learning ability to act as a teacher, OPSDL leverages the model's own inherently strong short-context capability as a self-teacher to supervise its own generation in long-context scenarios. The model first generates responses conditioned on the full long-context, then the self-teacher provides per-token supervision signals via point-wise reverse KL divergence under the relevant extracted short-context. This dense token-level signal encourages faithful use of relevant evidence and mitigates hallucinations induced by irrelevant context. We evaluate OPSDL on long-context benchmarks across a range of models from 7B to 32B parameters. Results show consistent and substantial improvements across varying context lengths, outperforming standard post-training approaches such as SFT and DPO with higher sample efficiency. Notably, these gains are achieved without degrading general short-context performance. These findings highlight the effectiveness of OPSDL as a scalable and stable approach for long-context learning.

item.title: Denoising, Fast and Slow: Difficulty-Aware Adaptive Sampling for Image Generation
item.content:encoded: Diffusion- and flow-based models usually allocate compute uniformly across space, updating all patches with the same timestep and number of function evaluations. While convenient, this ignores the heterogeneity of natural images: some regions are easy to denoise, whereas others benefit from more refinement or additional context. Motivated by this, we explore patch-level noise scales for image synthesis. We find that naively varying timesteps across image tokens performs poorly, as it exposes the model to overly informative training states that do not occur at inference. We therefore introduce a timestep sampler that explicitly controls the maximum patch-level information available during training, and show that moving from global to patch-level timesteps already improves image generation over standard baselines. By further augmenting the model with a lightweight per-patch difficulty head, we enable adaptive samplers that allocate compute dynamically where it is most needed. Combined with noise levels varying over both space and diffusion time, this yields Patch Forcing (PF), a framework that advances easier regions earlier so they can provide context for harder ones. PF achieves superior results on class-conditional ImageNet, remains orthogonal to representation alignment and guidance methods, and scales to text-to-image synthesis. Our results suggest that patch-level denoising schedules provide a promising foundation for adaptive image generation.

item.title: Towards Self-Improving Error Diagnosis in Multi-Agent Systems
item.content:encoded: Large Language Model (LLM)-based Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) enable complex problem-solving but introduce significant debugging challenges, characterized by long interaction traces, inter-agent dependencies, and delayed error manifestation. Existing diagnostic approaches often rely on expensive expert annotation or ''LLM-as-a-judge'' paradigms, which struggle to pinpoint decisive error steps within extended contexts. In this paper, we introduce ErrorProbe, a self-improving framework for semantic failure attribution that identifies responsible agents and the originating error step. The framework operates via a three-stage pipeline: (1) operationalizing the MAS failure taxonomy to detect local anomalies, (2) performing symptom-driven backward tracing to prune irrelevant context, and (3) employing a specialized multi-agent team (Strategist, Investigator, Arbiter) to validate error hypotheses through tool-grounded execution. Crucially, ErrorProbe maintains a verified episodic memory that updates only when error patterns are confirmed by executable evidence, without the need for annotation. Experiments across the TracerTraj and Who&When benchmarks demonstrate that ErrorProbe significantly outperforms baselines, particularly in step-level localization, while the verified memory enables robust cross-domain transfer without retraining.

item.title: Match-Any-Events: Zero-Shot Motion-Robust Feature Matching Across Wide Baselines for Event Cameras
item.content:encoded: Event cameras have recently shown promising capabilities in instantaneous motion estimation due to their robustness to low light and fast motions. However, computing wide-baseline correspondence between two arbitrary views remains a significant challenge, since event appearance changes substantially with motion, and learning-based approaches are constrained by both scalability and limited wide-baseline supervision. We therefore introduce the first event matching model that achieves cross-dataset wide-baseline correspondence in a zero-shot manner: a single model trained once is deployed on unseen datasets without any target-domain fine-tuning or adaptation. To enable this capability, we introduce a motion-robust and computationally efficient attention backbone that learns multi-timescale features from event streams, augmented with sparsity-aware event token selection, making large-scale training on diverse wide-baseline supervision computationally feasible. To provide the supervision needed for wide-baseline generalization, we develop a robust event motion synthesis framework to generate large-scale event-matching datasets with augmented viewpoints, modalities, and motions. Extensive experiments across multiple benchmarks show that our framework achieves a 37.7% improvement over the previous best event feature matching methods. Code and data are available at: https://github.com/spikelab-jhu/Match-Any-Events.

item.title: Analytical Extraction of Conditional Sobol' Indices via Basis Decomposition of Polynomial Chaos Expansions
item.content:encoded: In uncertainty quantification, evaluating sensitivity measures under specific conditions (i.e., conditional Sobol' indices) is essential for systems with parameterized responses, such as spatial fields or varying operating conditions. Traditional approaches often rely on point-wise modeling, which is computationally expensive and may lack consistency across the parameter space. This paper demonstrates that for a pre-trained global Polynomial Chaos Expansion (PCE) model, the analytical conditional Sobol' indices are inherently embedded within its basis functions. By leveraging the tensor-product property of PCE bases, we reformulate the global expansion into a set of analytical coefficient fields that depend on the conditioning variables. Based on the preservation of orthogonality under conditional probability measures, we derive closed-form expressions for conditional variances and Sobol' indices. This framework bypasses the need for repetitive modeling or additional sampling, transforming conditional sensitivity analysis into a purely algebraic post-processing step. Numerical benchmarks indicate that the proposed method ensures physical coherence and offers superior numerical robustness and computational efficiency compared to conventional point-wise approaches.

item.title: Beyond Reproduction: A Paired-Task Framework for Assessing LLM Comprehension and Creativity in Literary Translation
item.content:encoded: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used for creative tasks such as literary translation. Yet translational creativity remains underexplored and is rarely evaluated at scale, while source-text comprehension is typically studied in isolation, despite the fact that, in professional translation, comprehension and creativity are tightly intertwined. We address these gaps with a paired-task framework applied to literary excerpts from 11 books. Task 1 assesses source-text comprehension, and Task 2 evaluates translational creativity through Units of Creative Potential (UCPs), such as metaphors and wordplay. Using a scalable evaluation setup that combines expert human annotations with UCP-based automatic scoring, we benchmark 23 models and four creativity-oriented prompts. Our findings show that strong comprehension does not translate into human-level creativity: models often produce literal or contextually inappropriate renderings, with particularly large gaps for the more distant English-Chinese language pair. Creativity-oriented prompts yield only modest gains, and only one model, Mistral-Large, comes close to human-level creativity (0.167 vs. 0.246). Across all model-prompt combinations, only three exceed a creativity score of 0.1, while the rest remain at or near zero.

item.title: ReTrack: Evidence-Driven Dual-Stream Directional Anchor Calibration Network for Composed Video Retrieval
item.content:encoded: With the rapid growth of video data, Composed Video Retrieval (CVR) has emerged as a novel paradigm in video retrieval and is receiving increasing attention from researchers. Unlike unimodal video retrieval methods, the CVR task takes a multi-modal query consisting of a reference video and a piece of modification text as input. The modification text conveys the user's intended alterations to the reference video. Based on this input, the model aims to retrieve the most relevant target video. In the CVR task, there exists a substantial discrepancy in information density between video and text modalities. Traditional composition methods tend to bias the composed feature toward the reference video, which leads to suboptimal retrieval performance. This limitation is significant due to the presence of three core challenges: (1) modal contribution entanglement, (2) explicit optimization of composed features, and (3) retrieval uncertainty. To address these challenges, we propose the evidence-dRivRn dual-sTream diRectionAl anChor calibration networK (ReTrack). ReTrack is the first CVR framework that improves multi-modal query understanding by calibrating directional bias in composed features. It consists of three key modules: Semantic Contribution Disentanglement, Composition Geometry Calibration, and Reliable Evidence-driven Alignment. Specifically, ReTrack estimates the semantic contribution of each modality to calibrate the directional bias of the composed feature. It then uses the calibrated directional anchors to compute bidirectional evidence that drives reliable composed-to-target similarity estimation. Moreover, ReTrack exhibits strong generalization to the Composed Image Retrieval (CIR) task, achieving SOTA performance across three benchmark datasets in both CVR and CIR scenarios. Codes are available at https://github.com/Lee-zixu/ReTrack

item.title: Sharpening Lightweight Models for Generalized Polyp Segmentation: A Boundary Guided Distillation from Foundation Models
item.content:encoded: Automated polyp segmentation is critical for early colorectal cancer detection and its prevention, yet remains challenging due to weak boundaries, large appearance variations, and limited annotated data. Lightweight segmentation models such as U-Net, U-Net++, and PraNet offer practical efficiency for clinical deployment but struggle to capture the rich semantic and structural cues required for accurate delineation of complex polyp regions. In contrast, large Vision Foundation Models (VFMs), including SAM, OneFormer, Mask2Former, and DINOv2, exhibit strong generalization but transfer poorly to polyp segmentation due to domain mismatch, insufficient boundary sensitivity, and high computational cost. To bridge this gap, we propose \textit{\textbf{LiteBounD}, a \underline{Li}gh\underline{t}w\underline{e}ight \underline{Boun}dary-guided \underline{D}istillation} framework that transfers complementary semantic and structural priors from multiple VFMs into compact segmentation backbones. LiteBounD introduces (i) a dual-path distillation mechanism that disentangles semantic and boundary-aware representations, (ii) a frequency-aware alignment strategy that supervises low-frequency global semantics and high-frequency boundary details separately, and (iii) a boundary-aware decoder that fuses multi-scale encoder features with distilled semantically rich boundary information for precise segmentation. Extensive experiments on both seen (Kvasir-SEG, CVC-ClinicDB) and unseen (ColonDB, CVC-300, ETIS) datasets demonstrate that LiteBounD consistently outperforms its lightweight baselines by a significant margin and achieves performance competitive with state-of-the-art methods, while maintaining the efficiency required for real-time clinical use. Our code is available at https://github.com/lostinrepo/LiteBounD.

item.title: 高效深度补全网络EfficientPENet在嵌入式平台的应用
item.content:encoded:
EfficientPENet实现高效实时深度补全，适用于边缘计算。



摘要： 本文提出EfficientPENet，一种基于稀疏LiDAR和RGB图像的高效深度补全网络。该网络采用ConvNeXt作为主干架构，引入稀疏不变卷积和CSPN优化预测，通过双分支结构提升性能。RGB分支使用预训练的ConvNeXt模块，特征融合后通过多尺度监督解码。测试时引入位置感知增强策略，提升推理稳定性。在KITTI基准上，EfficientPENet达到631.94 mm的RMSE，参数量为36.24M，延迟20.51 ms，帧率48.76 FPS，相比BP-Net参数减少3.7倍，速度提升23倍，具备实时部署能力。

item.title: MedProbeBench：评估医学大语言模型证据整合能力的基准测试
item.content:encoded:
引入MedProbeBench评估医学大模型的证据整合与指南生成能力。



摘要： 本文介绍了MedProbeBench，这是首个利用高质量临床指南作为专家参考的基准测试，用于评估大语言模型在医学领域中整合多步骤证据和生成指南的能力。研究提出了MedProbe-Eval评估框架，包含1200多个任务适应性评分标准和5130多个原子声明的精细证据验证机制。对17个大语言模型和深度研究代理的评估揭示了当前模型在证据整合和指南生成方面的显著不足，突显出与专家级临床指南开发之间的差距。

item.title: 基于离散逻辑门的深度学习网络在硅芯片上的实现
item.content:encoded:
DLGNs可高效部署于硅芯片，实现高速图像分类。



摘要： 本文提出了一种将不同iable Logic Gate Networks (DLGNs) 映射到数字CMOS标准单元库的方法，通过将训练好的模型转换为门级网表，实现其在硅芯片上的定制化部署。研究还引入了一种新的损失函数，以优化电路面积和功耗。实验中首次在模拟环境中实现了DLGN的硅芯片布局，并在SkyWater 130nm工艺下进行了后布局功耗分析。结果表明，该定制宏可在MNIST数据集上以97%的准确率每秒执行4180万次分类，功耗仅为83.88mW。

item.title: ContraPrompt：通过对比推理过程优化提示方法
item.content:encoded:
ContraPrompt通过对比模型的两次推理过程提升提示优化效果。



摘要： 本文提出ContraPrompt，一种基于模型在相同输入下成功与失败推理过程对比的提示优化方法。该方法通过分析模型在重试中因反馈而改变的推理路径，提取出优化信号，优于现有方法GEPA。实验表明，在多个基准测试中，ContraPrompt表现出显著提升，且其核心组件——双推理路径对比在消融实验中被证实为关键因素。

item.title: 流式持续学习中任务划分对评估结果的影响研究
item.content:encoded:
任务划分方式显著影响流式持续学习的评估结果。



摘要： 本文探讨了流式持续学习（Streaming CL）中将连续数据流划分为离散任务的步骤对评估结果的影响。研究表明，不同的任务划分方式会引发不同的CL范式，从而导致不同的基准结论。作者引入了一种基于可塑性与稳定性轮廓的任务划分框架，并通过边界轮廓敏感度（BPS）分析任务划分边界扰动对CL性能的影响。实验表明，任务划分方式直接影响预测误差、遗忘和反向迁移，且较短的任务划分更易受边界变化影响。这表明任务划分应被视为评估的重要变量。

item.title: 扩散语言模型在低比特量化下的鲁棒性研究
item.content:encoded:
扩散模型在低比特量化下表现更稳定，适合高效部署。



摘要： 本文研究了后训练量化（PTQ）技术在基于扩散的代码语言模型（CoDA）中的应用与鲁棒性。对比了GPTQ和改进的HAWQ算法在CoDA与自回归模型Qwen3-1.7B上的表现，发现CoDA在低比特位宽（2-4位）下具有更高的鲁棒性，准确率下降更小。此外，基于HAWQ的混合精度配置能够在准确率、延迟和内存之间实现平滑权衡，表明扩散模型在量化部署方面具有潜在优势。

item.title: SignDPO：多层级直接偏好优化框架提升手语翻译对齐效果
item.content:encoded:
SignDPO通过多层级偏好对齐提升手语翻译准确性。



摘要： 本文提出SignDPO，一种多层级直接偏好优化框架，用于提升基于骨骼的手语翻译对齐效果。传统模型依赖模仿学习，难以捕捉手语的细粒度时空特征，导致语义漂移。SignDPO通过结构化偏好对齐，从空间、时间及语言维度优化模型。其包含三个关键设计：层次扰动策略生成非优选样本，自引导机制利用解码器注意力识别关键骨骼区域，以及自动语言级偏好生成器。实验表明，SignDPO在多个基准数据集上表现优异，超越现有无词干方法并接近有词干方法。

item.title: 基于数据增强的轻量级无人机检测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种高效数据增强方法提升轻量模型的无人机检测性能。



摘要： 本文针对边缘设备上轻量级目标检测模型（如YOLOv11 Nano）在无人机检测中的性能不足问题，提出了一种结合Mosaic策略和HSV颜色空间适应的数据增强方法。该方法有效避免了合成伪影和过拟合问题，并在四个标准数据集上显著提升了平均精度（mAP）。实验还表明，在雾天等复杂环境下，该方法在检测精度与稳定性之间取得了最佳平衡，优于MixUp等其他方法。

item.title: 通用大语言模型在药物流行病学研究设计中的表现评估
item.content:encoded:
通用大模型在药物流行病学设计中表现优于生物医学模型。



摘要： 本研究评估了通用大语言模型（GPT-4o 和 DeepSeek-R1）与生物医学微调模型在药物流行病学研究设计中的表现。通过分析 HMA-EMA 目录和 Sentinel 系统的 46 份协议，发现 GPT-4o 在 LTM 提示策略下表现出最高的相关性和逻辑性评分，而生物医学模型整体表现较差，尤其在解释力和本体编码匹配方面存在不足。所有模型在本体编码映射上均表现有限，但 LTM 提示策略显著提升了推理稳定性。研究结论表明，目前通用大模型在药物流行病学设计中仍更具优势。

item.title: 基于冻结大模型的高效情感对话系统研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种高效情感对话系统，提升机器情感表达能力。



摘要： 本文探讨了情感在语音对话系统中的重要性，并指出当前基于大语言模型的 empathetic chatbot 在训练过程中面临数据成本高、情感表达不足以及模型遗忘等问题。为解决这些挑战，研究者提出 FreezeEmpath 方法，通过仅使用现有语音指令数据和语音情绪识别数据进行训练，同时保持大语言模型参数不变。实验表明，该方法能够生成更具情感表达力的语音，并在情感对话、语音情绪识别和语音问答任务中表现优于其他模型，验证了其训练策略的有效性。

item.title: 大型语言模型在系统动力学AI辅助中的性能评估
item.content:encoded:
评估大语言模型在系统动力学任务中的表现，包括因果回路图提取和互动讨论。



摘要： 本文系统评估了从云服务API到本地开源模型的大语言模型家族在两个专为系统动力学AI辅助设计的基准测试中的表现：CLD Leaderboard（53项测试，结构化因果回路图提取）和Discussion Leaderboard（交互式模型讨论、反馈解释和模型构建指导）。结果显示，云模型在CLD提取任务中达到77%-89%的通过率，而最佳本地模型（Kimi K2.5 GGUF Q3）也达到77%。在Discussion任务中，本地模型在模型构建步骤上表现优异，但在错误修复方面仅达0%-50%，主要受本地部署长上下文提示的内存限制影响。研究还分析了模型类型对性能的影响，比较了推理与指令调优架构、GGUF与MLX后端以及不同量化级别（Q3/Q4_K_M/MLX-3bit/MLX-4bit/MLX-6bit）的表现。结果表明后端选择比量化级别对实际性能影响更大。此外，文章还提供了所有本地模型的完整参数扫描数据、清理后的计时数据及在Apple Silicon上运行671B-123B参数模型的实践指南。

item.title: 基于时空高斯场的电动汽车充电需求预测模型研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新型电动汽车充电需求预测方法，提升电网管理精度。



摘要： 本文针对电动汽车充电需求预测中依赖过时数据的问题，引入了苏格兰2022至2025年的大规模纵向数据集，并将其作为开放基准发布。在此基础上，作者将充电需求建模为时空潜在高斯场，并通过集成嵌套拉普拉斯近似（INLA）进行贝叶斯推断。该模型在统一的概率框架下同时捕捉空间依赖性、时间动态和协变量效应。实验表明，该方法在站点级预测任务中表现优于机器学习基线模型，同时提供可解释的空间和时间分解以及不确定性量化，对风险驱动的基础设施规划具有重要意义。

item.title: 基于CUTS的强化学习方法提升大语言模型推理能力
item.content:encoded:
CUTS方法提升大模型推理多样性与泛化能力。



摘要： 本文探讨了强化学习在大语言模型中的应用，指出随着模型规模扩大，基准测试中出现解法同质化问题，导致优势信号消失和模式崩溃。为此，提出一种无需参数调整的解码策略Constrained Uniform Top-K Sampling (CUTS)，通过从高置信度候选中均匀采样来保持结构探索。将CUTS整合到Mixed-CUTS训练框架中，结合利用与探索性 rollout，增强组内优势方差。实验表明该方法有效防止策略退化，并显著提升跨域泛化能力，在AIME25基准上Pass@1准确率提升15.1%。

item.title: Wan-Image：重塑图像生成的生产力工具
item.content:encoded:
Wan-Image提升图像生成专业性与可控性。



摘要： Wan-Image是一款统一的视觉生成系统，旨在将图像生成模型从随意合成工具转变为专业的生产力工具。它结合了大语言模型的认知能力与扩散变压器的高保真像素合成能力，实现对用户复杂意图的精准转化。该系统通过大规模多模态数据、精细标注引擎和强化学习数据，实现了超长文本渲染、多样肖像生成、调色板引导生成、多主体身份保留、连贯序列视觉生成、多模态交互编辑、原生透明通道生成和高效4K合成等专家级功能。在多项评估中，Wan-Image性能优于Seedream 5.0 Lite和GPT Image 1.5，与Nano Banana Pro相当。它正在重新定义电商、娱乐、教育和个人生产力领域的视觉合成边界。

item.title: 宗教语料微调大语言模型的伦理推理模式比较研究
item.content:encoded:
不同宗教语料微调模型在伦理推理上呈现系统性差异。



摘要： 本研究构建了六个Llama-3.1-8B变体，包括一个未修改的基准模型和五个分别基于基督教、伊斯兰教、犹太教、印度教和佛教经典文本进行LoRA微调的模型。所有模型使用相同的17个标准化伦理提示进行测试，涵盖道德困境、博弈论情景、公共政策问题及心理自我评估。研究发现，微调模型的伦理推理模式与基准模型有显著差异，且与训练传统一致，结构可解释。高共识伦理问题如电车难题在不同温度下保持高度一致性，而争议性问题在高温下出现传统特异性分歧。基准模型整体响应一致性最高（平均88.3%），表明微调引入了传统特异性信号和更高的采样敏感性。研究为文化与伦理分析提供了方法论参考。

item.title: 高爆炸驱动多材料冲击动力学AI模型数据集HEAT
item.content:encoded:
HEAT数据集用于训练和验证多材料冲击动力学的AI模型。



摘要： 本文介绍了HEAT数据集，这是一个用于训练和验证高爆炸驱动多材料冲击动力学AI模型的物理丰富数据集。该数据集包含二维、轴对称的模拟数据，涵盖多种材料和复杂物理现象，如冲击传播、动量传递、塑性变形和热效应。数据集分为两个部分：扩展冲击圆柱（CYL）和扰动层界面（PLI），提供了压力、密度、温度、位置、速度等时间序列信息，为AI/ML模型提供了基准测试数据。

item.title: 多模态特征对短视频观众参与度的影响研究
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示多模态特征对短视频感官与行为参与度的非线性影响。



摘要： 本研究基于信息感官价值理论，构建了一个结合多模态特征分析与人工评估的计算模型，用于预测短视频的感官和行为参与度。通过对1200个短视频的分析及在三个平台共14,492个数据集的验证，发现信息感官价值（MSV）与感官参与度呈正相关，但与行为参与度呈现倒U型关系：高MSV增强感官刺激，而中等MSV最优化行为参与。该研究深化了对短视频参与机制的理解，并提供了一个强大的计算工具，推动短视频领域的研究发展。

item.title: 基于场景注意力的驾驶员注视点估计方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出UD-FSG数据集与SGAP-Gaze模型提升驾驶员注视点估计精度。



摘要： 本文提出了一个包含同步驾驶员面部和交通场景图像的基准数据集UD-FSG，用于改进驾驶员注视点估计。基于此数据集，作者设计了SGAP-Gaze模型，通过融合面部特征和场景信息，利用Transformer注意力机制计算空间场景网格的注意力得分，从而更准确地预测注视点。实验结果显示，该模型在UD-FSG和LBW数据集上的平均像素误差分别达到104.73和63.48，比现有方法减少了23.5%。分析表明，SGAP-Gaze在所有空间范围内均表现优异，尤其在场景边缘区域，有助于更全面理解驾驶员注意力分布。

item.title: 基于意图精炼的机器人控制架构ResVLA
item.content:encoded:
ResVLA通过意图精炼提升机器人控制性能。



摘要： 本文针对具身智能中高阶语义理解与低阶物理控制之间的时空尺度不匹配问题，提出了一种新的架构ResVLA。该架构从传统的“从噪声生成”范式转向“从意图精炼”，通过频谱分析将控制分解为全局意图和局部动态。模型以预测的意图为锚点，专注于通过残差扩散桥优化局部动态，从而提升了生成效率和鲁棒性。实验表明，ResVLA在仿真和真实机器人任务中均表现出色，具有更快的收敛速度和更强的适应性。

item.title: 基于多模态的虚假新闻检测模型RAMM研究
item.content:encoded:
提出RAMM模型解决虚假新闻检测中的叙事一致性和领域知识不足问题。



摘要： 近年来，多模态多领域虚假新闻检测受到广泛关注，但仍面临两大挑战：一是现有模型无法捕捉跨实例的叙事一致性，难以应对社交媒体上集群式虚假新闻的传播；二是传统模型缺乏领域特定知识，难以适应新兴事件或小众话题。为此，本文提出 Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Model (RAMM)，该模型以多模态大语言模型为骨干，结合抽象叙事对齐模块和语义表示对齐模块，有效提升虚假新闻检测性能。实验结果表明，RAMM在三个公开数据集上表现优异，代码已开源。

item.title: DASH-KV：一种基于深度哈希的高效注意力机制框架
item.content:encoded:
DASH-KV通过近似最近邻搜索提升长上下文推理效率。



摘要： 本文提出DASH-KV，一种基于深度哈希的注意力机制加速框架。该方法将注意力重新定义为近似最近邻搜索，通过异构编码架构区分查询与键的精度和重用特性，并引入动态混合精度机制以平衡效率与准确性。实验表明，DASH-KV在LongBench数据集上显著优于现有方法，同时将推理复杂度从O(N^2)降低到线性O(N)，在保持全注意力性能的同时大幅提升了计算效率。

item.title: 欧盟AI法案在软件开发中的实践挑战与解决方案
item.content:encoded:
文章探讨欧盟AI法案在团队层面的实施难题及解决路径。



摘要： 文章研究了欧盟AI法案在实际软件开发中的应用挑战，指出当前缺乏团队层面的实证数据。通过一项嵌入AI初创公司的行动研究，作者提出了一种从法律文本到具体行动的转化流程，通过内部专家协作将法规要求转化为可执行策略。研究发现，从业者对监管要求的感知存在三种模式：趋同、现有实践和脱节。这揭示了治理可能被真正执行或仅流于形式的区别。研究强调，只有当合规性与用户体验或开发需求相关时，从业者才会优先考虑，而验证导向的要求常被视为形式主义。专家协作有助于将治理从外部强加转变为共同责任，使隐性的治理工作显性化并成为集体行动。

item.title: TrEEStealer：针对TEE保护决策树的高效提取攻击
item.content:encoded:
研究提出TrEEStealer攻击，可高效窃取TEE保护的决策树模型。



摘要： 本文研究了在可信执行环境（TEE）保护下的决策树模型面临的模型提取攻击问题。现有黑盒攻击方法存在查询密集、假设严格或依赖API信息等缺陷。作者提出TrEEStealer攻击方法，利用TEE特有的侧信道信息，无需强假设即可高效提取决策树模型。该方法通过结合控制流信息和被动追踪技术提高查询效率，并在AMD SEV和Intel SGX平台上验证其有效性。实验发现多个流行库存在漏洞，TrEEStealer相比以往攻击方法具有更高的效率和提取精度，表明TEE无法有效防止控制流泄露。

item.title: 多尺度深度结构相似性模型提升图像质量评估
item.content:encoded:
多尺度DeepSSIM模型提升图像质量评估效果。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于多尺度特征的深度结构相似性模型（MSDS），通过在不同金字塔层级上独立计算DeepSSIM并使用可学习的全局权重进行融合，有效提升了图像质量评估的准确性。实验表明，该方法在多个基准数据集上均优于单尺度基线模型，且计算复杂度几乎不变。研究证实了空间尺度在深度感知相似性建模中的重要性，并提供了一个最小化的测试平台来验证这一因素。

item.title: 临床适用的低分辨率MRI分辨率增强方法CAHAL
item.content:encoded:
CAHAL提升MRI图像分辨率并减少结构失真。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为CAHAL的临床适用性分辨率增强框架，旨在解决常规临床MRI中厚层、各向异性采集导致的定量分析限制。与现有生成式超分辨率方法不同，CAHAL通过物理信息引导，采用确定性的双变量专家混合架构，在患者原始采集空间中进行处理，避免了解剖幻觉和结构扭曲。该方法结合边缘惩罚的空间重建、频域谱一致性匹配以及分割引导的语义一致性约束进行优化，训练数据通过基于物理的降维生成，确保泛化能力。在T1加权和FLAIR序列上的验证表明，CAHAL在准确性和效率上均优于现有方法。

item.title: Transformer基因语言模型的可解释性研究
item.content:encoded:
研究证明Transformer模型也能提供可靠的生物学解释。



摘要： 本文探讨了基于Transformer的基因语言模型（gLMs）在基因序列预测中的可解释性。通过引入AttnLRP方法，对DNABERT-2模型进行解释分析，并与传统CNN模型进行对比。实验表明，AttnLRP能够捕捉到已知的生物学模式，说明gLMs同样可以用于生成有价值的生物学见解。该研究推动了基因语言模型的可解释性研究，并促进了不同架构间解释一致性的比较。

item.title: 线性教师-学生模型中的瞬态谱过渡现象研究
item.content:encoded:
研究线性模型中梯度下降过程的瞬态谱变化及过拟合现象。



摘要： 本文提出一个可解析的随机矩阵模型，用于模拟线性教师-学生设置下梯度流中的瞬态信号现象。研究发现，当孤立特征值从噪声主成分中分离后，会经历一个短暂的过渡阶段，随后在过拟合阶段重新融入噪声中。输入协方差的各向异性是导致学习动态快慢方向差异的关键因素。通过两块协方差模型，推导出对称权重矩阵的时变主成分谱，并利用秩二行列式公式得出单峰教师的异常值条件。结果揭示了依赖信号强度和协方差各向异性的瞬态Baik-Ben Arous-Péché（BBP）转变现象，验证了理论与有限规模模拟的一致性。研究为早期停止提供了一个基于各向异性和噪声的最小可解机制。

item.title: GSQ：一种高效的标量量化方法，缩小与向量量化之间的性能差距
item.content:encoded:
GSQ通过优化标量量化提升模型推理效率和准确性。



摘要： 本文探讨了标量量化与向量量化方法之间的性能差距是否可以被克服。作者提出了一种名为GSQ的后训练标量量化方法，通过联合学习坐标网格分配和组内尺度，使用Gumbel-Softmax松弛实现离散网格的优化。GSQ在低比特宽度下表现出色，在Llama-3.1-8B/70B-Instruct模型上接近向量量化方法的性能，同时兼容现有标量推理框架。此外，GSQ还能扩展到千亿参数的混合专家模型，显示出良好的可扩展性。

item.title: 面向连续查询空间的大型语言模型响应缓存框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新型LLM响应缓存框架，适应连续查询空间。



摘要： 随着大型语言模型（LLMs）的普及，通过缓存语义相似查询的响应以减少推理成本和延迟变得至关重要。传统缓存框架假设查询空间是离散且有限的，但在实际应用中，LLM的查询分布在无限、连续的嵌入空间中，这一假设不再适用。本文建立了首个在不确定环境下针对连续查询空间的LLM响应缓存理论框架。通过引入动态ε-网离散化与核岭回归，系统能够量化估计不确定性，并跨连续语义邻域泛化部分反馈。研究还开发了离线学习和在线自适应算法，以降低切换缓存响应的成本。理论分析表明，该在线算法在对抗最优连续预言机时具有次线性遗憾界，实验结果表明该框架在计算和切换开销上优于现有方法。

item.title: 基于嵌入空间的文本到图像生成公平性优化方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种无监督的文本到图像生成公平性优化方法。



摘要： 本文针对现代文本到图像生成模型中存在的社会偏见问题，提出了一种在推理阶段进行偏差缓解的方法。该方法通过嵌入空间分析和修正偏差，不改变模型权重、提示或数据集，同时保持提示语义和视觉上下文的一致性。实验表明，该方法在FLUX 1.0-Dev和Stable Diffusion 3.5-Large上表现出色，有效解决了公平性与一致性之间的权衡问题，并提出了新的评估指标Concept Coherence Score（CCS）以更准确地衡量语义保留程度。

item.title: BasketHAR: A Multimodal Dataset for Human Activity Recognition and Sport Analysis in Basketball Training Scenarios
item.content:encoded: Human Activity Recognition (HAR) involves the automatic identification of user activities and has gained significant research interest due to its broad applicability. Most HAR systems rely on supervised learning, which necessitates large, diverse, and well-annotated datasets. However, existing datasets predominantly focus on basic activities such as walking, standing, and stair navigation, limiting their utility in specialized contexts like sports performance analysis. To address this gap, we present BasketHAR, a novel multimodal HAR dataset tailored for basketball training, encompassing a diverse set of professional-level actions. BasketHAR includes comprehensive motion data from inertial measurement units (accelerometers and gyroscopes), angular velocity, magnetic field, heart rate, skin temperature, and synchronized video recordings. We also provide a baseline multimodal alignment method to benchmark performance. Experimental results underscore the dataset's complexity and suitability for advanced HAR tasks. Furthermore, we highlight its potential applications in the analysis of basketball training sessions and in the generation of specialized performance reports, representing a valuable resource for future research in HAR and sports analytics. The dataset are publicly accessible at https://huggingface.co/datasets/Xian-Gao/BasketHAR licensed under Apache License 2.0.

item.title: Neural Garbage Collection: Learning to Forget while Learning to Reason
item.content:encoded: Chain-of-thought reasoning has driven striking advances in language model capability, yet every reasoning step grows the KV cache, creating a bottleneck to scaling this paradigm further. Current approaches manage these constraints on the model's behalf using hand-designed criteria. A more scalable approach would let end-to-end learning subsume this design choice entirely, following a broader pattern in deep learning. After all, if a model can learn to reason, why can't it learn to forget? We introduce Neural Garbage Collection (NGC), in which a language model learns to forget while learning to reason, trained end-to-end from outcome-based task reward alone. As the model reasons, it periodically pauses, decides which KV cache entries to evict, and continues to reason conditioned on the remaining cache. By treating tokens in a chain-of-thought and cache-eviction decisions as discrete actions sampled from the language model, we can use reinforcement learning to jointly optimize how the model reasons and how it manages its own memory: what the model evicts shapes what it remembers, what it remembers shapes its reasoning, and the correctness of that reasoning determines its reward. Crucially, the model learns this behavior entirely from a single learning signal - the outcome-based task reward - without supervised fine-tuning or proxy objectives. On Countdown, AMC, and AIME tasks, NGC maintains strong accuracy relative to the full-cache upper bound at 2-3x peak KV cache size compression and substantially outperforms eviction baselines. Our results are a first step towards a broader vision where end-to-end optimization drives both capability and efficiency in language models.

item.title: Continual Safety Alignment via Gradient-Based Sample Selection
item.content:encoded: Large language models require continuous adaptation to new tasks while preserving safety alignment. However, fine-tuning on even benign data often compromises safety behaviors, including refusal of harmful requests, truthfulness, and commonsense reasoning. We investigate which training samples cause alignment drift through a data-centric lens. Our empirical analysis shows samples contribute unequally: high-gradient samples cause greater safety degradation and drive models toward pretrained distributions, while moderate-gradient samples enable task learning with minimal alignment loss. We propose gradient-based sample selection that filters high-gradient samples during fine-tuning. Across multiple model families on continual domain tasks, our method substantially improves alignment preservation while maintaining competitive task performance, without requiring curated safe data or architectural modifications. Our method is robust across selection ratios, task orderings, and diverse attack benchmarks.

item.title: Concept Inconsistency in Dermoscopic Concept Bottleneck Models: A Rough-Set Analysis of the Derm7pt Dataset
item.content:encoded: Concept Bottleneck Models (CBMs) route predictions exclusively through a clinically grounded concept layer, binding interpretability to concept-label consistency. When a dataset contains concept-level inconsistencies, identical concept profiles mapped to conflicting diagnosis labels create an unresolvable bottleneck that imposes a hard ceiling on achievable accuracy. In this paper, we apply rough set theory to the Derm7pt dermoscopy benchmark and characterize the full extent and clinical structure of this inconsistency. Among 305 unique concept profiles formed by the 7 dermoscopic criteria of the 7-point melanoma checklist, 50 (16.4%) are inconsistent, spanning 306 images (30.3% of the dataset). This yields a theoretical accuracy ceiling of 92.1%, independent of backbone architecture or training strategy for CBMs that exclusively operate with hard concepts. In addition, we characterize the conflict-severity distribution, identify the clinical features most responsible for boundary ambiguity, and evaluate two filtering strategies with quantified effects on dataset composition and CBM interpretability. Symmetric removal of all boundary-region images yields Derm7pt+, a fully consistent benchmark subset of 705 images with perfect quality of classification and no hard accuracy ceiling. Building on this filtered dataset, we present a hard CBM evaluated across 19 backbone architectures from the EfficientNet, DenseNet, ResNet, and Wide ResNet families. Under symmetric filtering, explored for completeness, EfficientNet-B5 achieves the best label F1 score (0.85) and label accuracy (0.90) on the held-out test set, with a concept accuracy of 0.70. Under asymmetric filtering, EfficientNet-B7 leads across all four metrics, reaching a label F1 score of 0.82 and concept accuracy of 0.70. These results establish reproducible baselines for concept-consistent CBM evaluation on dermoscopic data.

item.title: Scalable Memristive-Friendly Reservoir Computing for Time Series Classification
item.content:encoded: Memristive devices present a promising foundation for next-generation information processing by combining memory and computation within a single physical substrate. This unique characteristic enables efficient, fast, and adaptive computing, particularly well suited for deep learning applications. Among recent developments, the memristive-friendly echo state network (MF-ESN) has emerged as a promising approach that combines memristive-inspired dynamics with the training simplicity of reservoir computing, where only the readout layer is learned. Building on this framework, we propose memristive-friendly parallelized reservoirs (MARS), a simplified yet more effective architecture that enables efficient scalable parallel computation and deeper model composition through novel subtractive skip connections. This design yields two key advantages: substantial training speedups of up to 21x over the inherently lightweight echo state network baseline and significantly improved predictive performance. Moreover, MARS demonstrates what is possible with parallel memristive-friendly reservoir computing: on several long sequence benchmarks our compact gradient-free models substantially outperform strong gradient-based sequence models such as LRU, S5, and Mamba, while reducing full training time from minutes or hours down seconds or even only a few hundred milliseconds. Our work positions parallel memristive-friendly computing as a promising route towards scalable neuromorphic learning systems that combine high predictive capability with radically improved computational efficiency, while providing a clear pathway to energy-efficient, low-latency implementations on emerging memristive and in-memory hardware.

item.title: Frictionless Love: Associations Between AI Companion Roles and Behavioral Addiction
item.content:encoded: AI companion chatbots increasingly shape how people seek social and emotional connection, sometimes substituting for relationships with romantic partners, friends, teachers, or even therapists. When these systems adopt those metaphorical roles, they are not neutral: such roles structure people's ways of interacting, distribute perceived AI harms and benefits, and may reflect behavioral addiction signs. Yet these role-dependent risks remain poorly understood. We analyze 248,830 posts from seven prominent Reddit communities describing interactions with AI companions. We identify ten recurring metaphorical roles (for example, soulmate, philosopher, and coach) and show that each role supports distinct ways of interacting. We then extract the perceived AI harms and AI benefits associated with these role-specific interactions and link them to behavioral addiction signs, all of which has been inferred from the text in the posts. AI soulmate companions are associated with romance-centered ways of interacting, offering emotional support but also introducing emotional manipulation and distress, culminating in strong attachment. In contrast, AI coach and guardian companions are associated with practical benefits such as personal growth and task support, yet are nonetheless more frequently associated with behavioral addiction signs such as daily life disruptions and damage to offline relationships. These findings show that metaphorical roles are a central ethical design concern for responsible AI companions.

item.title: AdaCluster: Adaptive Query-Key Clustering for Sparse Attention in Video Generation
item.content:encoded: Video diffusion transformers (DiTs) suffer from prohibitive inference latency due to quadratic attention complexity. Existing sparse attention methods either overlook semantic similarity or fail to adapt to heterogeneous token distributions across layers, leading to model performance degradation. We propose AdaCluster, a training-free adaptive clustering framework that accelerates the generation of DiTs while preserving accuracy. AdaCluster applies an angle-similarity-preserving clustering method to query vectors for higher compression, and designs a euclidean-similarity-preserving clustering method for keys, covering cluster number assignment, threshold-wise adaptive clustering, and efficient critical cluster selection. Experiments on CogVideoX-2B, HunyuanVideo, and Wan-2.1 on one A40 GPU demonstrate up to 1.67-4.31x speedup with negligible quality degradation.

item.title: LiteResearcher: A Scalable Agentic RL Training Framework for Deep Research Agent
item.content:encoded: Reinforcement Learning (RL) has emerged as a powerful training paradigm for LLM-based agents. However, scaling agentic RL for deep research remains constrained by two coupled challenges: hand-crafted synthetic data fails to elicit genuine real-world search capabilities, and real-world search dependency during RL training introduces instability and prohibitive cost, which limits the scalability of Agentic RL. LiteResearcher is a training framework that makes Agentic RL scalable: by constructing a lite virtual world that mirrors real-world search dynamics, we enable a continuously improving training recipe that empowers a tiny search agent to outperform large-scale open-source and commercial models (e.g., Tongyi DeepResearch and Claude-4.5 Sonnet). Specifically, on common benchmarks such as GAIA and Xbench, our LiteResearcher-4B achieves open-source state-of-the-art results of 71.3% and 78.0% respectively, demonstrating that scalable RL training is a key enabler for Deep Research Agents.

item.title: TPS-CalcBench: A Benchmark and Diagnostic Evaluation Framework for LLM Analytical Calculation Competence in Hypersonic Thermal Protection System Engineering
item.content:encoded: Deploying LLMs as reasoning assistants in safety-critical aerospace engineering requires stricter evaluation criteria than general scientific benchmarks. In hypersonic thermal protection system (TPS) design, inaccurate stagnation-point heat flux or boundary-layer calculations may cause catastrophic design margin violations. Models with numerically reasonable but physically invalid answers are more dangerous than those declining to respond. Current scientific benchmarks only test abstract math and basic physics, evaluate final answers solely, ignore engineering reasoning processes, and cannot detect such critical failures. We propose TPS-CalcBench, the first diagnostic benchmark for closed-form analytical calculations in hypersonic aerodynamics and high-temperature gas dynamics that experienced TPS engineers conduct without simulations. Our contributions include domain-oriented task taxonomy with 4 difficulty levels and 8 categories from Anderson's textbook, dual-track evaluation measuring result accuracy and reasoning quality via an 8-dimension rubric and calibrated judge with human audit to identify right answer wrong reasoning issues, human-AI data pipeline producing 420 high-confidence core items and 810 noise-controlled pre-gating items from 4560 raw data, noise-sensitivity analysis measuring data quality impacts on model ranking, and three diagnostic intervention methods: DFA-TPS fine-tuning, RAG-EQ retrieval grounding and PA-CoT process-aware prompting. Tests on 13 models from 7 groups show wide performance differences (KPI 12.6-87.9), hidden formula selection defects, data-driven rank changes and effective intervention improvements, establishing a complete diagnose-evaluate-intervene framework for safety-critical engineering LLM deployment assessment.

item.title: 基于软谐波解的条件异常检测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种非参数条件异常检测方法，用于识别异常标签。



摘要： 本文研究了条件异常检测问题，旨在识别具有异常响应或类别标签的数据实例。作者提出了一种基于软谐波解的新非参数方法，通过估计标签置信度来检测异常标注。为进一步提高检测效果，对解进行正则化处理，避免检测孤立样本和分布支持边界上的样本。实验结果表明，该方法在多个合成数据集和UCI机器学习数据集上优于基线方法，并在真实世界电子健康记录数据集中有效识别出异常的患者管理决策。

item.title: 基于Pi-PINN的可迁移物理信息神经网络方法
item.content:encoded:
Pi-PINN提升PINN在PDE求解中的效率与泛化能力。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于快速伪逆物理信息神经网络（Pi-PINN）的可迁移学习方法，旨在解决传统PINN在缺乏训练样本时泛化能力差的问题。Pi-PINN通过共享嵌入空间学习可迁移的物理信息表示，并利用最小二乘最优伪逆实现快速求解。研究还探讨了数据驱动多任务损失与物理信息损失的协同作用，为更高效的PINN设计提供见解。实验表明，Pi-PINN在多种PDE问题中表现出色，预测速度比传统PINN快100-1000倍，相对误差低10-100倍，适用于未知PDE实例的求解。

item.title: SciML在大气气溶胶微物理过程模拟中的应用研究
item.content:encoded:
SciML可用于提升大气气溶胶模拟精度，关键设计影响其效果。



摘要： 本文研究了利用科学机器学习（SciML）模拟器改进全球大气模型中气溶胶微物理过程的可行性。通过在E3SMv2模型的MAM4模块中测试简单前馈神经网络，探讨了网络结构复杂度、变量归一化等关键设计对模拟精度的影响。研究发现，有效的缩放策略和训练收敛是提升模拟准确性的关键，简单的网络结构在适度规模下可有效再现气溶胶浓度变化特征。这些成果为后续模拟器开发提供了实用指导，并可能适用于其他大气物理过程的模拟。

item.title: 子令牌路由提升Transformer效率
item.content:encoded:
研究子令牌路由提升Transformer模型效率与压缩性能。



摘要： 本文探讨了在LoRA适配的Transformer中，通过子令牌级别的路由机制来提高模型效率。与以往基于token、页面、头或层的粗粒度控制不同，该方法在token内部进行更精细的路由。研究提出两种设计：一种是不依赖查询的路由子空间LoRA与值组路由结合，用于语言建模的压缩；另一种是基于查询的预测器选择器，用于保持下游任务行为。实验表明，这种细粒度路由机制能有效提升压缩与质量的平衡，并揭示了令牌级别与子令牌级别路由的互补性。

item.title: 视觉-语言-动作模型在家庭任务中的安全评估与改进
item.content:encoded:
研究分析VLAs在家庭任务中的安全性及性能评估问题。



摘要： 本文探讨了视觉-语言-动作模型（VLAs）在家庭任务中的应用，指出当前基于最终状态的评估方法可能忽略操作过程中的安全性问题，导致性能评估失真。作者对B1K基准上的先进模型进行了深入分析，从鲁棒性、安全性、任务意识等方面评估策略，并提出新的评估协议以更准确地衡量复杂场景下的真实性能。最后，文章讨论了现有VLAs的局限性，并为未来研究提供了方向。

item.title: AI辅助编程中的领域知识文档规范
item.content:encoded:
AI辅助编程引入领域知识文档以确保科学正确性。



摘要： 文章介绍了AI辅助编程的最新进展，强调通过引入如GROUNDING.md这样的领域知识文档来提升代码质量和科学准确性。该文档包含硬性约束和社区共识参数，确保生成的代码符合最佳实践，即使非专业开发者也能创建可靠软件。这种机制不仅提高开发效率，也增强了审查者和使用者的信心，同时让领域专家在去中心化的软件开发中保持参与。

item.title: 基于采样的有限时间轨迹与策略优化框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种基于采样的轨迹与策略优化方法，提升控制性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于采样的框架，用于在可微动力学下进行有限时间轨迹和策略优化。通过将控制器设计转化为推理问题，最小化KL正则化的预期轨迹成本，得到一个最优的“玻尔兹曼倾斜”分布。为了高效从尖锐且可能多模态的目标分布中采样，引入了温度序列蒙特卡洛（TSMC）方法，结合哈密顿蒙特卡洛再生技术以保持多样性并利用轨迹回放的精确梯度。该方法还扩展应用于策略优化，通过确定性经验近似和扩展空间构造处理回放随机性。实验表明，TSMC在多个基准测试中表现优异。

item.title: 基于SAT求解与大语言模型的双饱和Ramsey-good图研究
item.content:encoded:
通过SAT求解和LLM生成代码发现无限Ramsey-good图家族。



摘要： 本文研究双饱和Ramsey-good图，这类图既不包含s-团也不包含t-独立集，且任何边的增删都会破坏其性质。作者结合SAT求解与定制化的LLM生成代码，发现了无限图族，回应了Grinstead和Roberts在1982年提出的问题。同时利用LLM生成并形式化了正确性证明，使用Lean进行验证。该案例展示了自动化推理、大语言模型和形式化验证在数学发现中的潜力，强调工具驱动工作流在实验数学中的重要性。

item.title: 基于卷积循环神经网络的晶体生长模拟研究
item.content:encoded:
使用CNN模型模拟晶体生长，提升预测精度。



摘要： 本文利用卷积循环神经网络（CRNN）构建代理模型，对晶体生长进行仿真。模型基于Allen-Cahn动力学的时间序列数据训练，考虑了通过动能各向异性导致的晶面形成。文章开发了两种网络架构：一种通过输入小片段序列隐式推断过饱和度，另一种则将过饱和度作为显式输入进行预测。通过系统测试，分析了不同训练数据量和输入序列长度对模型性能的影响。结果表明，显式参数条件下的预测效果最佳，能高保真地再现真实生长形态。而隐式方法在数据量较大时也能获得相近结果。模型表现出对过饱和度的强依赖性，并具备良好的可扩展性。

item.title: 视频时间感知与操控的模型研究
item.content:encoded:
本文研究视频时间感知与操控方法。



摘要： 本文探讨如何判断视频是否被加速或减速，并提出生成不同速度视频的方法。研究将时间视为可学习的视觉概念，通过视频中的多模态线索和时间结构进行自监督学习，以检测速度变化并估计播放速度。基于此，研究人员构建了目前最大的慢动作视频数据集，并开发出能够控制视频时间的模型，包括按速度生成视频和时间超分辨率技术。研究结果表明时间是视频学习中可操控的感知维度，为时间可控的视频生成、时间取证检测和更丰富的世界模型提供了新方向。

item.title: 解码器模型在抽象符号推理中的泛化能力研究
item.content:encoded:
研究解码器模型在未见变量上的符号推理泛化能力及改进方法。



摘要： 本文研究了解码器-only Transformer 模型在命题逻辑推理任务中的表现，特别是其对未见过变量的泛化能力。研究发现，模型在处理未见过的变量时存在困难，原因包括表示坍缩现象，即未见词的解码权重趋于相同，导致难以区分多个未见变量。同时，文章提出了一种结合架构调整、数据多样性以及冻结或重置嵌入参数的方法，以提升模型对未见词的泛化能力。实验表明该方法有效，并在 Gemma 3 系列模型中观察到类似现象，指出这些未使用词的嵌入不利于微调应用。

SAE-SPLADE在保持效果的同时提升效率。



摘要： 本文提出使用稀疏自编码器（SAE）替代传统SPLADE模型中的基础词表，以解决多义性和同义性问题，并提升多语言和多模态应用的兼容性。通过实验验证，SAE-SPLADE在域内和域外任务中均达到与SPLADE相当的检索性能，同时具备更高的效率。研究还探讨了SAE与SPLADE的兼容性、训练方法及两者之间的差异。
