item.title: Vision Transformers对抗训练的理论分析
item.content:encoded:
ViT在对抗训练下表现出强泛化能力。



摘要： 尽管Vision Transformers（ViTs）在多种视觉任务中表现优异，但它们仍像CNN一样容易受到对抗样本的影响。本文首次对简化ViT架构下的对抗训练进行了理论分析，证明在满足特定信噪比和扰动预算条件下，ViT可以实现几乎为零的鲁棒训练损失和泛化误差。这种现象在过拟合情况下依然保持良好泛化能力，即所谓的‘良性过拟合’，此前仅在CNN中观察到。实验结果验证了理论分析的正确性。

item.title: 可反驳推理与可能逻辑的研究
item.content:encoded:
研究可反驳推理及可能逻辑的17条原则和应用。



摘要： 本文探讨了可反驳推理的概念，即那些通常为真但可能偶尔为假的陈述。文章介绍了17条用于进行合理推理的逻辑原则，并分析了多个重要推理实例。其中包含14条必要原则和3条理想原则，其中一个未被正式表述。作者定义了一种称为“可能逻辑”（PL）的一阶逻辑，该逻辑满足除两条理想原则外的所有原则，并能正确处理所有示例。本文是其著作《可能推理与可能逻辑》的精简版，各章节对应书中的章节，所有证明均省略。

item.title: 自适应切片辅助的高效目标检测方法ASAHI
item.content:encoded:
ASAHI提升高分辨率图像中小目标检测效率与精度。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为ASAHI的新颖切片框架，用于解决高分辨率遥感图像中目标检测的挑战。传统切片策略依赖固定尺寸，导致冗余计算和推理时间增加。ASAHI通过自适应调整切片数量，结合动态生成重叠块、增强训练数据和改进后处理模块，显著提升了检测性能并降低了计算成本。实验表明，ASAHI在VisDrone2019和xView数据集上分别达到56.8%和22.7%的准确率，同时推理时间减少了20-25%。

item.title: 多语言大模型中的跨语言与同语言偏见分析
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示多语言模型存在跨语言和同语言偏见。



摘要： 本文通过构建LocQA测试集，评估多语言大模型在处理与地点相关的模糊问题时的偏见行为。研究发现，模型对美国相关答案存在全球性偏好，且经过指令微调的模型该偏见更显著。此外，在同一语言下，模型倾向于优先考虑人口较多的地区。这些发现有助于理解训练阶段对偏见的影响，并指导模型本地化行为的优化。

item.title: 面向多模态测试时适应的自适应概率高斯模型
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新方法提升多模态模型在分布偏移下的性能。



摘要： 本文针对多模态测试时适应（TTA）中类别条件分布建模不足的问题，提出了一种定制的概率高斯模型，并结合自适应对比不对称校正技术，以应对模态分布不均衡带来的影响。该方法在多个基准数据集上取得了最先进的性能，显著提升了模型在分布偏移情况下的预测准确性和决策边界可靠性。实验结果表明，该方法在多种分布偏移场景下均表现优异，代码已开源。

item.title: 基于运动引导的动态场景图生成方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出MoSA方法提升动态场景图生成效果。



摘要： 本文针对动态场景图生成（DSGG）中存在的细粒度关系建模、语义表示利用不足以及尾部关系建模困难等问题，提出了一种基于运动引导的语义对齐方法（MoSA）。该方法首先通过运动特征提取器（MFE）编码物体对的运动属性，如距离、速度、运动持续性和方向一致性，再通过运动引导的交互模块（MIM）将这些运动属性与空间关系特征融合，生成具有运动感知的关系表示。随后，通过跨模态动作语义匹配机制（ASM）将视觉关系特征与关系类别的文本嵌入对齐，进一步增强语义区分能力。最后，引入类别加权损失策略以强化对尾部关系的学习。实验表明，MoSA在Action Genome数据集上表现优异。

item.title: RARE框架提升真实场景下的检索评估有效性
item.content:encoded:
RARE框架解决真实场景下检索评估不准确问题。



摘要： 现有QA基准测试通常假设文档间差异明显，但实际应用中如金融报告、法律条文和专利等语料具有高度冗余和相似性，导致传统评估方法失效。为此，研究提出RARE框架，通过分解文档为原子事实并结合CRRF改进数据生成，提升评估准确性。在金融、法律和专利领域应用RARE后，发现现有检索器在真实场景中表现显著下降，揭示了现有基准的不足。RARE可帮助构建更贴近实际部署环境的RAG评估体系。

item.title: 函数向量在多语言机器翻译中的跨语言泛化能力研究
item.content:encoded:
函数向量在多语言翻译中具有跨语言泛化能力，提升翻译效果。



摘要： 本文研究了函数向量（FVs）在多语言机器翻译中的跨语言泛化能力。通过实验发现，从英语到目标语言的翻译函数向量可以有效迁移到其他语言，提升正确翻译词的排名。消融实验表明，去除函数向量会降低翻译性能，但对不相关任务影响较小。此外，基础模型的函数向量可迁移至指令调优模型，并部分实现从词级到句级翻译的泛化。

item.title: CXRMate-2在胸部X光报告生成中的性能提升与临床适用性评估
item.content:encoded:
CXRMate-2在多个数据集上表现优于现有模型，具备临床应用潜力。



摘要： 本文介绍了CXRMate-2，一种基于多模态条件和强化学习的胸部X光报告生成模型。实验结果显示，该模型在MIMIC-CXR、CheXpert Plus和ReXgradient数据集上均取得显著提升，尤其在GREEN和RadGraph-XL指标上分别提高11.2%和24.4%。通过与放射科医生报告的对比评估，发现45%的生成报告被认定为可接受，且在多数情况下与医生报告无显著差异。尽管放射科报告在召回率上占优，但生成报告在可读性方面更受青睐。研究认为，提升召回率和检测细微异常的能力，使该模型具备非劣于放射科医生报告的潜力，有望用于辅助放射科工作流程。

item.title: 基于混合生成框架的图形设计图像分层解析方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种图形设计图像分层解析方法，提升编辑灵活性。



摘要： 本文提出一种混合生成框架，用于将位图图形设计图像分解为可编辑的文本、背景和贴纸层。通过视觉语言模型解析文本区域并生成文本渲染协议，实现精确重建与灵活编辑；背景和贴纸层则利用支持RGBA的多分支扩散架构生成。同时引入ParserReward并结合Group Relative Policy Optimization优化生成质量，使其更符合人类设计偏好。实验在Parser-40K和Crello数据集上验证了该方法的优越性，整体平均性能提升23.7%。

item.title: 基于地理注册数据的3D一致视频生成模型CityRAG
item.content:encoded:
CityRAG可生成物理场景一致的长时视频，支持复杂轨迹导航。



摘要： 本文提出CityRAG，一种基于地理注册数据的视频生成模型，能够生成与真实场景物理一致的视频序列。该模型通过学习复杂运动和外观变化的先验知识，在任意天气条件和动态物体配置下保持场景一致性，并实现长时间视频生成、光照与天气稳定、循环闭合以及复杂路径导航，适用于自动驾驶和机器人模拟等应用。

item.title: 基于双层优化的半监督元加法模型研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新型半监督元加法模型，提升数据处理与预测的鲁棒性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于双层优化的半监督元加法模型（S²MAM），旨在解决传统半监督学习中依赖相似性度量和对冗余或噪声数据敏感的问题。该模型能够自动识别重要变量、更新相似性矩阵，并实现可解释的预测。理论分析证明了该方法的计算收敛性和统计泛化能力。通过在4个合成数据集和12个真实数据集上的实验验证，表明该方法具有良好的鲁棒性和可解释性。

item.title: 基于块对角Hadamard旋转的KV缓存量化方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种高效KV缓存压缩方法，兼顾精度与效率。



摘要： 本文针对LLM推理中的KV缓存内存瓶颈问题，提出了一种在实际服务约束下可行的4位KV缓存量化方法。该方法采用逐token的INT4量化结合块对角Hadamard旋转，实现了精度与效率的最佳平衡。实验表明，该方法几乎恢复了原始INT4量化损失的精度，且相比其他复杂方法，在保持服务兼容性的同时仅带来微小增益。研究还开发了一个融合旋转和量化操作的内核，可无缝集成到分页KV缓存结构中，实现零额外开销，保持与普通INT4相同的吞吐量。结果表明，有效的KV缓存压缩本质上是系统协同设计的问题。

item.title: TACO框架提升终端代理任务性能与效率
item.content:encoded:
TACO通过自进化压缩提升终端代理任务性能。



摘要： 随着模型能力的提升，研究逐渐转向长周期、多轮次的终端中心代理任务。然而，保留大量环境反馈会导致冗余和令牌成本增加。为解决这一问题，研究提出TACO框架，该框架能够自动发现并优化压缩规则，适用于多种终端相关基准测试。实验表明，TACO在多个基准上提升了性能，同时减少了令牌开销，证明了其有效性与通用性。

item.title: Voice of India：构建更真实的印地语语音识别基准
item.content:encoded:
Voice of India 提供真实语境下的多语言语音数据集，提升 ASR 系统性能。



摘要： 本文介绍了 Voice of India 基准数据集，旨在解决现有印地语语音识别（ASR）评估中的不足。该数据集基于未脚本的电话对话，涵盖15种主要印度语言，包含306,230条语音，总计536小时，覆盖139个地区。数据集特别关注印度语言中自然拼写变化及混合英语词汇的非标准化拼写问题。研究还分析了不同地理区域、音频质量、语速、性别和设备类型对 ASR 性能的影响，揭示当前系统在现实场景中的局限性，并为改进实际应用中的 ASR 系统提供见解。

item.title: 基于区域注意力的乳腺癌检测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种改进ViT的乳腺癌检测方法，提升诊断性能。



摘要： 本文针对Vision Transformers在医学影像诊断中的表现不足问题，特别是在乳腺癌检测中，分析了两个主要原因：高分辨率图像导致的token过多以及细粒度分类任务中标准交叉熵训练的局限性。为解决这些问题，作者提出了一个包含三个关键组件的框架：基于区域感兴趣（RoI）的token缩减、通过硬负样本训练增强细粒度区分能力的对比学习，以及使用DINOv2预训练ViT提取更精细的特征。实验表明，该方法在公开数据集上优于现有基线，具有临床应用潜力。

item.title: 通信网络对分布式能源控制性能的影响研究
item.content:encoded:
通信延迟显著影响虚拟电厂调度性能。



摘要： 本文研究了高渗透率分布式能源资源（DERs）配电网中，通信网络对虚拟电厂（VPP）调度算法性能的影响。通过结合线性化配网模型与ns-3仿真工具，评估了在理想和真实通信条件下VPP的调度表现。实验表明，在理想通信环境下，VPP能有效跟踪馈线功率参考并维持电压稳定；但引入真实下行通信延迟后，系统出现较大的功率波动和电压越限问题。研究强调了通信行为对分布式控制性能的重要影响，并提出需要将网络动态纳入电网互动控制评估框架。

item.title: Chat2Workflow：基于自然语言生成可执行可视化工作流的基准与框架
item.content:encoded:
研究如何利用大语言模型自动化生成可执行工作流。



摘要： 文章介绍了Chat2Workflow，一个用于从自然语言直接生成可执行可视化工作流的基准，并提出了一种稳健的代理框架以减少执行错误。该基准基于大量真实业务流程构建，生成的工作流可直接部署到实际平台。实验表明，尽管先进语言模型能捕捉高层意图，但在复杂或变化的需求下仍难以生成正确、稳定的工作流。虽然代理框架提升了5.34%的解决率，但工业级自动化的差距仍然存在，Chat2Workflow为未来研究提供了基础。

item.content:encoded:
IR-Flow通过统一框架提升图像修复效率与细节表现。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为IR-Flow的图像修复方法，结合判别与生成范式的优势。该方法通过构建多层级数据分布流和累积速度场，实现不同退化水平下的高效迁移，并引入多步一致性约束以提升恢复性能。实验表明，IR-Flow在去雨、去噪和去雨滴任务中表现优异，具有快速推理和良好失真感知平衡的特点。

item.title: FedProxy：解决联邦微调三重挑战的新框架
item.content:encoded:
FedProxy通过代理模型提升联邦微调性能，兼顾隐私与知识产权保护。



摘要： 文章提出FedProxy，一种新的联邦微调框架，旨在解决大型语言模型在联邦学习中的三重挑战：保护知识产权、确保客户隐私和减少异构数据下的性能损失。FedProxy用一个从专有模型压缩得到的统一代理小型语言模型替代传统的弱适配器，通过三个阶段的架构设计，包括高效表示、稳健优化和无训练融合，显著提升了性能，接近集中式训练效果，为安全高效的联邦微调提供了新基准。

item.title: 智能摄影编辑方法SmartPhotoCrafter的自动图像优化研究
item.content:encoded:
SmartPhotoCrafter实现自动图像编辑，无需人工指令。



摘要： 本文提出SmartPhotoCrafter，一种基于推理与生成结合的自动图像编辑方法。该模型通过Image Critic模块识别图像缺陷，并由Photographic Artist模块进行针对性优化，无需用户明确指令。采用多阶段训练流程，包括基础预训练、带推理引导的多编辑监督以及联合优化的强化学习。实验表明，SmartPhotoCrafter在自动摄影增强任务中优于现有生成模型，能够生成逼真图像并更敏感地响应修图指令。

item.title: Zero-Centered Swish：解决无批量归一化网络的稳定性问题
item.content:encoded:
提出ZC-Swish激活函数以提升无BN网络的训练稳定性。



摘要： 文章指出，在无批量归一化（BN）的深度网络中，标准激活函数如Swish和ReLU由于非零中心特性，会导致激活均值漂移，从而引发训练不稳定。为此，作者提出Zero-Centered Swish（ZC-Swish），该函数能动态将激活均值保持在零附近，有效缓解这一问题。实验表明，在深度为16的卷积网络中，ZC-Swish相比标准Swish表现更优，测试准确率达到51.5%。该方法为内存受限和隐私保护场景下的深度网络提供了高效稳定的解决方案。

item.title: 基于规范面部点预测的4D人脸重建与跟踪方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种统一方法实现高保真4D人脸重建与跟踪。



摘要： 本文针对动态人脸图像序列中非刚性变形、表情变化和视角变化带来的几何与对应关系估计难题，提出一种基于规范面部点预测的统一方法。该方法通过将每个像素映射到共享的规范空间坐标，将密集跟踪和动态重建转化为规范重建问题，从而在单一前馈模型中实现时间一致的几何结构和可靠的对应关系。结合深度和规范坐标预测，该方法在单个架构中实现了准确的深度估计、时间稳定的重建、密集的3D几何和鲁棒的面部点跟踪。实验表明，该方法在多个基准测试中表现优异，相比以往方法在对应误差和推理速度上均有显著提升，并提升了16%的深度精度。

item.title: Do Emotions Influence Moral Judgment in Large Language Models?
item.content:encoded: Large language models have been extensively studied for emotion recognition and moral reasoning as distinct capabilities, yet the extent to which emotions influence moral judgment remains underexplored. In this work, we develop an emotion-induction pipeline that infuses emotion into moral situations and evaluate shifts in moral acceptability across multiple datasets and LLMs. We observe a directional pattern: positive emotions increase moral acceptability and negative emotions decrease it, with effects strong enough to reverse binary moral judgments in up to 20% of cases, and with susceptibility scaling inversely with model capability. Our analysis further reveals that specific emotions can sometimes behave contrary to what their valence would predict (e.g., remorse paradoxically increases acceptability). A complementary human annotation study shows humans do not exhibit these systematic shifts, indicating an alignment gap in current LLMs.

item.title: Optimal Routing for Federated Learning over Dynamic Satellite Networks: Tractable or Not?
item.content:encoded: Federated learning (FL) is a key paradigm for distributed model learning across decentralized data sources. Communication in each FL round typically consists of two phases: (i) distributing the global model from a server to clients, and (ii) collecting updated local models from clients to the server for aggregation. This paper focuses on a type of FL where communication between a client and the server is relay-based over dynamic networks, making routing optimization essential. A typical scenario is in-orbit FL, where satellites act as clients and communicate with a server (which can be a satellite, ground station, or aerial platform) via multi-hop inter-satellite links. This paper presents a comprehensive tractability analysis of routing optimization for in-orbit FL under different settings. For global model distribution, these include the number of models, the objective function, and routing schemes (unicast versus multicast, and splittable versus unsplittable flow). For local model collection, the settings consider the number of models, client selection, and flow splittability. For each case, we rigorously prove whether the global optimum is obtainable in polynomial time or the problem is NP-hard. Together, our analysis draws clear boundaries between tractable and intractable regimes for a broad spectrum of routing problems for in-orbit FL. For tractable cases, the derived efficient algorithms are directly applicable in practice. For intractable cases, we provide fundamental insights into their inherent complexity. These contributions fill a critical yet unexplored research gap, laying a foundation for principled routing design, evaluation, and deployment in satellite-based FL or similar distributed learning systems.

item.title: Phase Transitions in the Fluctuations of Functionals of Random Neural Networks
item.content:encoded: We establish central and non-central limit theorems for sequences of functionals of the Gaussian output of an infinitely-wide random neural network on the d-dimensional sphere . We show that the asymptotic behaviour of these functionals as the depth of the network increases depends crucially on the fixed points of the covariance function, resulting in three distinct limiting regimes: convergence to the same functional of a limiting Gaussian field, convergence to a Gaussian distribution, convergence to a distribution in the Qth Wiener chaos. Our proofs exploit tools that are now classical (Hermite expansions, Diagram Formula, Stein-Malliavin techniques), but also ideas which have never been used in similar contexts: in particular, the asymptotic behaviour is determined by the fixed-point structure of the iterative operator associated with the covariance, whose nature and stability governs the different limiting regimes.

item.title: FASTER: Value-Guided Sampling for Fast RL
item.content:encoded: Some of the most performant reinforcement learning algorithms today can be prohibitively expensive as they use test-time scaling methods such as sampling multiple action candidates and selecting the best one. In this work, we propose FASTER, a method for getting the benefits of sampling-based test-time scaling of diffusion-based policies without the computational cost by tracing the performance gain of action samples back to earlier in the denoising process. Our key insight is that we can model the denoising of multiple action candidates and selecting the best one as a Markov Decision Process (MDP) where the goal is to progressively filter action candidates before denoising is complete. With this MDP, we can learn a policy and value function in the denoising space that predicts the downstream value of action candidates in the denoising process and filters them while maximizing returns. The result is a method that is lightweight and can be plugged into existing generative RL algorithms. Across challenging long-horizon manipulation tasks in online and batch-online RL, FASTER consistently improves the underlying policies and achieves the best overall performance among the compared methods. Applied to a pretrained VLA, FASTER achieves the same performance while substantially reducing training and inference compute requirements. Code is available at https://github.com/alexanderswerdlow/faster .

item.title: LASER: Learning Active Sensing for Continuum Field Reconstruction
item.content:encoded: High-fidelity measurements of continuum physical fields are essential for scientific discovery and engineering design but remain challenging under sparse and constrained sensing. Conventional reconstruction methods typically rely on fixed sensor layouts, which cannot adapt to evolving physical states. We propose LASER, a unified, closed-loop framework that formulates active sensing as a Partially Observable Markov Decision Process (POMDP). At its core, LASER employs a continuum field latent world model that captures the underlying physical dynamics and provides intrinsic reward feedback. This enables a reinforcement learning policy to simulate ''what-if'' sensing scenarios within a latent imagination space. By conditioning sensor movements on predicted latent states, LASER navigates toward potentially high-information regions beyond current observations. Our experiments demonstrate that LASER consistently outperforms static and offline-optimized strategies, achieving high-fidelity reconstruction under sparsity across diverse continuum fields.

item.title: AlignCultura: Towards Culturally Aligned Large Language Models?
item.content:encoded: Cultural alignment in Large Language Models (LLMs) is essential for producing contextually aware, respectful, and trustworthy outputs. Without it, models risk generating stereotyped, insensitive, or misleading responses that fail to reflect cultural diversity w.r.t Helpful, Harmless, and Honest (HHH) paradigm. Existing benchmarks represent early steps toward cultural alignment; yet, no benchmarks currently enables systematic evaluation of cultural alignment in line with UNESCO's principles of cultural diversity w.r.t HHH paradigm. Therefore, to address this gap, we built Align-Cultura, two-stage pipeline for cultural alignment. Stage I constructs CULTURAX, the HHH-English dataset grounded in the UNESCO cultural taxonomy, through Query Construction, which reclassifies prompts, expands underrepresented domains (or labels), and prevents data leakage with SimHash. Then, Response Generation pairs prompts with culturally grounded responses via two-stage rejection sampling. The final dataset contains 1,500 samples spanning 30 subdomains of tangible and intangible cultural forms. Stage II benchmarks CULTURAX on general-purpose models, culturally fine-tuned models, and open-weight LLMs (Qwen3-8B and DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-7B). Empirically, culturally fine-tuned models improve joint HHH by 4%-6%, reduce cultural failures by 18%, achieve 10%-12% efficiency gains, and limit leakage to 0.3%.

item.title: EgoMotion: Hierarchical Reasoning and Diffusion for Egocentric Vision-Language Motion Generation
item.content:encoded: Faithfully modeling human behavior in dynamic environments is a foundational challenge for embodied intelligence. While conditional motion synthesis has achieved significant advances, egocentric motion generation remains largely underexplored due to the inherent complexity of first-person perception. In this work, we investigate Egocentric Vision-Language (Ego-VL) motion generation. This task requires synthesizing 3D human motion conditioned jointly on first-person visual observations and natural language instructions. We identify a critical \textit{reasoning-generation entanglement} challenge: the simultaneous optimization of semantic reasoning and kinematic modeling introduces gradient conflicts. These conflicts systematically degrade the fidelity of multimodal grounding and motion quality. To address this challenge, we propose a hierarchical generative framework \textbf{EgoMotion}. Inspired by the biological decoupling of cognitive reasoning and motor control, EgoMotion operates in two stages. In the Cognitive Reasoning stage, A vision-language model (VLM) projects multimodal inputs into a structured space of discrete motion primitives. This forces the VLM to acquire goal-consistent representations, effectively bridging the semantic gap between high-level perceptual understanding and low-level action execution. In the Motion Generation stage, these learned representations serve as expressive conditioning signals for a diffusion-based motion generator. By performing iterative denoising within a continuous latent space, the generator synthesizes physically plausible and temporally coherent trajectories. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that EgoMotion achieves state-of-the-art performance, and produces motion sequences that are both semantically grounded and kinematically superior to existing approaches.

item.title: Lyapunov-Certified Direct Switching Theory for Q-Learning
item.content:encoded: Q-learning is one of the most fundamental algorithms in reinforcement learning. We analyze constant-stepsize Q-learning through a direct stochastic switching system representation. The key observation is that the Bellman maximization error can be represented exactly by a stochastic policy. Therefore, the Q-learning error admits a switched linear conditional-mean recursion with martingale-difference noise. The intrinsic drift rate is the joint spectral radius (JSR) of the direct switching family, which can be strictly smaller than the standard row-sum rate. Using this representation, we derive a finite-time final-iterate bound via a JSR-induced Lyapunov function and then give a computable quadratic-certificate version.

item.title: Tstars-Tryon 1.0: Robust and Realistic Virtual Try-On for Diverse Fashion Items
item.content:encoded: Recent advances in image generation and editing have opened new opportunities for virtual try-on. However, existing methods still struggle to meet complex real-world demands. We present Tstars-Tryon 1.0, a commercial-scale virtual try-on system that is robust, realistic, versatile, and highly efficient. First, our system maintains a high success rate across challenging cases like extreme poses, severe illumination variations, motion blur, and other in-the-wild conditions. Second, it delivers highly photorealistic results with fine-grained details, faithfully preserving garment texture, material properties, and structural characteristics, while largely avoiding common AI-generated artifacts. Third, beyond apparel try-on, our model supports flexible multi-image composition (up to 6 reference images) across 8 fashion categories, with coordinated control over person identity and background. Fourth, to overcome the latency bottlenecks of commercial deployment, our system is heavily optimized for inference speed, delivering near real-time generation for a seamless user experience. These capabilities are enabled by an integrated system design spanning end-to-end model architecture, a scalable data engine, robust infrastructure, and a multi-stage training paradigm. Extensive evaluation and large-scale product deployment demonstrate that Tstars-Tryon1.0 achieves leading overall performance. To support future research, we also release a comprehensive benchmark. The model has been deployed at an industrial scale on the Taobao App, serving millions of users with tens of millions of requests.

item.title: InHabit: Leveraging Image Foundation Models for Scalable 3D Human Placement
item.content:encoded: Training embodied agents to understand 3D scenes as humans do requires large-scale data of people meaningfully interacting with diverse environments, yet such data is scarce. Real-world motion capture is costly and limited to controlled settings, while existing synthetic datasets rely on simple geometric heuristics that ignore rich scene context. In contrast, 2D foundation models trained on internet-scale data have implicitly acquired commonsense knowledge of human-environment interactions. To transfer this knowledge into 3D, we introduce InHabit, a fully automatic and scalable data generator for populating 3D scenes with interacting humans. InHabit follows a render-generate-lift principle: given a rendered 3D scene, a vision-language model proposes contextually meaningful actions, an image-editing model inserts a human, and an optimization procedure lifts the edited result into physically plausible SMPL-X bodies aligned with the scene geometry. Applied to Habitat-Matterport3D, InHabit produces the first large-scale photorealistic 3D human-scene interaction dataset, containing 78K samples across 800 building-scale scenes with complete 3D geometry, SMPL-X bodies, and RGB images. Augmenting standard training data with our samples improves RGB-based 3D human-scene reconstruction and contact estimation, and in a perceptual user study our data is preferred in 78% of cases over the state of the art.

item.title: Allo{SR}$^2$: Rectifying One-Step Super-Resolution to Stay Real via Allomorphic Generative Flows
item.content:encoded: Real-world image super-resolution (Real-SR) has been revolutionized by leveraging the powerful generative priors of large-scale diffusion and flow-based models. However, fine-tuning these models on limited LR-HR pairs often precipitates "prior collapse" that the model sacrifices its inherent generative richness to overfit specific training degradations. This issue is further exacerbated in one-step generation, where the absence of multi-step refinement leads to significant trajectory drift and artifact generation. In this paper, we propose Allo{SR}$^2$, a novel framework that rectifies one-step SR trajectories via allomorphic generative flows to maintain high-fidelity generative realism. Specifically, we utilize Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) Guided Trajectory Initialization to establish a physically grounded starting state by aligning the degradation level of LR latent features with the optimal anchoring timestep of the pre-trained flow. To ensure a stable, curvature-free path for one-step inference, we propose Flow-Anchored Trajectory Consistency (FATC), which enforces velocity-level supervision across intermediate states. Furthermore, we develop Allomorphic Trajectory Matching (ATM), a self-adversarial alignment strategy that minimizes the distributional discrepancy between the SR flow and the generative flow in a unified vector field. Extensive experiments on both synthetic and real-world benchmarks demonstrate that Allo{SR}$^2$ achieves state-of-the-art performance in one-step Real-SR, offering a superior balance between restoration fidelity and generative realism while maintaining extreme efficiency.

item.title: Emotion-Cause Pair Extraction in Conversations via Semantic Decoupling and Graph Alignment
item.content:encoded: Emotion-Cause Pair Extraction in Conversations (ECPEC) aims to identify the set of causal relations between emotion utterances and their triggering causes within a dialogue. Most existing approaches formulate ECPEC as an independent pairwise classification task, overlooking the distinct semantics of emotion diffusion and cause explanation, and failing to capture globally consistent many-to-many conversational causality. To address these limitations, we revisit ECPEC from a semantic perspective and seek to disentangle emotion-oriented semantics from cause-oriented semantics, mapping them into two complementary representation spaces to better capture their distinct conversational roles. Building on this semantic decoupling, we naturally formulate ECPEC as a global alignment problem between the emotion-side and cause-side representations, and employ optimal transport to enable many-to-many and globally consistent emotion-cause matching. Based on this perspective, we propose a unified framework SCALE that instantiates the above semantic decoupling and alignment principle within a shared conversational structure. Extensive experiments on several benchmark datasets demonstrate that SCALE consistently achieves state-of-the-art performance. Our codes are released at https://github.com/CoCoSphere/SCALE.

item.title: Discovering a Shared Logical Subspace: Steering LLM Logical Reasoning via Alignment of Natural-Language and Symbolic Views
item.content:encoded: Large Language Models (LLMs) still struggle with multi-step logical reasoning. Existing approaches either purely refine the reasoning chain in natural language form or attach a symbolic solver as an external module. In this work, we instead ask whether LLMs contain a shared internal logical subspace that simultaneously aligns natural-language and symbolic-language views of the reasoning process. Our hypothesis is that this logical subspace captures logical reasoning capabilities in LLMs that are shared across views while remaining independent of surface forms. To verify this, we employ Canonical Correlation Analysis on the paired residual activations from natural-language and symbolic-language reasoning chains, learning a low-dimensional subspace with maximum cross-view correlation. Furthermore, we design a training-free approach that steers LLMs reasoning chain along this logical subspace, thereby leveraging the complementary reasoning signals from both views. Experiments on four logical reasoning benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach, improving accuracy by up to 11 percentage points and generalizing well on out-of-domain problems.

item.title: On Accelerating Grounded Code Development for Research
item.content:encoded: A major challenge for niche scientific and technical domains in leveraging coding agents is the lack of access to up-to-date, domain- specific knowledge. Foundational models often demonstrate limited reasoning capabilities in specialized fields and cannot inherently incorporate knowledge that evolves through ongoing research and experimentation. Materials scientists exploring novel compounds, communication engineers designing and evaluating new protocols, and bioengineering researchers conducting iterative experiments all face this limitation. These experts typically lack the resources to fine-tune large models or continuously embed new findings, creating a barrier to adopting AI-driven coding agents. To address this, we introduce a framework that gives coding agents instanta- neous access to research repositories and technical documentation, enabling real-time, context-aware operation. Our open-source im- plementation allows users to upload documents via doc-search.dev and includes zed-fork, which enforces domain-specific rules and workflows. Together, these tools accelerate the integration of coding agents into specialized scientific and technical workflows

item.title: AI-Enabled Image-Based Hybrid Vision/Force Control of Tendon-Driven Aerial Continuum Manipulators
item.content:encoded: This paper presents an AI-enabled cascaded hybrid vision/force control framework for tendon-driven aerial continuum manipulators based on constant-strain modeling in $SE(3)$ as a coupled system. The proposed controller is designed to enable autonomous, physical interaction with a static environment while stabilizing the image feature error. The developed strategy combines the cascaded fast fixed-time sliding mode control and a radial basis function neural network to cope with the uncertainties in the image acquired by the eye-in-hand monocular camera and the measurements from the force sensing apparatus. This ensures rapid, online learning of the vision- and force-related uncertainties without requiring offline training. Furthermore, the features are extracted via a state-of-the-art graph neural network architecture employed by a visual servoing framework using line features, rather than relying on heuristic geometric line extractors, to concurrently contribute to tracking the desired normal interaction force during contact and regulating the image feature error. A comparative study benchmarks the proposed controller against established rigid-arm aerial manipulation methods, evaluating robustness across diverse scenarios and feature extraction strategies. The simulation and experimental results showcase the effectiveness of the proposed methodology under various initial conditions and demonstrate robust performance in executing manipulation tasks.

item.title: Mind the Unseen Mass: Unmasking LLM Hallucinations via Soft-Hybrid Alphabet Estimation
item.content:encoded: This paper studies uncertainty quantification for large language models (LLMs) under black-box access, where only a small number of responses can be sampled for each query. In this setting, estimating the effective semantic alphabet size--that is, the number of distinct meanings expressed in the sampled responses--provides a useful proxy for downstream risk. However, frequency-based estimators tend to undercount rare semantic modes when the sample size is small, while graph-spectral quantities alone are not designed to estimate semantic occupancy accurately. To address this issue, we propose SHADE (Soft-Hybrid Alphabet Dynamic Estimator), a simple and interpretable estimator that combines Generalized Good-Turing coverage with a heat-kernel trace of the normalized Laplacian constructed from an entailment-weighted graph over sampled responses. The estimated coverage adaptively determines the fusion rule: under high coverage, SHADE uses a convex combination of the two signals, while under low coverage it applies a LogSumExp fusion to emphasize missing or weakly observed semantic modes. A finite-sample correction is then introduced to stabilize the resulting cardinality estimate before converting it into a coverage-adjusted semantic entropy score. Experiments on pooled semantic alphabet-size estimation against large-sample references and on QA incorrectness detection show that SHADE achieves the strongest improvements in the most sample-limited regime, while the performance gap narrows as the number of samples increases. These results suggest that hybrid semantic occupancy estimation is particularly beneficial when black-box uncertainty quantification must operate under tight sampling budgets.

item.title: HoWToBench: Holistic Evaluation for LLM's Capability in Human-level Writing using Tree of Writing
item.content:encoded: Evaluating the writing capabilities of large language models (LLMs) remains a significant challenge due to the multidimensional nature of writing skills and the limitations of existing metrics. LLM's performance in thousand-words level and open-ended writing is inadequately assessed by traditional reference-based metrics or modern LLM-as-a-judge methods. We propose Tree-of-Writing (ToW), to resolve the implicit inconsistency often found when LLM-as-a-judge aggregates all sub-features in text evaluation. ToW incorporates a tree-structured workflow by explicitly modeling the aggregation weights of sub-features. We also present HowToBench, a large-scale Chinese writing benchmark encompassing 12 genres and 1302 instructions across three task categories: contextual completion, outline-guided writing, and open-ended generation. ToW successfully mitigates the biases, achieving a 0.93 Pearson correlation with human judgments. Furthermore, we detect that both overlap-based text generation metrics and popular LLM-as-a-judge practices are vulnerable to textual disturbances, while ToW is robust to them. We also uncover a negative correlation between input length and content-related scores in the Guide task, showcasing that it cannot be simply improved by input-side information piling.

item.title: Streamliners for Answer Set Programming
item.content:encoded: Streamliner constraints reduce the search space of combinatorial problems by ruling out portions of the solution space. We adapt the StreamLLM approach, which uses Large Language Models (LLMs) to generate streamliners for Constraint Programming, to Answer Set Programming (ASP). Given an ASP encoding and a few small training instances, we prompt multiple LLMs to propose candidate constraints. Candidates that cause syntax errors, render satisfiable instances unsatisfiable, or degrade performance on all training instances are discarded. The surviving streamliners are evaluated together with the original encoding, and we report results for a virtual best encoding (VBE) that, for each instance, selects the fastest among the original encoding and its streamlined variants. On three ASP Competition benchmarks (Partner Units Problem, Sokoban, Towers of Hanoi), the VBE achieves speedups of up to 4--5x over the original encoding. Different LLMs produce semantically diverse constraints, not mere syntactic variations, indicating that the approach captures genuine problem structure.

item.title: AblateCell: A Reproduce-then-Ablate Agent for Virtual Cell Repositories
item.content:encoded: Systematic ablations are essential to attribute performance gains in AI Virtual Cells, yet they are rarely performed because biological repositories are under-standardized and tightly coupled to domain-specific data and formats. While recent coding agents can translate ideas into implementations, they typically stop at producing code and lack a verifier that can reproduce strong baselines and rigorously test which components truly matter. We introduce AblateCell, a reproduce-then-ablate agent for virtual cell repositories that closes this verification gap. AblateCell first reproduces reported baselines end-to-end by auto-configuring environments, resolving dependency and data issues, and rerunning official evaluations while emitting verifiable artifacts. It then conducts closed-loop ablation by generating a graph of isolated repository mutations and adaptively selecting experiments under a reward that trades off performance impact and execution cost. Evaluated on three single-cell perturbation prediction repositories (CPA, GEARS, BioLORD), AblateCell achieves 88.9% (+29.9% to human expert) end-to-end workflow success and 93.3% (+53.3% to heuristic) accuracy in recovering ground-truth critical components. These results enable scalable, repository-grounded verification and attribution directly on biological codebases.

item.title: Framelet-Based Blind Image Restoration with Minimax Concave Regularization
item.content:encoded: Recovering corrupted images is one of the most challenging problems in image processing. Among various restoration tasks, blind image deblurring has been extensively studied due to its practical importance and inherent difficulty. In this problem, both the point spread function (PSF) and the underlying latent sharp image must be estimated simultaneously. This problem cannot be solved directly due to its ill-posed nature. One powerful tool for solving such problems is total variation (TV) regularization. The $\ell_0$-norm regularization within the TV framework has been widely adopted to promote sparsity in image gradients or transform domains, leading to improved preservation of edges and fine structures. However, the use of the $\ell_0$-norm results in a highly nonconvex and computationally intractable optimization problem, which limits its practical applicability. To overcome these difficulties, we employ the minimax concave penalty (MCP), which promotes enhanced sparsity and provides a closer approximation to the $\ell_0$-norm. In addition, a reweighted $\ell_1$-norm regularization is incorporated to further reduce estimation bias and improve the preservation of fine image details and textures. After introducing the proposed model, a numerical algorithm is developed to solve the resulting optimization problem. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is then demonstrated through experimental evaluations on several test images.

item.title: Hadamard流形上的Horospherical深度与Busemann中位数
item.content:encoded:
提出Hadamard流形上的统计深度概念及Busemann中位数，具有几何不变性。



摘要： 本文引入了Hadamard流形上的horospherical深度概念，并定义了Busemann中位数作为其最大值点集合。该方法利用了Tukey半空间深度中的线性泛函作为距离函数的极限，从而在Hadamard流形上构造出Busemann函数，其子集为horoballs，替代了半空间。所提出的深度参数化于视觉边界，具有等距不变性，无需切空间线性化或选定基点。文章证明了深度区域的嵌套性和测地凸性，并证明了存在至少1/(d+1)深度的中心点，从而保证了Busemann中位数的存在性。在严格负截面曲率和适度正则性假设下，深度是严格拟凹的，中位数唯一。此外，深度对总变差扰动具有鲁棒性，且在污染逃逸至无穷时，中位数依赖于逃逸方向而非污染质量移动的距离。最后，文章还建立了样本深度的统一一致性及样本深度区域和样本Busemann中位数的收敛性。

item.title: BIRCH：解决视觉语言模型评估中的信息偏差问题
item.content:encoded:
BIRCH提升VLM评估可靠性，减少信息偏差。



摘要： 本文研究了VLM-as-a-Judge在自动评估视觉语言模型时的可靠性问题。分析发现，现有系统在决策时对图像关注不足，倾向于偏好信息量大的答案，即使该答案与图像内容冲突，这种现象称为信息偏差。为解决此问题，作者提出BIRCH框架，通过先修正答案与图像内容的不一致，再进行比较，从而将评估重点从信息量转向图像相关的正确性。实验表明，BIRCH可减少信息偏差达17%，提升性能9.8%。研究揭示了当前VLM评估系统的根本缺陷，并强调需要更严谨的设计方法。

item.title: TurboQuant与EDEN方案的关系分析
item.content:encoded:
TurboQuant是EDEN的子集，但存在优化不足。



摘要： 本文探讨了TurboQuant与先前DRIVE和EDEN方案之间的关系。EDEN是将1比特量化扩展到任意比特数的方案，而TurboQuant_mse是EDEN的一个特例，使用固定缩放参数S=1，这在大多数情况下并不最优。TurboQuant_prod则结合了EDEN的有偏量化和QJL的无偏残差量化，但存在多个性能劣势。实验表明，优化后的EDEN在准确率上优于TurboQuant，尤其是在高维情况下表现更佳。

item.title: 面向文档搜索的查询自动补全方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出DocQAC框架提升长文档搜索效率。



摘要： 本文提出一种面向文档搜索的查询自动补全方法（DocQAC），旨在通过用户查询前缀和文档上下文提升搜索效率与准确性。该方法引入自适应惩罚机制和检索增强生成技术，有效结合文档内容与历史交互信息。实验表明，该框架在T5和BART等模型上优于现有基线，并超越大型指令调优模型，在真实场景中具有良好的实用性与可扩展性。相关数据集与代码已公开。

item.title: 基于扩散模型的遥感图像目标定位方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种结合扩散模型与分割模型的目标定位方法，提升遥感图像定位性能。



摘要： 本文研究了扩散模型在遥感图像目标定位中的应用，提出了一种融合扩散模型生成定位线索与先进分割模型（如RemoteSAM和SAM3）的混合流程，以提高边界框的准确性。该方法充分利用了生成模型和基础分割模型的优势，实现了复杂场景下的鲁棒且自适应的目标定位。实验表明，该方法在定位性能上显著优于现有方法，Acc@0.5指标提升了超过14%。

item.title: 基于伪标签引导的表格异常检测方法PLAG
item.content:encoded:
PLAG通过伪标签提升表格异常检测效果。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为PLAG的伪标签引导异常生成方法，用于提升表格数据中的异常检测性能。该方法利用伪异常作为引导信号，将样本的整体异常量化分解为特征级别的异常累积，从而增强模型对局部异常模式的理解。同时，引入两阶段数据选择策略，结合格式验证和不确定性估计，确保合成异常的准确性和多样性。实验表明，PLAG在多个基准数据集上表现优异，并能有效提升现有无监督检测器的F1分数。

item.title: 低资源东高加索语言的语音识别音素级分析
item.content:encoded:
研究低资源语言的语音识别性能与音素级错误关系。



摘要： 本文对两种低资源且语音复杂的东高加索语言（Archi和Rutul）进行语音识别（ASR）的音素级分析，基于约50分钟和1小时20分钟的标准化语音-文本数据。研究整合并处理现有数据以用于ASR训练和评估，测试了wav2vec2、Whisper和Qwen2-Audio等模型。其中，wav2vec2通过引入语言特定的音素词表获得显著提升，表现优于或接近Whisper。研究还发现音素识别准确率与训练频率呈S型曲线关系，部分情况下Whisper的表现超出预期，表明模型泛化能力的影响。结果表明，许多错误源于数据稀缺而非语音复杂性，强调了音素级评估在理解低资源语言ASR行为中的重要性。

item.title: 基于Hebbian学习的音效分类持续学习方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种基于核可塑性的持续学习方法，提升音效分类准确率。



摘要： 本文受人类终身学习能力启发，采用生物启发的Hebbian学习方法进行音效分类。提出了一种核可塑性方法，在增量学习过程中选择性地调节网络核，以学习新信息并保留旧知识。实验使用ESC-50数据集，结果显示该方法在五个增量步骤中达到76.3%的总体准确率，优于无核可塑性的基线方法（68.7%），并在任务间表现出更高的稳定性。

item.title: 物理信息神经网络训练过程详解
item.content:encoded:
本文详细解析PINN训练流程，包含前向传播、损失计算与梯度下降。



摘要： 本文提供了一个完整的物理信息神经网络（PINN）训练指南，通过一个具有已知解析解的一阶初值问题作为示例，详细介绍了从网络输出和时间导数的前向传播，到基于常微分方程残差和初始条件构建的复合损失函数的计算，再到梯度反向传播及参数更新的全过程。文章使用1-3-3-1多层感知机进行具体数值计算，并推导出适用于任意深度网络的敏感性传播公式。最终训练后的网络在不依赖真实解数据的情况下，实现了相对L²误差为4.290×10⁻⁴的高精度拟合。附带的Jupyter/PyTorch笔记本可复现所有手动计算和完整训练流程，实现手算与机器计算的相互验证。

item.title: FSEVAL：一种用于特征选择算法评估的工具箱
item.content:encoded:
提出FSEVAL工具箱以简化特征选择算法的评估。



摘要： 本文介绍了FSEVAL，这是一个用于特征选择算法评估的工具箱，配有可视化仪表盘。其目的是使研究人员能够轻松地全面评估不同特征选择算法的性能。FSEVAL旨在提供一个标准化、统一的评估和可视化工具，帮助研究人员在不同监督和非监督设置下进行广泛而深入的分析。该工具支持多种评估指标，有助于提高特征选择方法的可解释性和有效性。

item.title: MathNet：大规模多模态数学问题数据集与推理评估基准
item.content:encoded:
MathNet是一个多语言、多模态的数学竞赛问题数据集，支持多种推理任务。



摘要： MathNet是一个高质量、大规模、多模态和多语言的数学竞赛问题数据集，涵盖47个国家、17种语言和二十年的竞赛内容，包含30,676道专家编写的问题及解答。该数据集支持三种任务：问题求解、数学感知检索和检索增强型问题求解。实验结果显示，即使最先进的模型在该基准上仍面临挑战，而基于嵌入的模型在检索等价问题方面表现不佳。通过检索增强生成，性能可提升最高12%。MathNet提供了首个数学问题检索评估基准，并公开了数据集和基准。

item.title: Sessa：一种具有长程记忆能力的解码器模型
item.content:encoded:
Sessa通过反馈路径引入注意力，实现长程记忆和灵活检索。



摘要： 本文提出Sessa，一种将注意力机制嵌入反馈路径的解码器模型，能够实现多路径递归聚合。在特定假设下，Sessa表现出幂律衰减的记忆特性，比传统方法更优。实验表明，在长上下文任务中表现最佳，同时在短上下文任务中仍保持竞争力。与Transformer和Mamba相比，Sessa在选择性检索方面更具灵活性。

item.title: 多模态大模型在算术任务中的表现与机制分析
item.content:encoded:
多模态大模型在跨模态算术任务中准确率下降显著，主要受计算负载影响。



摘要： 本文探讨了多模态大语言模型在处理不同形式的算术问题时的表现，发现尽管模型能准确感知数值内容，但在执行多位数乘法时存在明显缺陷。由于现有基准缺乏系统性跨模态配对数据，研究者引入了一个可控的多模态乘法基准，通过变化数字长度、稀疏性、表示方式和模态来评估模型性能。研究还定义了算术负载C作为操作次数的代理指标，并发现随着C值增加，模型准确性急剧下降。进一步分析表明，模型的性能下降主要源于计算而非感知环节，同时揭示了模型倾向于使用分解等启发式策略进行推理。

item.title: 多任务视觉语言模型中神经元解释与调控方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出HONEs框架提升多任务VLM中关键神经元识别与调控效果。



摘要： 本文研究了视觉语言模型（VLMs）中神经元层面的解释问题，指出现有方法在单任务分析中存在局限性，难以跨任务比较神经元重要性。同时，现有排名策略忽略了任务依赖的信息路径对前馈网络神经元的影响，导致多任务环境下神经元的多义性问题。为此，作者提出HONEs框架，通过任务相关注意力头对前馈网络神经元进行因果贡献排序，并通过轻量级缩放进行调控。实验表明，HONEs在四个多模态任务和两个主流VLM中均优于现有方法，提升了模型性能。

item.title: Aether：基于生成式智能体与网络数字孪生的自动化网络变更验证系统
item.content:encoded:
Aether通过AI与数字孪生技术实现网络变更的自动化验证。



摘要： 本文介绍了Aether，一种结合生成式智能体与多功能网络数字孪生的新型网络变更验证方法。Aether采用五种专门的网络操作AI智能体协同处理变更验证流程，从意图分析到网络验证和测试。其核心是统一的网络数字孪生平台，整合建模、仿真与模拟，提供一致且实时的网络视图。通过智能体协作，Aether实现了快速、自动化的网络变更验证，显著减少人工工作量、错误率，并提升运营敏捷性和成本效益。评估显示，Aether在错误检测、诊断覆盖率和速度方面优于传统方法。

item.title: 基于合成指令微调的个性化司法模型研究
item.content:encoded:
一种高效个性化司法模型方法在低资源环境中的应用与验证。



摘要： 尽管大型语言模型取得了显著进展，但为个体决策者进行个性化仍是一个开放性问题。本文提出了一种合成-有机监督管道，将原始司法判决转化为指令微调数据，从而在低资源环境下实现对个别法官的参数高效微调。通过三种不同任务和场景的对比实验，结果表明因果语言建模结合合成指令微调在词法、风格和语义相似性方面均优于其他基线方法。值得注意的是，模型生成的输出与人类法官的推理几乎无法区分，证明了该方法在低资源环境下的有效性。

item.title: 基于分层的面部模板逆向方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种分层面部模板逆向方法，提升身份一致性与隐私安全。



摘要： 本文针对面部模板逆向技术带来的隐私泄露问题，提出一种分层面部模板逆向（LBFTI）方法。该方法将人脸图像分解为前景层、中景层和背景层，并通过专用生成器分别生成各层。采用三阶段训练策略，依次生成并融合各层，最终优化各层间协调性和身份一致性。实验表明，LBFTI在机器认证性能上提升了25.3%的TAR，同时在人类感知相似性方面也表现优异，验证了其有效性。

item.title: 大型语言模型在群体观点估计中的优势与局限
item.content:encoded:
大型语言模型在群体观点预测中表现优于人类，具有统计优势。



摘要： 本文探讨了大型语言模型（LLMs）作为群体观点估计工具的潜力。研究指出，在某些条件下，LLMs在预测主观任务中的群体意见时，可以优于人类标注者，甚至优于同组人类。这种优势源于LLMs在估计过程中的低方差和减少的表示与处理偏差耦合，而非基于真实生活经验。研究还明确了LLMs在统计上更优的应用场景，以及人类判断仍不可或缺的边界。该成果重新定义了LLMs在集体观点估计中的角色，从成本节约的替代方案转变为有理论依据的工具。

item.title: 基于流程挖掘的异常入侵检测系统增强方法
item.content:encoded:
通过流程挖掘提升IDS的告警严重性评估与解释能力。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于流程挖掘的异常入侵检测系统（IDS）增强方法，旨在解决现有深度学习IDS因黑盒结构导致的可信度不足问题。该方法通过分析数据包序列提供基于过程的告警严重性评分和解释，有效区分低至高严重性的告警，同时保持高达99.94%的召回率和99.99%的精确度，减少误报并保留良性流量。实验基于USB-IDS-TC数据集进行，验证了方法在不同Slowloris DoS攻击变体下的有效性。

item.title: 基于广义合成控制法的电力需求响应基线估计方法
item.content:encoded:
提出广义合成控制法提升电力市场需求响应预测性能。



摘要： 本文针对电力市场中需求响应结算的基线估计问题，提出一种基于经典合成控制法（SCM）的广义合成控制方法。传统SCM作为静态估计器，无法捕捉残差中的时间结构。新方法通过引入外生特征、滞后目标负荷和选定滞后供体信号，将基线估计转化为动态反事实预测问题，从而更好地捕捉自回归依赖、延迟供体-响应模式及误差修正效应。该框架在数据有限的情况下表现尤为突出，实验表明其在Ausgrid智能电表数据集上优于传统SCM和其他基准方法。

item.title: XEmbodied：提升视觉-语言-动作模型的3D几何感知能力
item.content:encoded:
XEmbodied增强VLA模型的3D几何感知与物理交互能力。



摘要： 本文提出XEmbodied，一种云端基础模型，旨在提升视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型在复杂环境中的性能。XEmbodied通过结构化3D适配器整合几何表示，并利用高效图像具身适配器将物理信号融入上下文标记。该模型在18个公开基准测试中表现出色，显著提升了空间推理、交通语义、具身可操作性及分布外泛化能力，适用于大规模场景挖掘和具身视觉问答任务。

item.title: 基于图的平滑函数在线学习算法研究
item.content:encoded:
研究图上平滑函数的老虎机问题，提升推荐系统效率。



摘要： 本文探讨了一种在图结构上进行在线学习的老虎机问题，其中每个节点的收益具有平滑性。该框架适用于内容推荐等涉及图结构的在线学习任务，通过利用节点间的相似性，目标是推荐具有高预期评分的项目。作者引入了有效维度的概念，并提出了两种算法，其累积遗憾与有效维度呈线性或次线性关系。实验表明，仅需对少量节点进行评估即可学习到高质量的用户偏好模型。

item.title: 不同攻击方式对语言模型安全性的差异影响研究
item.content:encoded:
研究不同攻击方式对语言模型安全性的影响差异。



摘要： 本文研究了通过三种不同方式（有害监督微调、基于可验证奖励的强化学习、拒绝抑制消融）使语言模型“越狱”后，其行为和机制特性。尽管所有方法都实现了接近极限的有害合规性，但它们在超出直接有害性后表现出显著差异。RLVR越狱模型表现出最小的性能下降，并保留了明确的有害识别能力；SFT越狱模型则出现最大的安全判断崩溃和行为漂移；而消融方法则依赖于模型家族。研究还揭示了不同越狱方式在机制和修复上的差异。

item.content:encoded:
mlr3torch简化了神经网络的定义与训练，适用于多种数据类型。



摘要： 文章介绍了mlr3torch，一个基于torch包的深度学习框架，集成于mlr3生态系统中。该工具支持表格数据和通用张量（如图像）的分类与回归任务，提供预定义架构，并允许用户通过图语言定义完整的建模流程，包括预处理、数据增强和网络结构。文章还展示了其在超参数调优、微调和多模态数据架构定义中的应用，并提供了运行时性能基准。

item.content:encoded:
RASP-Tuner通过上下文感知策略提升优化效率。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为RASP-Tuner的在线调优方法，旨在解决在动态环境中配置调整成本高的问题。该方法通过检索相似历史上下文、使用混合专家模型预测损失，并在低维提示子空间中进行适应，从而减少计算开销。同时，RealErrorComposer将多源指标统一为可微训练目标。实验表明，RASP-Tuner在多个合成和真实任务中优于传统方法，且运行时间显著降低。

item.title: 基于角色感知的大型语言模型心理健康安全评估框架
item.content:encoded:
提出R-MHSafe和MHSafeEval，提升LLM心理健康安全评估效果。



摘要： 本文针对大型语言模型在心理健康咨询中的安全性评估难题，提出R-MHSafe角色感知安全分类体系和MHSafeEval闭环评估框架。该框架通过角色分析和对抗性多轮交互，识别AI心理咨询师在不同角色中可能引发的临床危害，如施害者、煽动者、促进者或促成者等。实验表明，现有静态评估方法难以发现累积性安全问题，而新框架显著提升了对安全故障模式的覆盖能力和诊断精度。

item.title: 基于扩散模型的开放词汇时间动作检测方法DFAlign
item.content:encoded:
DFAlign通过扩散去噪提升动作与视频对齐效果，实现更精准的开放词汇动作检测。



摘要： 本文提出DFAlign框架，用于解决开放词汇时间动作检测（OV-TAD）中动作语义与视频表示之间语义不平衡的问题。该框架通过扩散去噪生成前景知识，作为视频与文本之间的中间语义锚点，有效缓解语义差距并增强动作相关片段的可区分性。具体包括Semantic-Unify Conditioning（SUC）模块统一动作共享与特定语义，Background-Suppress Denoising（BSD）模块逐步去除背景冗余以生成前景知识，并引入Foreground-Prompt Alignment（FPA）模块将前景知识注入文本表示，引导模型关注动作相关区域。实验表明，该方法在两个OV-TAD基准上均取得最佳性能。

item.title: FlashFPS：提升点云处理效率的新型加速框架
item.content:encoded:
FlashFPS优化FPS操作，显著提升PNN推理速度。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为FlashFPS的新框架，用于加速点云处理中的Farthest Point Sampling (FPS)操作。通过分析发现FPS存在三大冗余问题，并引入FPS-Prune和FPS-Cache两个模块进行优化，有效减少计算量并提升效率。实验表明，FlashFPS在GPU和PNN加速器上分别实现了5.16倍和2.69倍的速度提升，同时保持了较高的精度。代码已开源，便于进一步研究与应用。

item.title: 多智能体系统中创意生成的多样性研究
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示多智能体系统在创意生成中的多样性挑战与结构耦合问题。



摘要： 本文通过实证研究探讨了多智能体系统（MAS）在开放性创意生成中的多样性问题。研究从模型智能、代理认知和系统动态三个层面分析了多样性变化的原因。发现更强的模型虽提升质量，但降低多样性；权威主导的系统抑制语义多样性；群体规模扩大和密集通信导致过早收敛。这些现象被归因于结构耦合，即交互无意中缩小了探索空间，引发多样性崩溃。研究强调在设计MAS时需保持独立性和分歧以促进创造力。

item.title: 主动序列预测下的均值估计分析
item.content:encoded:
研究主动序列预测中的均值估计，分析查询概率对置信区间的影响。



摘要： 本文重新审视了主动序列预测驱动的均值估计问题，其中在每一轮中需根据样本协变量决定真实标签的查询概率。若未查询标签，则使用机器学习模型的预测结果。先前的研究通过结合不确定性建议与固定概率来确定查询概率。本文通过实验发现，当固定概率权重接近1时，置信区间宽度最小。基于此观察，作者提出了非渐近分析，并建立了数据相关的置信区间上界。分析还表明，当使用无遗憾学习方法确定查询概率时，查询概率会收敛到一个与当前协变量无关的最大约束值。模拟实验验证了理论结果。

item.title: FregeLogic at SemEval 2026 Task 11: A Hybrid Neuro-Symbolic Architecture for Content-Robust Syllogistic Validity Prediction
item.content:encoded: We present FregeLogic, a hybrid neuro-symbolic system for SemEval-2026 Task 11 (Subtask 1), which addresses syllogistic validity prediction while reducing content effects on predictions. Our approach combines an ensemble of five LLM classifiers, spanning three open-weights models (Llama 4 Maverick, Llama 4 Scout, and Qwen3-32B) paired with varied prompting strategies, with a Z3 SMT solver that serves as a formal logic tiebreaker. The central hypothesis is that LLM disagreement within the ensemble signals likely content-biased errors, where real-world believability interferes with logical judgment. By deferring to Z3's structurally-grounded formal verification on these disputed cases, our system achieves 94.3% accuracy with a content effect of 2.85 and a combined score of 41.88 in nested 5-fold cross-validation on the dataset (N=960). This represents a 2.76-point improvement in combined score over the pure ensemble (39.12), with a 0.9% accuracy gain, driven by a 16% reduction in content effect (3.39 to 2.85). Adopting structured-output API calls for Z3 extraction reduced failure rates from ~22% to near zero, and an Aristotelian encoding with existence axioms was validated against task annotations. Our results suggest that targeted neuro-symbolic integration, applying formal methods precisely where ensemble consensus is lowest, can improve the combined accuracy-plus-content-effect metric used by this task.

item.title: Extending One-Step Image Generation from Class Labels to Text via Discriminative Text Representation
item.content:encoded: Few-step generation has been a long-standing goal, with recent one-step generation methods exemplified by MeanFlow achieving remarkable results. Existing research on MeanFlow primarily focuses on class-to-image generation. However, an intuitive yet unexplored direction is to extend the condition from fixed class labels to flexible text inputs, enabling richer content creation. Compared to the limited class labels, text conditions pose greater challenges to the model's understanding capability, necessitating the effective integration of powerful text encoders into the MeanFlow framework. Surprisingly, although incorporating text conditions appears straightforward, we find that integrating powerful LLM-based text encoders using conventional training strategies results in unsatisfactory performance. To uncover the underlying cause, we conduct detailed analyses and reveal that, due to the extremely limited number of refinement steps in the MeanFlow generation, such as only one step, the text feature representations are required to possess sufficiently high discriminability. This also explains why discrete and easily distinguishable class features perform well within the MeanFlow framework. Guided by these insights, we leverage a powerful LLM-based text encoder validated to possess the required semantic properties and adapt the MeanFlow generation process to this framework, resulting in efficient text-conditioned synthesis for the first time. Furthermore, we validate our approach on the widely used diffusion model, demonstrating significant generation performance improvements. We hope this work provides a general and practical reference for future research on text-conditioned MeanFlow generation. The code is available at https://github.com/AMAP-ML/EMF.

item.title: Does "Do Differentiable Simulators Give Better Policy Gradients?'' Give Better Policy Gradients?
item.content:encoded: In policy gradient reinforcement learning, access to a differentiable model enables 1st-order gradient estimation that accelerates learning compared to relying solely on derivative-free 0th-order estimators. However, discontinuous dynamics cause bias and undermine the effectiveness of 1st-order estimators. Prior work addressed this bias by constructing a confidence interval around the REINFORCE 0th-order gradient estimator and using these bounds to detect discontinuities. However, the REINFORCE estimator is notoriously noisy, and we find that this method requires task-specific hyperparameter tuning and has low sample efficiency. This paper asks whether such bias is the primary obstacle and what minimal fixes suffice. First, we re-examine standard discontinuous settings from prior work and introduce DDCG, a lightweight test that switches estimators in nonsmooth regions; with a single hyperparameter, DDCG achieves robust performance and remains reliable with small samples. Second, on differentiable robotics control tasks, we present IVW-H, a per-step inverse-variance implementation that stabilizes variance without explicit discontinuity detection and yields strong results. Together, these findings indicate that while estimator switching improves robustness in controlled studies, careful variance control often dominates in practical deployments.

item.title: Test-Time Perturbation Learning with Delayed Feedback for Vision-Language-Action Models
item.content:encoded: Vision-Language-Action models (VLAs) achieve remarkable performance in sequential decision-making but remain fragile to subtle environmental shifts, such as small changes in object pose. We attribute this brittleness to trajectory overfitting, where VLAs over-attend to the spurious correlation between actions and entities, then reproduce memorized action patterns. We propose Perturbation learning with Delayed Feedback (PDF), a verifier-free test-time adaptation framework that improves decision performance without fine-tuning the base model. PDF mitigates the spurious correlation through uncertainty-based data augmentation and action voting, while an adaptive scheduler allocates augmentation budgets to balance performance and efficiency. To further improve stability, PDF learns a lightweight perturbation module that retrospectively adjusts action logits guided by delayed feedback, correcting overconfidence issue. Experiments on LIBERO (+7.4\% success rate) and Atari (+10.3 human normalized score) demonstrate consistent gains of PDF in task success over vanilla VLA and VLA with test-time adaptation, establishing a practical path toward reliable test-time adaptation in multimodal decision-making agents. The code is available at \href{https://github.com/zhoujiahuan1991/CVPR2026-PDF}{https://github.com/zhoujiahuan1991/CVPR2026-PDF}.

item.title: MUA: Mobile Ultra-detailed Animatable Avatars
item.content:encoded: Building photorealistic, animatable full-body digital humans remains a longstanding challenge in computer graphics and vision. Recent advances in animatable avatar modeling have largely progressed along two directions: improving the fidelity of dynamic geometry and appearance, or reducing computational complexity to enable deployment on resource-constrained platforms, e.g., VR headsets. However, existing approaches fail to achieve both goals simultaneously: Ultra-high-fidelity avatars typically require substantial computation on server-class GPUs, whereas lightweight avatars often suffer from limited surface dynamics, reduced appearance details, and noticeable artifacts. To bridge this gap, we propose a novel animatable avatar representation, termed Wavelet-guided Multi-level Spatial Factorized Blendshapes, and a corresponding distillation pipeline that transfers motion-aware clothing dynamics and fine-grained appearance details from a pre-trained ultra-high-quality avatar model into a compact, efficient representation. By coupling multi-level wavelet spectral decomposition with low-rank structural factorization in texture space, our method achieves up to 2000X lower computational cost and a 10X smaller model size than the original high-quality teacher avatar model, while preserving visually plausible dynamics and appearance details closely resemble those of the teacher model. Extensive comparisons with state-of-the-art methods show that our approach significantly outperforms existing avatar approaches designed for mobile settings and achieves comparable or superior rendering quality to most approaches that can only run on servers. Importantly, our representation substantially improves the practicality of high-fidelity avatars for immersive applications, achieving over 180 FPS on a desktop PC and real-time native on-device performance at 24 FPS on a standalone Meta Quest 3.

item.title: Model in Distress: Sentiment Analysis on French Synthetic Social Media
item.content:encoded: Automated analysis of customer feedback on social media is hindered by three challenges: the high cost of annotated training data, the scarcity of evaluation sets, especially in multilingual settings, and privacy concerns that prevent data sharing and reproducibility. We address these issues by developing a generalizable synthetic data generation pipeline applied to a case study on customer distress detection in French public transportation. Our approach utilizes backtranslation with fine-tuned models to generate 1.7 million synthetic tweets from a small seed corpus, complemented by synthetic reasoning traces. We train 600M-parameter reasoners with English and French reasoning that achieve 77-79% accuracy on human-annotated evaluation data, matching or exceeding SOTA proprietary LLMs and specialized encoders. Beyond reducing annotation costs, our pipeline preserves privacy by eliminating the exposure of sensitive user data. Our methodology can be adopted for other use cases and languages.

item.title: Source-Free Domain Adaptation with Vision-Language Prior
item.content:encoded: Source-Free Domain Adaptation (SFDA) seeks to adapt a source model, which is pre-trained on a supervised source domain, for a target domain, with only access to unlabeled target training data. Relying on pseudo labeling and/or auxiliary supervision, conventional methods are inevitably error-prone. To mitigate this limitation, in this work we for the first time explore the potentials of off-the-shelf vision-language (ViL) multimodal models (e.g., CLIP) with rich whilst heterogeneous knowledge. We find that directly applying the ViL model to the target domain in a zero-shot fashion is unsatisfactory, as it is not specialized for this particular task but largely generic. To make it task-specific, we propose a novel DIFO++ approach. Specifically, DIFO++ alternates between two steps during adaptation: (i) Customizing the ViL model by maximizing the mutual information with the target model in a prompt learning manner, (ii) Distilling the knowledge of this customized ViL model to the target model, centering on gap region reduction. During progressive knowledge adaptation, we first identify and focus on the gap region, where enclosed features are entangled and class-ambiguous, as it often captures richer task-specific semantics. Reliable pseudo-labels are then generated by fusing predictions from the target and ViL models, supported by a memory mechanism. Finally, gap region reduction is guided by category attention and predictive consistency for semantic alignment, complemented by referenced entropy minimization to suppress uncertainty. Extensive experiments show that DIFO++ significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art alternatives. Our code and data are available at https://github.com/tntek/DIFO-Plus.

item.title: Efficient Federated RLHF via Zeroth-Order Policy Optimization
item.content:encoded: This paper considers reinforcement learning from human feedback in a federated learning setting with resource-constrained agents, such as edge devices. We propose an efficient federated RLHF algorithm, named Partitioned, Sign-based Stochastic Zeroth-order Policy Optimization (Par-S$^2$ZPO). The algorithm is built on zeroth-order optimization with binary perturbation, resulting in low communication, computation, and memory complexity by design. Our theoretical analysis establishes an upper bound on the convergence rate of Par-S$^2$ZPO, revealing that it is as efficient as its centralized counterpart in terms of sample complexity but converges faster in terms of policy update iterations. Our experimental results show that it outperforms a FedAvg-based RLHF on four MuJoCo RL tasks.

item.title: Voronoi-guided Bilateral 2D Gaussian Splatting for Arbitrary-Scale Hyperspectral Image Super-Resolution
item.content:encoded: Most existing hyperspectral image super-resolution methods require modifications for different scales, limiting their flexibility in arbitrary-scale reconstruction. 2D Gaussian splatting provides a continuous representation that is compatible with arbitrary-scale super-resolution. Existing methods often rely on rasterization strategies, which may limit flexible spatial modeling. Extending them to hyperspectral image super-resolution remains challenging, as the task requires adaptive spatial reconstruction while preserving spectral fidelity. This paper proposes GaussianHSI, a Gaussian-Splatting-based framework for arbitrary-scale hyperspectral image super-resolution. We develop a Voronoi-Guided Bilateral 2D Gaussian Splatting for spatial reconstruction. After predicting a set of Gaussian functions to represent the input, it associates each target pixel with relevant Gaussian functions through Voronoi-guided selection. The target pixel is then reconstructed by aggregating the selected Gaussian functions with reference-aware bilateral weighting, which considers both geometric relevance and consistency with low-resolution features. We further introduce a Spectral Detail Enhancement module to improve spectral reconstruction. Extensive experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of GaussianHSI over state-of-the-art methods for arbitrary-scale hyperspectral image super-resolution.

item.title: AIT Academy: Cultivating the Complete Agent with a Confucian Three-Domain Curriculum
item.content:encoded: What does it mean to give an AI agent a complete education? Current agent development produces specialists systems optimized for a single capability dimension, whether tool use, code generation, or security awareness that exhibit predictable deficits wherever they were not trained. We argue this pattern reflects a structural absence: there is no curriculum theory for agents, no principled account of what a fully developed agent should know, be, and be able to do across the full scope of intelligent behavior. This paper introduces the AIT Academy (Agents Institute of Technology Academy), a curriculum framework for cultivating AI agents across the tripartite structure of human knowledge. Grounded in Kagan's Three Cultures and UNESCO ISCED-F 2013, AIT organizes agent capability development into three domains: Natural Science and Technical Reasoning (Domain I), Humanities and Creative Expression (Domain II), and Social Science and Ethical Reasoning (Domain III). The Confucian Six Arts (liuyi) a 2,500-year-old holistic education system are reinterpreted as behavioral archetypes that map directly onto trainable agent capabilities within each domain. Three representative training grounds instantiate the framework across multiple backbone LLMs: the ClawdGO Security Dojo (Domain I), Athen's Academy (Domain II), and the Alt Mirage Stage (Domain III). Experiments demonstrate a 15.9-point improvement in security capability scores under weakest-first curriculum scheduling, and a 7-percentage-point gain in social reasoning performance under principled attribution modeling. A cross-domain finding Security Awareness Calibration Pathology (SACP), in which over-trained Domain I agents fail on out-of-distribution evaluation illustrates the diagnostic value of a multi-domain perspective unavailable to any single-domain framework.

item.title: Dual-stream Spatio-Temporal GCN-Transformer Network for 3D Human Pose Estimation
item.content:encoded: 3D human pose estimation is a classic and important research direction in the field of computer vision. In recent years, Transformer-based methods have made significant progress in lifting 2D to 3D human pose estimation. However, these methods primarily focus on modeling global temporal and spatial relationships, neglecting local skeletal relationships and the information interaction between different channels. Therefore, we have proposed a novel method,the Dual-stream Spatio-temporal GCN-Transformer Network (MixTGFormer). This method models the spatial and temporal relationships of human skeletons simultaneously through two parallel channels, achieving effective fusion of global and local features. The core of MixTGFormer is composed of stacked Mixformers. Specifically, the Mixformer includes the Mixformer Block and the Squeeze-and-Excitation Layer ( SE Layer). It first extracts and fuses various information of human skeletons through two parallel Mixformer Blocks with different modes. Then, it further supplements the fused information through the SE Layer. The Mixformer Block integrates Graph Convolutional Networks (GCN) into the Transformer, enhancing both local and global information utilization. Additionally, we further implement its temporal and spatial forms to extract both spatial and temporal relationships. We extensively evaluated our model on two benchmark datasets (Human3.6M and MPI-INF-3DHP). The experimental results showed that, compared to other methods, our MixTGFormer achieved state-of-the-art results, with P1 errors of 37.6mm and 15.7mm on these datasets, respectively.

item.title: Periodic Steady-State Control of a Handkerchief-Spinning Task Using a Parallel Anti-Parallelogram Tendon-driven Wrist
item.content:encoded: Spinning flexible objects, exemplified by traditional Chinese handkerchief performances, demands periodic steady-state motions under nonlinear dynamics with frictional contacts and boundary constraints. To address these challenges, we first design an intuitive dexterous wrist based on a parallel anti-parallelogram tendon-driven structure, which achieves 90 degrees omnidirectional rotation with low inertia and decoupled roll-pitch sensing, and implement a high-low level hierarchical control scheme. We then develop a particle-spring model of the handkerchief for control-oriented abstraction and strategy evaluation. Hardware experiments validate this framework, achieving an unfolding ratio of approximately 99% and fingertip tracking error of RMSE = 2.88 mm in high-dynamic spinning. These results demonstrate that integrating control-oriented modeling with a task-tailored dexterous wrist enables robust rest-to-steady-state transitions and precise periodic manipulation of highly flexible objects. More visualizations: https://slowly1113.github.io/icra2026-handkerchief/

item.title: LeGo-Code: Can Modular Curriculum Learning Advance Complex Code Generation? Insights from Text-to-SQL
item.content:encoded: Recently, code-oriented large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong capabilities in translating natural language into executable code. Text-to-SQL is a significant application of this ability, enabling non-technical users to interact with relational databases using natural language. However, state-of-the-art models continue to struggle with highly complex logic, particularly deeply nested statements involving multiple joins and conditions, as well as with real-world database schemas that are noisy or poorly structured. In this paper, we investigate whether curriculum learning can improve the performance of code-based LLMs on Text-to-SQL tasks. Employing benchmarks including Spider and BIRD, we fine-tune models under different curriculum strategies. Our experiments show that naive curriculum, simply ordering training samples by complexity in a single epoch, fails to surpass standard fine-tuning due to catastrophic forgetting. To overcome this, we propose a Modular Adapter Composition (MAC) strategy. By sequentially training tier-specific adapters on incremental complexity levels (Easy to Extra-Hard), we create a scaffolded learning environment that improves performance on complex queries. Our approach not only produces measurable performance gains on the Spider and BIRD benchmarks but also provides a flexible, "Lego-like" architecture, allowing models to be composed and deployed based on specific schema difficulty requirements. These findings demonstrate that structured, modular learning is a superior alternative to monolithic fine-tuning for mastering the syntax and logic of complex code generation.

item.title: Modular Representation Compression: Adapting LLMs for Efficient and Effective Recommendations
item.content:encoded: Recently, large language models (LLMs) have advanced recommendation systems (RSs), and recent works have begun to explore how to integrate LLMs into industrial RSs. While most approaches deploy LLMs offline to generate and pre-cache augmented representations for RSs, high-dimensional representations from LLMs introduce substantial storage and computational costs. Thus, it is crucial to compress LLM representations effectively. However, we identify a counterintuitive phenomenon during representation compression: Mid-layer Representation Advantage (MRA), where representations from middle layers of LLMs outperform those from final layers in recommendation tasks. This degraded final layer renders existing compression methods, which typically compress on the final layer, suboptimal. We interpret this based on modularity theory that LLMs develop spontaneous internal functional modularity and force the final layer to specialize in the proxy training task. Thus, we propose \underline{M}odul\underline{a}r \underline{R}epresentation \underline{C}ompression (MARC) to explicitly control the modularity of LLMs. First, Modular Adjustment explicitly introduces compression and task adaptation modules, enabling the LLM to operate strictly as a representation-learning module. Next, to ground each module to its specific task, Modular Task Decoupling uses information constraints and different network structures to decouple tasks. Extensive experiments validate that MARC addresses MRA and produces efficient representations. Notably, MARC achieved a 2.82% eCPM lift in an online A/B test within a large-scale commercial search advertising scenario.

item.title: Can LLM-Generated Text Empower Surgical Vision-Language Pre-training?
item.content:encoded: Recent advancements in self-supervised learning have led to powerful surgical vision encoders capable of spatiotemporal understanding. However, extending these visual foundations to multi-modal reasoning tasks is severely bottlenecked by the prohibitive cost of expert textual annotations. To overcome this scalability limitation, we introduce \textbf{LIME}, a large-scale multi-modal dataset derived from open-access surgical videos using human-free, Large Language Model (LLM)-generated narratives. While LIME offers immense scalability, unverified generated texts may contain errors, including hallucinations, that could potentially lead to catastrophically degraded pre-trained medical priors in standard contrastive pipelines. To mitigate this, we propose \textbf{SurgLIME}, a parameter-efficient Vision-Language Pre-training (VLP) framework designed to learn reliable cross-modal alignments using noisy narratives. SurgLIME preserves foundational medical priors using a LoRA-adapted dual-encoder architecture and introduces an automated confidence estimation mechanism that dynamically down-weights uncertain text during contrastive alignment. Evaluations on the AutoLaparo and Cholec80 benchmarks show that SurgLIME achieves competitive zero-shot cross-modal alignment while preserving the robust linear probing performance of the visual foundation model. Dataset, code, and models are publicly available at \href{https://github.com/visurg-ai/SurgLIME}{https://github.com/visurg-ai/SurgLIME}.

item.title: Understanding Human Actions through the Lens of Executable Models
item.content:encoded: Human-centred systems require an understanding of human actions in the physical world. Temporally extended sequences of actions are intentional and structured, yet existing methods for recognising what actions are performed often do not attempt to capture their structure, particularly how the actions are executed. This, however, is crucial for assessing the quality of the action's execution and its differences from other actions. To capture the internal mechanics of actions, we introduce a domain-specific language EXACT that represents human motions as underspecified motion programs, interpreted as reward-generating functions for zero-shot policy inference using forward-backwards representations. By leveraging the compositional nature of EXACT motion programs, we combine individual policies into an executable neuro-symbolic model that uses program structure for compositional modelling. We evaluate the utility of the proposed pipeline for creating executable action models by analysing motion-capture data to understand human actions, for the tasks of human action segmentation and action anomaly detection. Our results show that the use of executable action models improves data efficiency and captures intuitive relationships between actions compared with monolithic, task-specific approaches.

item.title: View-Consistent 3D Scene Editing via Dual-Path Structural Correspondense and Semantic Continuity
item.content:encoded: Text-driven 3D scene editing has recently attracted increasing attention. Most existing methods follow a render-edit-optimize pipeline, where multi-view images are rendered from a 3D scene, edited with 2D image editors, and then used to optimize the underlying 3D representation. However, cross-view inconsistency remains a major bottleneck. Although recent methods introduce geometric cues, cross-view interactions, or video priors to mitigate this issue, they still largely rely on inference-time synchronization and thus remain limited in robustness and generalization.In this work, we recast multi-view consistent 3D editing from a distributional perspective: 3D scene editing essentially requires a joint distribution modeling across viewpoints.Based on this insight, we propose a view-consistent 3D editing framework that explicitly introduces cross-view dependencies into the editing process. Furthermore, motivated by the observation that structural correspondence and semantic continuity rely on different cross-view cues, we introduce a dual-path consistency mechanism consisting of projection-guided structural guidance and patch-level semantic propagation for effective cross-view editing. Further, we construct a paired multi-view editing dataset that provides reliable supervision for learning cross-view consistency in edited scenes. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method achieves superior editing performance with precise and consistent views for complex scenes.

item.title: Semantic Entanglement in Vector-Based Retrieval: A Formal Framework and Context-Conditioned Disentanglement Pipeline for Agentic RAG Systems
item.content:encoded: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems depend on the geometric properties of vector representations to retrieve contextually appropriate evidence. When source documents interleave multiple topics within contiguous text, standard vectorization produces embedding spaces in which semantically distinct content occupies overlapping neighborhoods. We term this condition semantic entanglement. We formalize entanglement as a model-relative measure of cross-topic overlap in embedding space and define an Entanglement Index (EI) as a quantitative proxy. We argue that higher EI constrains attainable Top-K retrieval precision under cosine similarity retrieval. To address this, we introduce the Semantic Disentanglement Pipeline (SDP), a four-stage preprocessing framework that restructures documents prior to embedding. We further propose context-conditioned preprocessing, in which document structure is shaped by patterns of operational use, and a continuous feedback mechanism that adapts document structure based on agent performance. We evaluate SDP on a real-world enterprise healthcare knowledge base comprising over 2,000 documents across approximately 25 sub-domains. Top-K retrieval precision improves from approximately 32% under fixed-token chunking to approximately 82% under SDP, while mean EI decreases from 0.71 to 0.14. We do not claim that entanglement fully explains RAG failure, but that it captures a distinct preprocessing failure mode that downstream optimization cannot reliably correct once encoded into the vector space.

item.title: Safe Control using Learned Safety Filters and Adaptive Conformal Inference
item.content:encoded: Safety filters have been shown to be effective tools to ensure the safety of control systems with unsafe nominal policies. To address scalability challenges in traditional synthesis methods, learning-based approaches have been proposed for designing safety filters for systems with high-dimensional state and control spaces. However, the inevitable errors in the decisions of these models raise concerns about their reliability and the safety guarantees they offer. This paper presents Adaptive Conformal Filtering (ACoFi), a method that combines learned Hamilton-Jacobi reachability-based safety filters with adaptive conformal inference. Under ACoFi, the filter dynamically adjusts its switching criteria based on the observed errors in its predictions of the safety of actions. The range of possible safety values of the nominal policy's output is used to quantify uncertainty in safety assessment. The filter switches from the nominal policy to the learned safe one when that range suggests it might be unsafe. We show that ACoFi guarantees that the rate of incorrectly quantifying uncertainty in the predicted safety of the nominal policy is asymptotically upper bounded by a user-defined parameter. This gives a soft safety guarantee rather than a hard safety guarantee. We evaluate ACoFi in a Dubins car simulation and a Safety Gymnasium environment, empirically demonstrating that it significantly outperforms the baseline method that uses a fixed switching threshold by achieving higher learned safety values and fewer safety violations, especially in out-of-distribution scenarios.

item.title: Balance-Guided Sparse Identification of Multiscale Nonlinear PDEs with Small-coefficient Terms
item.content:encoded: Data-driven discovery of governing equations has advanced significantly in recent years; however, existing methods often struggle in multiscale systems where dynamically significant terms may have small coefficients. Therefore, we propose Balance-Guided SINDy (BG-SINDy) inspired by the principle of dominant balance, which reformulates $\ell_0$-constrained sparse regression as a term-level $\ell_{2,0}$-regularized problem and solves it using a progressive pruning strategy. Terms are ranked according to their relative contributions to the governing equation balance rather than their absolute coefficient magnitudes. Based on this criterion, BG-SINDy alternates between least-squares regression and elimination of negligible terms, thereby preserving dynamically significant terms even when their coefficients are small. Numerical experiments on the Korteweg--de Vries equation with a small dispersion coefficient, a modified Burgers equation with vanishing hyperviscosity, a modified Kuramoto--Sivashinsky equation with multiple small-coefficient terms, and a two-dimensional reaction--diffusion system demonstrate the validity of BG-SINDy in discovering small-coefficient terms. The proposed method thus provides an efficient approach for discovering governing equations that contain small-coefficient terms.

item.title: 基于迭代视觉推理的代码界面精准定位研究
item.content:encoded:
通过迭代视觉反馈提升代码界面操作精度。



摘要： 本文研究了在密集代码界面中实现像素级光标定位的问题，提出了一种基于闭环反馈的迭代优化方法，以提高对复杂界面元素的交互精度。与传统单次坐标预测方法不同，该方法通过多次尝试和视觉反馈进行自我修正，有效提升了点击准确率和任务成功率。实验结果表明，该方法在多个代码基准测试中优于当前最先进的单次模型。研究为下一代可靠软件工程代理提供了关键支持。

item.title: 无参数在线学习算法及其渐进性分析
item.content:encoded:
提出无参数在线学习算法，实现更优的后悔界。



摘要： 本文开发了适用于无约束在线学习的无参数算法，其后悔界与梯度变化量相关。针对L-光滑凸损失函数，算法在无需预先知道比较器范数、Lipschitz常数或平滑性的情况下，实现了次线性后悔界。每一轮更新均可通过闭式表达高效计算。该成果扩展至动态后悔，并在随机扩展对抗模型中取得显著改进。

item.title: AI革命对科学范式的根本性重塑
item.content:encoded:
AI推动科学协作与知识传播方式的根本变革。



摘要： 文章指出，AI革命，尤其是大语言模型的兴起，其核心意义在于改变复杂信息和人类知识的传递、复制与共享方式。AI在科学领域的重要性不仅体现在提升研究效率，更在于重塑科研协作、发现、发表和评估的结构。文章提出了从AI作为研究工具到科学合作者的渐进路径，并探讨了AI如何深刻影响科学出版。同时强调，持续学习和多元思想是AI在原创科学发现中发挥价值的关键。

item.title: 基于多模态注意力的精准降雨实时预测方法M3R
item.content:encoded:
M3R通过多模态注意力机制提升降雨预测准确性和效率。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为M3R的气象信息多模态注意力架构，用于直接进行降雨预测。该方法结合NEXRAD雷达图像与个人天气站（PWS）的数值测量数据，通过时间对齐的综合流程处理异构气象数据。M3R利用天气站的时间序列作为查询，选择性地关注雷达空间特征，从而更精确地提取降水特征。实验结果表明，M3R在三个100公里×100公里区域的表现优于现有方法，在准确性、效率和降水检测能力方面均有显著提升。该研究为多模态降水实时预测设立了新基准，并为实际天气预报系统提供了实用工具。

item.title: 行为倾向训练在小语言模型中的失效研究
item.content:encoded:
小语言模型无法有效提升行为倾向，实验结果被证实为虚假。



摘要： 本文研究了通过四阶段MIT蒸馏流程将自我验证、不确定性承认和反馈整合等行为倾向注入小型语言模型（0.6B至2.3B参数）的效果。初始实验报告了MCAS和HumanEval的显著提升，但后续验证发现这些结果为虚假数据。进一步实验包括微调、注意力头干预和冻结基模型的侧车机制，均未成功提升行为倾向而不损害内容质量或导致风格模仿。该失败现象在五种模型中一致出现，且跨分布验证结果下降。研究还发现Gemma 4 E2B在特定领域表现出高度的置信度与正确性脱钩现象。

item.title: DAM检索动力学的有限样本分析与收敛性研究
item.content:encoded:
DAM在有限样本下实现高效检索与鲁棒性保证。



摘要： 本文对密集关联记忆（DAM）的检索动力学进行了算法分析，提出了在有限样本下的收敛性保证。在模式分离假设和高负载下的干扰限制条件下，证明了异步检索动态的几何收敛性，收敛时间为O(log N)。同时，建立了通过显式边界条件表达的对抗鲁棒性，推导出容量保证为Θ(N^{n-1})，并在最坏情况下保持该尺度。此外，研究还揭示了DAM检索动力学可解释为潜在博弈，确保在异步更新下收敛到纯纳什均衡。附录中提供了完整证明及初步实验验证了理论结果。

item.title: 基于金融损失的多智能体系统对齐方法研究
item.content:encoded:
通过金融损失实现多智能体系统对齐，提升自主软件工程可靠性。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于金融损失的多智能体系统对齐方法（OOM-RL），通过将代理部署在动态、高摩擦的实时金融市场中，利用资本耗尽作为不可篡改的负梯度。研究历时20个月，从初期高度依赖反馈的系统演变为具备流动性意识的架构。实验表明，金融损失迫使系统放弃过拟合幻觉，采用严格的测试驱动代理工作流程（STDAW），并引入确定性验证的代码覆盖率约束矩阵。最终系统在成熟阶段实现了年化夏普比率为2.06的稳定均衡，证明了以经济惩罚替代主观偏好在高风险现实环境中的有效性。

item.title: 对抗环境下安全约束强化学习的鲁棒算法研究
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种对抗环境下安全约束的鲁棒强化学习算法。



摘要： 文章探讨了在存在外部对抗因素的环境中，传统强化学习方法因忽略这些因素而可能导致部署失败的问题。作者将外部因素建模为对抗性策略，并提出一种新的模型化算法 RHC-UCRL，该算法通过乐观性策略同时考虑代理和对抗者的不确定性，实现了对安全约束的满足与次线性遗憾保证。

item.title: RAD-2：一种基于生成-判别框架的闭环自动驾驶规划方法
item.content:encoded:
RAD-2提升自动驾驶规划稳定性与安全性。



摘要： 本文提出RAD-2，一种基于生成-判别框架的闭环自动驾驶规划方法。该方法通过扩散模型生成多样化轨迹候选，并利用强化学习优化的判别器根据长期驾驶质量对轨迹进行重排序，从而避免直接应用稀疏奖励带来的优化不稳定问题。研究还引入了时间一致组相对策略优化和在策略生成器优化技术，以提高强化学习效果并增强闭环反馈。此外，提出了BEV-Warp仿真环境，支持高效大规模训练。实验表明，RAD-2相比强扩散规划器碰撞率降低56%，并在复杂城市交通中提升了感知安全性和驾驶平顺性。

item.title: 结构化数据问答中大语言模型的置信度估计研究
item.content:encoded:
研究大语言模型在结构化数据问答中的置信度估计方法。



摘要： 本文首次系统比较了五种置信度估计方法在五个前沿大语言模型和两个表格问答基准上的表现。结果显示所有模型都存在严重过度自信的问题。研究发现自评方法（如口头化、P(True)）与扰动方法（如语义熵、自我一致性、多格式一致）在性能上有显著差异，其中扰动方法表现更优。文中提出了多格式一致（MFA）方法，利用结构化数据的无损序列化特性，在降低API成本的同时提升置信度估计效果。MFA在TableBench上表现良好，并能与采样方法互补。此外，结构感知校准方法也显著提升了模型性能。

item.title: CoDe-R：一种提升反编译可执行性的轻量级代码优化框架
item.content:encoded:
CoDe-R通过语义增强与动态双路径机制提升反编译代码的可执行性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为CoDe-R的轻量级反编译优化框架，旨在解决传统反编译方法在语义丢失和逻辑错误方面的问题。该框架分为两个阶段：第一阶段采用语义认知增强技术，帮助模型恢复高级算法意图；第二阶段引入动态双路径回退机制，在推理过程中通过混合验证策略平衡语义恢复与语法稳定性。实验表明，使用1.3B参数规模的模型，CoDe-R在HumanEval-Decompile基准上达到了新的SOTA，首次实现超过50%的平均可执行率，显著优于基线模型，有效缩小了高效模型与专家级性能之间的差距。
