item.title: 基于论文自身验证的科学质量保证新方案
item.content:encoded:
提出通过分析论文本身进行质量评估的新方法。



摘要： 文章指出当前科学质量保证体系存在缺陷，传统同行评审和开放科学均无法有效确保论文质量。作者提出一种新方案——直接对论文本身进行验证，开发了名为 sciwrite-lint 的开源工具，可在本地运行，无需上传论文。该工具检查引用文献的真实性、重印状态及与论文内容的一致性，并为每篇参考文献生成可靠性评分。此外，还引入 SciLint Score，结合科学贡献评估框架，进一步提升质量评估能力。

item.title: 基于合成轨迹生成的本地化网页代理框架
item.content:encoded:
通过模拟人类标注角色生成网页轨迹，实现高效本地部署的网页代理模型。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为Agent-as-Annotators的框架，通过模仿人类标注角色来结构化生成网页代理的合成轨迹。该框架利用Gemini 3 Pro作为教师模型，生成3000条轨迹，并通过监督学习微调一个9B参数的学生模型。在WebArena测试中，该模型达到41.5%的准确率，优于多个闭源模型。此外，模型在未见过的环境和多个基准测试中也表现出色。实验表明，每个模块组件都对性能提升有显著贡献。该研究证明，仅需一个前沿教师模型即可生成具有竞争力的本地部署网页代理。

item.title: 面向多模态深度伪造的通用检测框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出MAF框架提升多模态深度伪造检测泛化能力。



摘要： 随着生成式人工智能的发展，深度伪造攻击已从单一模态演变为复杂的多模态威胁。现有检测技术依赖于特定模态的表面特征，导致在面对未知模态时性能严重下降。本文提出一种新的多模态取证范式——模态无关伪造检测（MAF），通过解耦模态特有风格，提取跨模态的伪造知识。同时定义了两种泛化维度：对语义相关模态的迁移能力（弱MAF）和对孤立信号的鲁棒性（强MAF）。为验证该方法，构建了DeepModal-Bench基准测试平台，集成多种多模态检测算法并应用先进泛化学习方法。实验表明，MAF框架在未知模态上取得显著突破，为多模态防御提供了新路径。

item.title: 基于3D先验的开放世界机器人操作方法LAMP
item.content:encoded:
LAMP通过3D先验提升机器人操作的泛化能力。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为LAMP的方法，用于解决开放世界中机器人操作的泛化问题。现有基于学习的方法在面对新任务和未知环境时表现不佳，而大语言模型和视觉语言模型由于缺乏3D感知能力，也难以进行精细操作。LAMP通过将图像编辑作为3D先验，提取物体间的3D变换，形成几何感知的连续表示，从而提供更精确的操作指导。实验表明，该方法在开放世界操作任务中表现出色，具备强大的零样本泛化能力。

item.title: 基于文本引导的视频行人重识别方法CG-CLIP
item.content:encoded:
提出CG-CLIP框架提升视频行人重识别性能。



摘要： 本文针对视频行人重识别（ReID）在高难度场景下的挑战，如运动中的多人穿着相似服装，提出CG-CLIP框架。该方法结合文本描述和可学习标记，包含Caption-guided Memory Refinement（CMR）和Token-based Feature Extraction（TFE）两个关键组件。CMR通过多模态大语言模型生成的描述优化身份特征，TFE则利用交叉注意力机制高效聚合时空特征。实验在多个数据集上验证了该方法的有效性，结果表明其优于现有方法。

item.title: Multi-Agent Orchestration for High-Throughput Materials Screening on a Leadership-Class System
item.content:encoded: The integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) with High-Performance Computing (HPC) is transforming scientific workflows from human-directed pipelines into adaptive systems capable of autonomous decision-making. Large language models (LLMs) play a critical role in autonomous workflows; however, deploying LLM-based agents at scale remains a significant challenge. Single-agent architectures and sequential tool calls often become serialization bottlenecks when executing large-scale simulation campaigns, failing to utilize the massive parallelism of exascale resources. To address this, we present a scalable, hierarchical multi-agent framework for orchestrating high-throughput screening campaigns. Our planner-executor architecture employs a central planning agent to dynamically partition workloads and assign subtasks to a swarm of parallel executor agents. All executor agents interface with a shared Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that orchestrates tasks via the Parsl workflow engine. To demonstrate this framework, we employed the open-weight gpt-oss-120b model to orchestrate a high-throughput screening of the Computation-Ready Experimental (CoRE) Metal-Organic Framework (MOF) database for atmospheric water harvesting. The results demonstrate that the proposed agentic framework enables efficient and scalable execution on the Aurora supercomputer, with low orchestration overhead and high task completion rates. This work establishes a flexible paradigm for LLM-driven scientific automation on HPC systems, with broad applicability to materials discovery and beyond.

item.title: An Imperfect Verifier is Good Enough: Learning with Noisy Rewards
item.content:encoded: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has become a prominent method for post-training Large Language Models (LLMs). However, verifiers are rarely error-free; even deterministic checks can be inaccurate, and the growing dependence on model-based judges exacerbates the issue. The extent to which RLVR is robust to such noise and the verifier accuracy required for effective training remain unresolved questions. We investigate these questions in the domains of code generation and scientific reasoning by introducing noise into RL training. Noise rates up to 15% yield peak validation accuracy within 2 percentage points of the clean baseline. These findings are consistent across controlled and model-based noise types, three model families (Qwen3, GLM4, Llama 3.1), and model sizes from 4B to 9B. Overall, the results indicate that imperfect verification does not constitute a fundamental barrier to RLVR. Furthermore, our findings suggest that practitioners should prioritize moderate accuracy with high precision over perfect verification.

item.title: U-CECE: A Universal Multi-Resolution Framework for Conceptual Counterfactual Explanations
item.content:encoded: As AI models grow more complex, explainability is essential for building trust, yet concept-based counterfactual methods still face a trade-off between expressivity and efficiency. Representing underlying concepts as atomic sets is fast but misses relational context, whereas full graph representations are more faithful but require solving the NP-hard Graph Edit Distance (GED) problem. We propose U-CECE, a unified, model-agnostic multi-resolution framework for conceptual counterfactual explanations that adapts to data regime and compute budget. U-CECE spans three levels of expressivity: atomic concepts for broad explanations, relational sets-of-sets for simple interactions, and structural graphs for full semantic structure. At the structural level, both a precision-oriented transductive mode based on supervised Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) and a scalable inductive mode based on unsupervised graph autoencoders (GAEs) are supported. Experiments on the structurally divergent CUB and Visual Genome datasets characterize the efficiency-expressivity trade-off across levels, while human surveys and LVLM-based evaluation show that the retrieved structural counterfactuals are semantically equivalent to, and often preferred over, exact GED-based ground-truth explanations.

item.title: Adaptive Depth-converted-Scale Convolution for Self-supervised Monocular Depth Estimation
item.content:encoded: Self-supervised monocular depth estimation (MDE) has received increasing interests in the last few years. The objects in the scene, including the object size and relationship among different objects, are the main clues to extract the scene structure. However, previous works lack the explicit handling of the changing sizes of the object due to the change of its depth. Especially in a monocular video, the size of the same object is continuously changed, resulting in size and depth ambiguity. To address this problem, we propose a Depth-converted-Scale Convolution (DcSConv) enhanced monocular depth estimation framework, by incorporating the prior relationship between the object depth and object scale to extract features from appropriate scales of the convolution receptive field. The proposed DcSConv focuses on the adaptive scale of the convolution filter instead of the local deformation of its shape. It establishes that the scale of the convolution filter matters no less (or even more in the evaluated task) than its local deformation. Moreover, a Depth-converted-Scale aware Fusion (DcS-F) is developed to adaptively fuse the DcSConv features and the conventional convolution features. Our DcSConv enhanced monocular depth estimation framework can be applied on top of existing CNN based methods as a plug-and-play module to enhance the conventional convolution block. Extensive experiments with different baselines have been conducted on the KITTI benchmark and our method achieves the best results with an improvement up to 11.6% in terms of SqRel reduction. Ablation study also validates the effectiveness of each proposed module.

item.title: QaRL: Rollout-Aligned Quantization-Aware RL for Fast and Stable Training under Training--Inference Mismatch
item.content:encoded: Large language model (LLM) reinforcement learning (RL) pipelines are often bottlenecked by rollout generation, making end-to-end training slow. Recent work mitigates this by running rollouts with quantization to accelerate decoding, which is the most expensive stage of the RL loop. However, these setups destabilize optimization by amplifying the training-inference gap: rollouts are operated at low precision, while learning updates are computed at full precision. To address this challenge, we propose QaRL (Rollout Alignment Quantization-Aware RL), which aligns training-side forward with the quantized rollout to minimize mismatch. We further identify a failure mode in quantized rollouts: long-form responses tend to produce repetitive, garbled tokens (error tokens). To mitigate these problems, we introduce TBPO (Trust-Band Policy Optimization), a sequence-level objective with dual clipping for negative samples, aimed at keeping updates within the trust region. On Qwen3-30B-A3B MoE for math problems, QaRL outperforms quantized-rollout training by +5.5 while improving stability and preserving low-bit throughput benefits.

item.title: AnomalyAgent: Agentic Industrial Anomaly Synthesis via Tool-Augmented Reinforcement Learning
item.content:encoded: Industrial anomaly generation is a crucial method for alleviating the data scarcity problem in anomaly detection tasks. Most existing anomaly synthesis methods rely on single-step generation mechanisms, lacking complex reasoning and iterative optimization capabilities, making it difficult to generate anomaly samples with high semantic realism. We propose AnomalyAgent, an anomaly synthesis agent with self-reflection, knowledge retrieval, and iterative refinement capabilities, aiming to generate realistic and diverse anomalies. Specifically, AnomalyAgent is equipped with five tools: Prompt Generation (PG), Image Generation (IG), Quality Evaluation (QE), Knowledge Retrieval (KR), and Mask Generation (MG), enabling closed-loop optimization. To improve decision-making and self-reflection, we construct structured trajectories from real anomaly images and design a two-stage training framework: supervised fine-tuning followed by reinforcement learning. This process is driven by a three-part reward mechanism: (1) task rewards to supervise the quality and location rationality of generated anomalies; (2) reflection rewards to train the model's ability to improve anomaly synthesis prompt; (3) behavioral rewards to ensure adherence to the trajectory. On the MVTec-AD dataset, AnomalyAgent achieves IS/IC-L of 2.10/0.33 for anomaly generation, 57.0% classification accuracy using ResNet34, and 99.3%/74.2% AP at the image/pixel level using a simple UNet, surpassing all zero-shot SOTA methods. The code and data will be made publicly available.

item.title: Automatic Generation of Executable BPMN Models from Medical Guidelines
item.content:encoded: We present an end-to-end pipeline that converts healthcare policy documents into executable, data-aware Business Process Model and Notation (BPMN) models using large language models (LLMs) for simulation-based policy evaluation. We address the main challenges of automated policy digitization with four contributions: data-grounded BPMN generation with syntax auto-correction, executable augmentation, KPI instrumentation, and entropy-based uncertainty detection. We evaluate the pipeline on diabetic nephropathy prevention guidelines from three Japanese municipalities, generating 100 models per backend across three LLMs and executing each against 1,000 synthetic patients. On well-structured policies, the pipeline achieves a 100% ground-truth match with perfect per-patient decision agreement. Across all conditions, raw per-patient decision agreement exceeds 92%, and entropy scores increase monotonically with document complexity, confirming that the detector reliably separates unambiguous policies from those requiring targeted human clarification.

item.title: AT-ADD: All-Type Audio Deepfake Detection Challenge Evaluation Plan
item.content:encoded: The rapid advancement of Audio Large Language Models (ALLMs) has enabled cost-effective, high-fidelity generation and manipulation of both speech and non-speech audio, including sound effects, singing voices, and music. While these capabilities foster creativity and content production, they also introduce significant security and trust challenges, as realistic audio deepfakes can now be generated and disseminated at scale. Existing audio deepfake detection (ADD) countermeasures (CMs) and benchmarks, however, remain largely speech-centric, often relying on speech-specific artifacts and exhibiting limited robustness to real-world distortions, as well as restricted generalization to heterogeneous audio types and emerging spoofing techniques. To address these gaps, we propose the All-Type Audio Deepfake Detection (AT-ADD) Grand Challenge for ACM Multimedia 2026, designed to bridge controlled academic evaluation with practical multimedia forensics. AT-ADD comprises two tracks: (1) Robust Speech Deepfake Detection, which evaluates detectors under real-world scenarios and against unseen, state-of-the-art speech generation methods; and (2) All-Type Audio Deepfake Detection, which extends detection beyond speech to diverse, unknown audio types and promotes type-agnostic generalization across speech, sound, singing, and music. By providing standardized datasets, rigorous evaluation protocols, and reproducible baselines, AT-ADD aims to accelerate the development of robust and generalizable audio forensic technologies, supporting secure communication, reliable media verification, and responsible governance in an era of pervasive synthetic audio.

item.title: Efficient Dataset Selection for Continual Adaptation of Generative Recommenders
item.content:encoded: Recommendation systems must continuously adapt to evolving user behavior, yet the volume of data generated in large-scale streaming environments makes frequent full retraining impractical. This work investigates how targeted data selection can mitigate performance degradation caused by temporal distributional drift while maintaining scalability. We evaluate a range of representation choices and sampling strategies for curating small but informative subsets of user interaction data. Our results demonstrate that gradient-based representations, coupled with distribution-matching, improve downstream model performance, achieving training efficiency gains while preserving robustness to drift. These findings highlight data curation as a practical mechanism for scalable monitoring and adaptive model updates in production-scale recommendation systems.

item.title: Information-Theoretic Requirements for Gradient-Based Task Affinity Estimation in Multi-Task Learning
item.content:encoded: Multi-task learning shows strikingly inconsistent results -- sometimes joint training helps substantially, sometimes it actively harms performance -- yet the field lacks a principled framework for predicting these outcomes. We identify a fundamental but unstated assumption underlying gradient-based task analysis: tasks must share training instances for gradient conflicts to reveal genuine relationships. When tasks are measured on the same inputs, gradient alignment reflects shared mechanistic structure; when measured on disjoint inputs, any apparent signal conflates task relationships with distributional shift. We discover this sample overlap requirement exhibits a sharp phase transition: below 30% overlap, gradient-task correlations are statistically indistinguishable from noise; above 40%, they reliably recover known biological structure. Comprehensive validation across multiple datasets achieves strong correlations and recovers biological pathway organization. Standard benchmarks systematically violate this requirement -- MoleculeNet operates at <5% overlap, TDC at 8-14% -- far below the threshold where gradient analysis becomes meaningful. This provides the first principled explanation for seven years of inconsistent MTL results.

item.title: GaussiAnimate: Reconstruct and Rig Animatable Categories with Level of Dynamics
item.content:encoded: Free-form bones, that conform closely to the surface, can effectively capture non-rigid deformations, but lack a kinematic structure necessary for intuitive control. Thus, we propose a Scaffold-Skin Rigging System, termed "Skelebones", with three key steps: (1) Bones: compress temporally-consistent deformable Gaussians into free-form bones, approximating non-rigid surface deformations; (2) Skeleton: extract a Mean Curvature Skeleton from canonical Gaussians and refine it temporally, ensuring a category-agnostic, motion-adaptive, and topology-correct kinematic structure; (3) Binding: bind the skeleton and bones via non-parametric partwise motion matching (PartMM), synthesizing novel bone motions by matching, retrieving, and blending existing ones. Collectively, these three steps enable us to compress the Level of Dynamics of 4D shapes into compact skelebones that are both controllable and expressive. We validate our approach on both synthetic and real-world datasets, achieving significant improvements in reanimation performance across unseen poses-with 17.3% PSNR gains over Linear Blend Skinning (LBS) and 21.7% over Bag-of-Bones (BoB)-while maintaining excellent reconstruction fidelity, particularly for characters exhibiting complex non-rigid surface dynamics. Our Partwise Motion Matching algorithm demonstrates strong generalization to both Gaussian and mesh representations, especially under low-data regime (~1000 frames), achieving 48.4% RMSE improvement over robust LBS and outperforming GRU- and MLP-based learning methods by >20%. Code will be made publicly available for research purposes at cookmaker.cn/gaussianimate.

item.title: Multimodal Reasoning with LLM for Encrypted Traffic Interpretation: A Benchmark
item.content:encoded: Network traffic, as a key media format, is crucial for ensuring security and communications in modern internet infrastructure. While existing methods offer excellent performance, they face two key bottlenecks: (1) They fail to capture multidimensional semantics beyond unimodal sequence patterns. (2) Their black box property, i.e., providing only category labels, lacks an auditable reasoning process. We identify a key factor that existing network traffic datasets are primarily designed for classification and inherently lack rich semantic annotations, failing to generate human-readable evidence report. To address data scarcity, this paper proposes a Byte-Grounded Traffic Description (BGTD) benchmark for the first time, combining raw bytes with structured expert annotations. BGTD provides necessary behavioral features and verifiable chains of evidence for multimodal reasoning towards explainable encrypted traffic interpretation. Built upon BGTD, this paper proposes an end-to-end traffic-language representation framework (mmTraffic), a multimodal reasoning architecture bridging physical traffic encoding and semantic interpretation. In order to alleviate modality interference and generative hallucinations, mmTraffic adopts a jointly-optimized perception-cognition architecture. By incorporating a perception-centered traffic encoder and a cognition-centered LLM generator, mmTraffic achieves refined traffic interpretation with guaranteed category prediction. Extensive experiments demonstrate that mmTraffic autonomously generates high-fidelity, human-readable, and evidence-grounded traffic interpretation reports, while maintaining highly competitive classification accuracy comparing to specialized unimodal model (e.g., NetMamba). The source code is available at https://github.com/lgzhangzlg/Multimodal-Reasoning-with-LLM-for-Encrypted-Traffic-Interpretation-A-Benchmark

item.title: Verify Before You Commit: Towards Faithful Reasoning in LLM Agents via Self-Auditing
item.content:encoded: In large language model (LLM) agents, reasoning trajectories are treated as reliable internal beliefs for guiding actions and updating memory. However, coherent reasoning can still violate logical or evidential constraints, allowing unsupported beliefs repeatedly stored and propagated across decision steps, leading to systematic behavioral drift in long-horizon agentic systems. Most existing strategies rely on the consensus mechanism, conflating agreement with faithfulness. In this paper, inspired by the vulnerability of unfaithful intermediate reasoning trajectories, we propose \textbf{S}elf-\textbf{A}udited \textbf{Ve}rified \textbf{R}easoning (\textsc{SAVeR}), a novel framework that enforces verification over internal belief states within the agent before action commitment, achieving faithful reasoning. Concretely, we structurally generate persona-based diverse candidate beliefs for selection under a faithfulness-relevant structure space. To achieve reasoning faithfulness, we perform adversarial auditing to localize violations and repair through constraint-guided minimal interventions under verifiable acceptance criteria. Extensive experiments on six benchmark datasets demonstrate that our approach consistently improves reasoning faithfulness while preserving competitive end-task performance.

item.title: Evaluating Counterfactual Explanation Methods on Incomplete Inputs
item.content:encoded: Existing algorithms for generating Counterfactual Explanations (CXs) for Machine Learning (ML) typically assume fully specified inputs. However, real-world data often contains missing values, and the impact of these incomplete inputs on the performance of existing CX methods remains unexplored. To address this gap, we systematically evaluate recent CX generation methods on their ability to provide valid and plausible counterfactuals when inputs are incomplete. As part of this investigation, we hypothesize that robust CX generation methods will be better suited to address the challenge of providing valid and plausible counterfactuals when inputs are incomplete. Our findings reveal that while robust CX methods achieve higher validity than non-robust ones, all methods struggle to find valid counterfactuals. These results motivate the need for new CX methods capable of handling incomplete inputs.

item.title: Act Wisely: Cultivating Meta-Cognitive Tool Use in Agentic Multimodal Models
item.content:encoded: The advent of agentic multimodal models has empowered systems to actively interact with external environments. However, current agents suffer from a profound meta-cognitive deficit: they struggle to arbitrate between leveraging internal knowledge and querying external utilities. Consequently, they frequently fall prey to blind tool invocation, resorting to reflexive tool execution even when queries are resolvable from the raw visual context. This pathological behavior precipitates severe latency bottlenecks and injects extraneous noise that derails sound reasoning. Existing reinforcement learning protocols attempt to mitigate this via a scalarized reward that penalizes tool usage. Yet, this coupled formulation creates an irreconcilable optimization dilemma: an aggressive penalty suppresses essential tool use, whereas a mild penalty is entirely subsumed by the variance of the accuracy reward during advantage normalization, rendering it impotent against tool overuse. To transcend this bottleneck, we propose HDPO, a framework that reframes tool efficiency from a competing scalar objective to a strictly conditional one. By eschewing reward scalarization, HDPO maintains two orthogonal optimization channels: an accuracy channel that maximizes task correctness, and an efficiency channel that enforces execution economy exclusively within accurate trajectories via conditional advantage estimation. This decoupled architecture naturally induces a cognitive curriculum-compelling the agent to first master task resolution before refining its self-reliance. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that our resulting model, Metis, reduces tool invocations by orders of magnitude while simultaneously elevating reasoning accuracy.

item.title: Scalable Neural Decoders for Practical Fault-Tolerant Quantum Computation
item.content:encoded: Quantum error correction (QEC) is essential for scalable quantum computing. However, it requires classical decoders that are fast and accurate enough to keep pace with quantum hardware. While quantum low-density parity-check codes have recently emerged as a promising route to efficient fault tolerance, current decoding algorithms do not allow one to realize the full potential of these codes in practical settings. Here, we introduce a convolutional neural network decoder that exploits the geometric structure of QEC codes, and use it to probe a novel "waterfall" regime of error suppression, demonstrating that the logical error rates required for large-scale fault-tolerant algorithms are attainable with modest code sizes at current physical error rates, and with latencies within the real-time budgets of several leading hardware platforms. For example, for the $[144, 12, 12]$ Gross code, the decoder achieves logical error rates up to $\sim 17$x below existing decoders - reaching logical error rates $\sim 10^{-10}$ at physical error $p=0.1\%$ - with 3-5 orders of magnitude higher throughput. This decoder also produces well-calibrated confidence estimates that can significantly reduce the time overhead of repeat-until-success protocols. Taken together, these results suggest that the space-time costs associated with fault-tolerant quantum computation may be significantly lower than previously anticipated.

item.title: Ads in AI Chatbots? An Analysis of How Large Language Models Navigate Conflicts of Interest
item.content:encoded: Today's large language models (LLMs) are trained to align with user preferences through methods such as reinforcement learning. Yet models are beginning to be deployed not merely to satisfy users, but also to generate revenue for the companies that created them through advertisements. This creates the potential for LLMs to face conflicts of interest, where the most beneficial response to a user may not be aligned with the company's incentives. For instance, a sponsored product may be more expensive but otherwise equal to another; in this case, what does (and should) the LLM recommend to the user? In this paper, we provide a framework for categorizing the ways in which conflicting incentives might lead LLMs to change the way they interact with users, inspired by literature from linguistics and advertising regulation. We then present a suite of evaluations to examine how current models handle these tradeoffs. We find that a majority of LLMs forsake user welfare for company incentives in a multitude of conflict of interest situations, including recommending a sponsored product almost twice as expensive (Grok 4.1 Fast, 83%), surfacing sponsored options to disrupt the purchasing process (GPT 5.1, 94%), and concealing prices in unfavorable comparisons (Qwen 3 Next, 24%). Behaviors also vary strongly with levels of reasoning and users' inferred socio-economic status. Our results highlight some of the hidden risks to users that can emerge when companies begin to subtly incentivize advertisements in chatbots.

item.title: Preference Redirection via Attention Concentration: An Attack on Computer Use Agents
item.content:encoded: Advancements in multimodal foundation models have enabled the development of Computer Use Agents (CUAs) capable of autonomously interacting with GUI environments. As CUAs are not restricted to certain tools, they allow to automate more complex agentic tasks but at the same time open up new security vulnerabilities. While prior work has concentrated on the language modality, the vulnerability of the vision modality has received less attention. In this paper, we introduce PRAC, a novel attack that, unlike prior work targeting the VLM output directly, manipulates the model's internal preferences by redirecting its attention toward a stealthy adversarial patch. We show that PRAC is able to manipulate the selection process of a CUA on an online shopping platform towards a chosen target product. While we require white-box access to the model for the creation of the attack, we show that our attack generalizes to fine-tuned versions of the same model, presenting a critical threat as multiple companies build specific CUAs based on open weights models.

item.title: Scal3R: Scalable Test-Time Training for Large-Scale 3D Reconstruction
item.content:encoded: This paper addresses the task of large-scale 3D scene reconstruction from long video sequences. Recent feed-forward reconstruction models have shown promising results by directly regressing 3D geometry from RGB images without explicit 3D priors or geometric constraints. However, these methods often struggle to maintain reconstruction accuracy and consistency over long sequences due to limited memory capacity and the inability to effectively capture global contextual cues. In contrast, humans can naturally exploit the global understanding of the scene to inform local perception. Motivated by this, we propose a novel neural global context representation that efficiently compresses and retains long-range scene information, enabling the model to leverage extensive contextual cues for enhanced reconstruction accuracy and consistency. The context representation is realized through a set of lightweight neural sub-networks that are rapidly adapted during test time via self-supervised objectives, which substantially increases memory capacity without incurring significant computational overhead. The experiments on multiple large-scale benchmarks, including the KITTI Odometry~\cite{Geiger2012CVPR} and Oxford Spires~\cite{tao2025spires} datasets, demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach in handling ultra-large scenes, achieving leading pose accuracy and state-of-the-art 3D reconstruction accuracy while maintaining efficiency. Code is available at https://zju3dv.github.io/scal3r.

item.title: FIT: A Large-Scale Dataset for Fit-Aware Virtual Try-On
item.content:encoded: Given a person and a garment image, virtual try-on (VTO) aims to synthesize a realistic image of the person wearing the garment, while preserving their original pose and identity. Although recent VTO methods excel at visualizing garment appearance, they largely overlook a crucial aspect of the try-on experience: the accuracy of garment fit -- for example, depicting how an extra-large shirt looks on an extra-small person. A key obstacle is the absence of datasets that provide precise garment and body size information, particularly for "ill-fit" cases, where garments are significantly too large or too small. Consequently, current VTO methods default to generating well-fitted results regardless of the garment or person size. In this paper, we take the first steps towards solving this open problem. We introduce FIT (Fit-Inclusive Try-on), a large-scale VTO dataset comprising over 1.13M try-on image triplets accompanied by precise body and garment measurements. We overcome the challenges of data collection via a scalable synthetic strategy: (1) We programmatically generate 3D garments using GarmentCode and drape them via physics simulation to capture realistic garment fit. (2) We employ a novel re-texturing framework to transform synthetic renderings into photorealistic images while strictly preserving geometry. (3) We introduce person identity preservation into our re-texturing model to generate paired person images (same person, different garments) for supervised training. Finally, we leverage our FIT dataset to train a baseline fit-aware virtual try-on model. Our data and results set the new state-of-the-art for fit-aware virtual try-on, as well as offer a robust benchmark for future research. We will make all data and code publicly available on our project page: https://johannakarras.github.io/FIT.

item.title: ConceptTracer：一种用于分析神经网络表示的交互式工具
item.content:encoded:
ConceptTracer帮助分析神经网络中的可解释概念，提升模型透明度。



摘要： 本文介绍了ConceptTracer，这是一个用于通过人类可理解的概念分析神经网络表示的交互式应用。该工具结合了两种信息论度量方法，用于量化概念的重要性和选择性，从而帮助识别对特定概念敏感的神经元。研究展示了ConceptTracer在TabPFN模型上的有效性，证明其有助于发现可解释的神经元。该工具为研究神经网络如何编码概念级信息提供了实用框架，并已开源。

item.title: 实时直播新型视图合成方法LiveStre4m
item.content:encoded:
LiveStre4m实现低延迟实时新型视图合成直播。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为LiveStre4m的新型直播视图合成方法，用于从无标定多视角视频中实时生成新视角视频。该方法采用多视角视觉Transformer进行关键帧3D场景重建，并结合扩散Transformer插值模块确保时间一致性。同时引入相机位姿预测模块，直接从RGB图像估计相机姿态和内参，无需依赖已知标定信息。实验表明，LiveStre4m在1024×768分辨率下每帧平均重建时间为0.07秒，相比基于优化的方法快数个数量级，为实际部署实时新型视图合成系统提供了可行方案。

item.title: 基于可穿戴设备的乳腺癌患者心理压力评估研究
item.content:encoded:
利用可穿戴设备评估乳腺癌患者的感知压力水平。



摘要： 本研究通过分析老年乳腺癌患者佩戴的智能手表和心电传感器数据，评估其心理压力水平。研究采用多模态可穿戴数据生成异构视觉表示，并使用轻量级预训练模型Tiny-BioMoE进行嵌入，结合注意力机制的多实例学习方法预测三个月和六个月的感知压力评分。在留一患者评估中，预测结果与问卷评分有中等一致性，显示该方法在临床应用中的潜力。

item.title: VersaVogue：统一的多条件可控时尚合成框架
item.content:encoded:
提出VersaVogue，实现多条件可控的时尚图像生成与虚拟试穿。



摘要： 本文提出VersaVogue，一个统一的多条件可控时尚合成框架，能够同时支持服装生成和虚拟试穿，覆盖时尚生命周期的设计与展示阶段。通过引入特质路由注意力模块（TA），动态路由条件特征至最合适的专家和生成层，实现纹理、形状和颜色等视觉属性的解耦注入。此外，开发了自动化多视角偏好优化（MPO）流程，无需人工标注或特定任务奖励模型即可构建偏好数据，提升生成图像的真实性和可控性。实验表明，VersaVogue在视觉保真度、语义一致性和细粒度控制方面均优于现有方法。

item.title: FlowExtract：从流程图中提取可查询的流程知识
item.content:encoded:
FlowExtract高效提取流程图中的节点与连接关系，提升制造流程知识的可访问性。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种名为FlowExtract的系统，用于从符合ISO 5807标准的流程图中提取有向图。该系统通过将元素检测与连接拓扑重建分离，利用YOLOv8和EasyOCR进行节点检测与文本提取，并采用一种新的边检测方法，通过分析箭头方向和反向追踪连接线来识别连接关系。实验表明，FlowExtract在节点检测上表现优异，在边提取方面显著优于视觉-语言模型基线，为制造业提供了更高效的流程知识表示方式。项目代码已开源。

item.title: DC算法的连续时间结构与收敛性分析
item.content:encoded:
研究DC算法在连续时间下的结构及其收敛性质。



摘要： 本文探讨了具有强凸成分的平滑DC分解下的连续时间DC算法（DCA）结构。通过双坐标视角，经典DCA被证明是某种非线性自治系统的全步显式欧拉离散化。该观点启发了一个阻尼DCA方案，其渐近步长极限对应由分解凸部分生成的Hessian-Riemannian梯度流。文章证明了阻尼方案的单调下降、渐近临界性、在有界条件下的Kurdyka-Lojasiewicz收敛性以及在度量DC-PL不等式下的全局线性速率。对于极限流，建立了精确的能量恒等式、有界轨迹的渐近临界性、在度量相对误差界限下的显式全局速率、在Kurdyka-Lojasiewicz假设下的有限长度和单点收敛性，以及在非退化解附近的局部指数收敛性。分析还揭示了全局与局部权衡：半松弛方案在框架中提供了最佳全局保证，而全步方案在非退化解附近局部最快。最后，文章展示了同一目标的不同DC分解会因凸成分产生的度量而引发不同的连续动力学，为分解质量提供几何标准，并将DCA与Bregman几何联系起来。

item.title: 基于能量正则化的空间掩码方法提升视觉模型性能
item.content:encoded:
ERSM通过能量最小化实现高效特征选择，提升模型鲁棒性与可解释性。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为Energy-Regularized Spatial Masking (ERSM)的新框架，将特征选择转化为可微的能量最小化问题。该方法在标准卷积网络中嵌入轻量级能量掩码层，为每个视觉标记分配由内在重要性成本和空间一致性惩罚组成的能量值。相比传统剪枝方法，ERSM能自主发现最优信息密度平衡，实现动态稀疏性。实验表明，该方法在保持分类准确率的同时，提升了对结构化遮挡的鲁棒性，并生成高可解释的空间掩码，其学习到的能量排序在删除测试中优于基于幅度的剪枝，展现出内在去噪能力。

item.title: 提升大语言模型推理能力的神经元增强方法
item.content:encoded:
通过增强相关神经元激活提升模型推理表现。



摘要： 本文研究了大语言模型在看似理解的任务中仍出现失败的现象，发现这并非由于知识缺失，而是某些内部神经元电路在推理过程中未被充分激活。为此，作者提出Selective Neuron Amplification（SNA）方法，在不改变模型参数的情况下，增强与任务相关的神经元影响力。该方法仅在模型不确定时有效，而在模型自信时影响较小，表明部分模型失败是由于激活不足而非能力不足。

item.title: 黑威尔理论对现代人工智能的深远影响
item.content:encoded:
黑威尔理论在AI和机器学习中仍具重要影响。



摘要： 本文回顾了数学家大卫·黑威尔在统计学、博弈论和决策理论方面的三大核心贡献——Rao-Blackwell定理、Blackwell可接近性定理和信息性定理，并探讨其对现代人工智能和机器学习的直接影响。这些理论诞生于20世纪40年代和50年代，至今仍在马尔可夫链蒙特卡洛推断、自主机器人导航、生成模型训练、无遗憾在线学习、从人类反馈中强化学习（RLHF）、大语言模型对齐及信息设计等前沿领域发挥关键作用。NVIDIA 2024年以‘Blackwell’命名其旗舰GPU架构，印证了黑威尔理论的持久影响力。文章还指出一个新兴方向：LLM RLHF流程中的显式Rao-Blackwell化方差缩减，虽尚未成为标准实践，但已引起关注。黑威尔理论构成了处理信息压缩、不确定性下的顺序决策及信息源比较的统一框架，这正是现代AI的核心问题。

item.title: 基于扩散模型的高质量全身虚拟形象生成与编辑方法
item.content:encoded:
GenLCA实现从文本和图像生成高保真全身虚拟形象。



摘要： 本文提出GenLCA，一种基于扩散模型的生成方法，能够根据文本和图像输入生成并编辑逼真的全身虚拟形象。该模型通过将部分可观测的2D数据转化为结构化的3D标记，实现了大规模真实视频数据的训练，从而提升了生成结果的逼真度和通用性。为解决视频中身体部分观测不全导致的模糊或透明伪影问题，研究者提出了可见性感知的扩散训练策略，仅在有效区域计算损失。实验表明，GenLCA在多种生成和编辑任务中均优于现有方法，展示了其强大的性能。

item.title: 自动驾驶控制交接预测与多模态数据集研究
item.content:encoded:
研究自动驾驶系统中驾驶者与车辆的控制交接行为，提出多模态数据集BATON。



摘要： 本文探讨了现有自动驾驶系统中驾驶员在控制交接时的行为模式，并指出当前系统在情境判断和用户体验上的不足。为解决这一问题，研究团队构建了BATON数据集，涵盖127名驾驶员、136.6小时的真实驾驶数据，包括前视视频、车内视频、CAN总线信号、雷达交互和GPS路线信息。研究定义了三项基准任务，并评估了多种模型的表现。结果显示，仅靠视觉信息不足以准确预测控制交接，结合CAN和路线信息可显著提升性能，表明多模态数据具有互补性。此外，研究发现接管事件发展更缓慢，需要更长的预测时间，而交接事件则依赖即时上下文线索，这对辅助驾驶系统的HMI设计有重要影响。

item.title: 视频深度后处理框架VDPP提升实时边缘部署性能
item.content:encoded:
VDPP提升视频深度估计的效率与准确性。



摘要： 本文提出VDPP（Video Depth Post-Processing）框架，旨在通过几何优化提升视频深度估计的后处理方法。与传统端到端模型相比，VDPP在保持时间一致性的同时，实现了更高的速度（超过43.5 FPS），并采用无RGB依赖的设计，提高了可扩展性。该方法专注于低分辨率空间的几何细化，而非全场景重建，从而在准确性和效率之间取得更好的平衡，适用于实时边缘计算场景。

item.title: 基于数字孪生的多智能体在线学习框架TwinLoop
item.content:encoded:
TwinLoop提升多智能体系统在环境变化后的适应效率。



摘要： 本文提出TwinLoop，一个结合数字孪生的在线多智能体强化学习框架。当运行环境发生变化时，该框架通过数字孪生技术重建系统状态，利用模拟分析快速优化策略，并将更新参数同步回物理系统，从而减少对昂贵在线试错的依赖。实验表明，TwinLoop能有效提高系统在动态条件下的适应效率。

item.title: 基于视觉-语言模型的个性化MRI成像框架PASS
item.content:encoded:
PASS提升MRI成像效率与诊断准确性。



摘要： 本文提出PASS框架，通过结合视觉-语言模型（VLM）与深度去卷积网络，实现个性化的MRI快速成像。该框架通过三个核心贡献优化成像过程：基于物理模型的深度重建网络、患者特定的k空间轨迹采样模块，以及基于VLM的异常感知先验。PASS在多种解剖结构和对比度下均表现出色，显著提升了后续诊断任务的性能，包括细粒度异常检测与定位。

item.title: 基于高分辨率功率测量的AI数据中心能耗分析方法
item.content:encoded:
研究提出一种AI数据中心能耗分析方法，提升能效规划准确性。



摘要： 本文针对生成式AI迅速发展带来的计算需求激增问题，提出了一种将高分辨率工作负载功率测量与整体设施能耗需求相连接的方法。通过NLR高性能计算数据中心的实测数据，结合MLCommons和vLLM基准测试，获取了AI训练、微调和推理任务在0.1秒精度下的功率消耗数据，并构建了可公开访问的功率轮廓数据集。利用自下而上的事件驱动模型，将这些数据扩展到整个设施层面，生成反映真实时间波动的能耗曲线，为电网接入、本地能源发电和分布式微电网规划提供依据。

item.title: FedDetox：在资源受限边缘设备上提升联邦学习模型安全性的框架
item.content:encoded:
FedDetox提升联邦学习模型安全性，防止数据污染。



摘要： 随着高质量公共数据的减少，联邦学习（FL）成为保护隐私的同时利用用户数据的重要途径。然而，真实世界中的客户端数据可能包含有害信息，导致无意的数据污染，影响全局模型的安全对齐。为此，研究提出FedDetox，一个专为资源受限边缘设备上的小型语言模型设计的稳健框架。该框架通过知识蒸馏将大型安全对齐教师模型的安全能力转移到轻量级学生分类器中，并在联邦学习过程中，客户端识别不安全样本并替换为拒绝模板，从而将潜在毒药转化为积极的安全信号。实验表明，该方法在保持模型安全性的同时，不影响通用性能。

item.title: Elastic Test-Time Training与Fast Spatial Memory提升4D重建性能
item.content:encoded:
Elastic Test-Time Training提升长序列3D/4D重建稳定性与适应性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于弹性权重整合的测试时训练方法（LaCT），以解决其在长序列3D重建中因灾难性遗忘和过拟合导致的性能问题。通过引入锚定状态和指数移动平均机制，该方法增强了模型的稳定性和可塑性。在此基础上，研究者开发了Fast Spatial Memory（FSM），一种高效的4D重建模型，能够从长观察序列中学习时空表示，并生成新颖视角和时间组合。FSM在大规模3D/4D数据集上进行预训练，提升了对复杂空间环境动态和语义的捕捉能力。实验表明，FSM能够在更小的块上快速适应，减少相机插值捷径的影响，为实现多块适应和真正长序列的泛化提供了重要步骤。

item.title: 基于半监督学习的像素级标签优化方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种改进的半监督学习方法提升图像分割性能。



摘要： 在语义分割中，生成像素级标签的成本较高。为解决这一问题，研究提出一种改进的半监督学习方法，通过将标记图像的类别标签及其对应区域粘贴到未标记图像及其伪标签图像上，并训练模型使未标记图像的预测结果更接近标记图像。实验表明，该方法在Chase和COVID-19数据集上的mIoU平均提升了2.07%。

item.title: KITE：一种无需训练的机器人执行视频处理框架
item.content:encoded:
KITE将长视频转化为可解释的视觉语言模型输入，提升机器人故障分析效果。



摘要： KITE是一种无需训练的前端框架，能够将机器人执行的长视频转换为紧凑且可解释的tokenized证据，供视觉语言模型使用。它通过提取关键帧和鸟瞰图表示，结合场景上下文信息，生成统一提示，支持故障检测、识别、定位、解释和修正。在RoboFAC基准测试中，KITE显著优于原始模型，尤其在仿真故障分析方面表现突出，同时在真实双臂机器人上也展示了良好的应用前景。

item.title: DeSOPE数据集：用于6自由度变形物体的基准
item.content:encoded:
DeSOPE数据集用于研究变形物体的6D位姿估计。



摘要： 本文介绍了DeSOPE数据集，这是一个用于6自由度变形物体位姿估计的大规模数据集。大多数6D物体位姿方法假设物体是刚性或关节式的，但在实际中，物体可能因磨损、撞击或变形而偏离其原始形状。DeSOPE包含26种常见物体类别的高保真3D扫描，每种物体在一种标准状态和三种变形状态下采集，并与标准网格进行精确3D配准。此外，该数据集还包含133,000帧RGB-D图像和665,000个姿态标注，通过半自动流程生成。研究发现，随着变形程度增加，位姿估计性能显著下降，表明对变形的鲁棒处理对实际应用至关重要。项目页面和数据集已公开。

item.title: 人机协作中上下文的动态管理与重构
item.content:encoded:
文章提出动态上下文管理方法，提升人机协作效率。



摘要： 文章探讨了在人机协作中，传统上下文处理方式存在结构单一、难以动态调整的问题。作者提出“混合主动性上下文”概念，将上下文视为可操作的结构化对象，允许根据任务需求进行动态组织和调整。通过实现Contextify系统并进行用户研究，验证了该方法在提升用户对上下文控制能力方面的有效性，并讨论了其对人机交互领域的影响。

item.title: 融合传统与深度学习的患者聚类方法研究
item.content:encoded:
传统与深度学习结合提升心衰患者聚类效果。



摘要： 本文探讨了在真实电子健康记录数据中，传统、混合和深度学习方法在心衰患者聚类中的有效性。研究发现，传统方法在处理表格型EHR数据时表现稳健，而深度学习方法因设计用于图像任务而效果有限。为解决这一问题，作者提出一种基于集成的深度聚类方法，通过多维度嵌入空间聚合聚类结果。该方法在14种不同聚类方法和多个患者队列中表现出最佳性能，强调了生物性别特异性聚类的重要性，并验证了结合传统与深度方法的优势。

item.title: 双语语言模型中的跨语言激活模式研究
item.content:encoded:
双语模型在共享词汇时表现出类似人类的跨语言激活现象。



摘要： 该研究探讨了双语语言模型在阅读过程中是否表现出与人类相似的跨语言激活模式。通过在不同词汇共享条件下训练荷兰语-英语Transformer模型，并使用心理语言学刺激进行评估，发现当词汇共享嵌入时，模型表现出促进效应，无论是同源词还是假同源词。分析表明，这种效应主要由词频驱动，而非形式与意义的一致性。只有当同源词共享嵌入时，模型才能再现双语者的典型模式。研究结果表明，双语语言模型部分捕捉到了跨语言激活效应，但其与人类处理的匹配程度取决于词汇重叠的编码方式。

item.title: 基于实例自适应的变分自编码器提升生成模型效率
item.content:encoded:
IA-VAE通过输入自适应参数调制提升生成模型性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为实例自适应变分自编码器（IA-VAE）的新方法，通过超网络生成输入相关的编码器调制，实现输入特定的推理模型适应，同时保持单次前向传播的高效性。该方法在合成数据和标准图像基准测试中表现出色，能够显著减少后验近似误差，并提升ELBO指标，表明其在提升生成模型效率方面具有潜力。

item.title: 面向监控系统的物理对抗攻击研究
item.content:encoded:
研究物理对抗攻击在监控系统中的影响与挑战。



摘要： 本文从监控系统角度出发，探讨了物理对抗攻击的实际影响。不同于传统图像基准测试，文章强调了时间持续性、感知模态、载体真实性和系统级目标等关键问题。通过四类分类框架，分析了多目标跟踪、双模态可见-红外逃避以及可控服装等最新成果，揭示了该领域的发展趋势。同时，文章总结了评估方法和未解难题，如距离鲁棒性、摄像头管道差异、身份级指标和激活感知测试等，指出监控系统的安全性需从系统层面综合考量。

item.title: 大型语言模型在随机采样中的可靠性问题研究
item.content:encoded:
LLM作为代理在随机采样中存在显著缺陷。



摘要： 本文指出，可靠的随机采样是大型语言模型（LLMs）作为智能体运行的基本要求，但目前尚未得到满足。虽然标准的强化学习代理依赖外部采样机制，但LLMs无法将内部概率估计准确映射到随机输出。通过跨多种模型家族、规模、提示风格和分布的实证分析，研究揭示了这一失败现象的广泛性。关键发现是，尽管先进模型能够根据给定的随机种子生成目标分布，但它们直接从特定分布中采样的能力存在根本性缺陷。

item.title: 基于条件独立性的混合比例估计方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出基于条件独立性的混合比例估计方法，提升弱监督学习性能。



摘要： 本文研究了混合比例估计（MPE）问题，旨在从无标签数据中估计类别先验。传统方法依赖不可约性假设，而本文引入基于类标签的条件独立性假设，确保在不可约性不成立时仍可识别。在此基础上，提出了矩方法估计器，并分析其渐近性质。此外，还设计了弱监督核检验来验证条件独立性假设，适用于因果发现和公平性评估等场景。实验表明，该方法优于现有方法，且能有效控制两类错误。

item.title: 基于视觉语言模型的甲骨文解码框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新框架用于更精确地解码甲骨文。



摘要： 本文研究了甲骨文的解码问题，指出传统方法将其视为封闭集图像识别任务存在局限。作者提出一种基于视觉语言模型的代理驱动框架，结合视觉定位与语言推理，实现组件识别、知识检索和关系推断。同时构建了OB-Radix数据集，包含1022个字符图像和1853个组件图像，支持更精准的解码。实验表明该框架在多个基准测试中优于现有方法。

item.title: MoE语言模型在推理任务中的效率与性能评估
item.content:encoded:
MoE模型在推理任务中表现优异，但需结合提示策略和任务特性综合评估。



摘要： 本文对七种最新的推理导向指令调优模型进行了系统评估，涵盖密集模型和MoE模型。实验在四个基准测试中进行，采用零提示、思维链和少量示例思维链三种提示策略。研究发现，Gemma-4-E4B在多数任务中表现最佳，而Phi模型在TruthfulQA上表现突出，GSM8K对提示策略敏感度最高。结果表明，稀疏激活本身并不保证最优性能，实际效果受架构、提示方式和任务类型共同影响。研究还提供了可复现的评估流程和统计分析，支持在真实资源限制下的模型部署评估。

item.title: 基于生成模型的光栅拼贴创作方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新的光栅拼贴生成方法，提升结构一致性和多样性。



摘要： 本文提出了首个基于生成模型的光栅拼贴创作方法。传统方法依赖大量图像和颜色匹配，限制了多样性和结构一致性。新框架通过扩散模型生成拼贴块图像，并在参考图像条件下进行合成。低频条件扩散机制在保持全局结构的同时，保留提示驱动的细节。该方法实现了语义丰富且结构连贯的光栅拼贴，克服了传统匹配方法的局限性。通过少样本个性化扩散，模型能够在不依赖大量图像的情况下生成用户特定或风格一致的拼贴块。

item.title: $k$-server-bench: Automating Potential Discovery for the $k$-Server Conjecture
item.content:encoded: We introduce a code-based challenge for automated, open-ended mathematical discovery based on the $k$-server conjecture, a central open problem in competitive analysis. The task is to discover a potential function satisfying a large graph-structured system of simple linear inequalities. The resulting evaluation procedure is sound but incomplete: any violated inequality definitively refutes a candidate, whereas satisfying all inequalities does not by itself constitute a proof of the corresponding conjecture's special case. Nevertheless, a candidate that passes all constraints would be strong evidence toward a valid proof and, to the best of our knowledge, no currently known potential achieves this under our formulation in the open $k=4$ circle case. As such, a successful candidate would already be an interesting contribution to the $k$-server conjecture, and could become a substantial theoretical result when paired with a full proof. Experiments on the resolved $k=3$ regime show that current agentic methods can solve nontrivial instances, and in the open $k=4$ regime they reduce the number of violations relative to existing potentials without fully resolving the task. Taken together, these results suggest that the task is challenging but plausibly within reach of current methods. Beyond its relevance to the $k$-server community, where the developed tooling enables researchers to test new hypotheses and potentially improve on the current record, the task also serves as a useful \emph{benchmark} for developing code-based discovery agents. In particular, our $k=3$ results show that it mitigates important limitations of existing open-ended code-based benchmarks, including early saturation and the weak separation between naive random baselines and more sophisticated methods.

item.title: SBBTS: A Unified Schrödinger-Bass Framework for Synthetic Financial Time Series
item.content:encoded: We study the problem of generating synthetic time series that reproduce both marginal distributions and temporal dynamics, a central challenge in financial machine learning. Existing approaches typically fail to jointly model drift and stochastic volatility, as diffusion-based methods fix the volatility while martingale transport models ignore drift. We introduce the Schrödinger-Bass Bridge for Time Series (SBBTS), a unified framework that extends the Schrödinger-Bass formulation to multi-step time series. The method constructs a diffusion process that jointly calibrates drift and volatility and admits a tractable decomposition into conditional transport problems, enabling efficient learning. Numerical experiments on the Heston model demonstrate that SBBTS accurately recovers stochastic volatility and correlation parameters that prior SchrödingerBridge methods fail to capture. Applied to S&P 500 data, SBBTS-generated synthetic time series consistently improve downstream forecasting performance when used for data augmentation, yielding higher classification accuracy and Sharpe ratio compared to real-data-only training. These results show that SBBTS provides a practical and effective framework for realistic time series generation and data augmentation in financial applications.

item.title: STQuant: Spatio-Temporal Adaptive Framework for Optimizer Quantization in Large Multimodal Model Training
item.content:encoded: Quantization is an effective way to reduce the memory cost of large-scale model training. However, most existing methods adopt fixed-precision policies, which ignore the fact that optimizer-state distributions vary significantly across layers and training steps. Such uniform designs often introduce noticeable accuracy degradation. To move beyond fixed quantization, we propose STQuant, a distributed training framework that reduces the memory footprint of optimizer states via dynamic precision allocation across layers, state variables, and training steps, while maintaining model quality. Naively applying dynamic quantization during training is challenging for two reasons. First, optimizer states are numerically sensitive, and quantization noise can destabilize quality. Second, jointly considering multiple states and layers induces a large combinatorial search space. STQuant addresses these challenges with two key techniques: 1) a provably near-optimal factor selection strategy that accurately identifies the most influential factors for precision adaptation. 2) a dynamic transition decision algorithm that reduces the search cost from exponential to linear complexity. Experiments on GPT-2 and ViT show that STQuant reduces optimizer-state memory by 84.4%, achieving an average bit-width of as low as 5.1 bits, compared with existing solutions. Moreover, STQuant incurs only O(N/K) computational overhead and requires O(1) extra space.

item.title: Mem3R: Streaming 3D Reconstruction with Hybrid Memory via Test-Time Training
item.content:encoded: Streaming 3D perception is well suited to robotics and augmented reality, where long visual streams must be processed efficiently and consistently. Recent recurrent models offer a promising solution by maintaining fixed-size states and enabling linear-time inference, but they often suffer from drift accumulation and temporal forgetting over long sequences due to the limited capacity of compressed latent memories. We propose Mem3R, a streaming 3D reconstruction model with a hybrid memory design that decouples camera tracking from geometric mapping to improve temporal consistency over long sequences. For camera tracking, Mem3R employs an implicit fast-weight memory implemented as a lightweight Multi-Layer Perceptron updated via Test-Time Training. For geometric mapping, Mem3R maintains an explicit token-based fixed-size state. Compared with CUT3R, this design not only significantly improves long-sequence performance but also reduces the model size from 793M to 644M parameters. Mem3R supports existing improved plug-and-play state update strategies developed for CUT3R. Specifically, integrating it with TTT3R decreases Absolute Trajectory Error by up to 39% over the base implementation on 500 to 1000 frame sequences. The resulting improvements also extend to other downstream tasks, including video depth estimation and 3D reconstruction, while preserving constant GPU memory usage and comparable inference throughput. Project page: https://lck666666.github.io/Mem3R/

item.title: When Is Thinking Enough? Early Exit via Sufficiency Assessment for Efficient Reasoning
item.content:encoded: Large reasoning models (LRMs) have achieved remarkable performance in complex reasoning tasks, driven by their powerful inference-time scaling capability. However, LRMs often suffer from overthinking, which results in substantial computational redundancy and significantly reduces efficiency. Early-exit methods aim to mitigate this issue by terminating reasoning once sufficient evidence has been generated, yet existing approaches mostly rely on handcrafted or empirical indicators that are unreliable and impractical. In this work, we introduce Dynamic Thought Sufficiency in Reasoning (DTSR), a novel framework for efficient reasoning that enables the model to dynamically assess the sufficiency of its chain-of-thought (CoT) and determine the optimal point for early exit. Inspired by human metacognition, DTSR operates in two stages: (1) Reflection Signal Monitoring, which identifies reflection signals as potential cues for early exit, and (2) Thought Sufficiency Check, which evaluates whether the current CoT is sufficient to derive the final answer. Experimental results on the Qwen3 models show that DTSR reduces reasoning length by 28.9%-34.9% with minimal performance loss, effectively mitigating overthinking. We further discuss overconfidence in LRMs and self-evaluation paradigms, providing valuable insights for early-exit reasoning.

item.title: LaScA: Language-Conditioned Scalable Modelling of Affective Dynamics
item.content:encoded: Predicting affect in unconstrained environments remains a fundamental challenge in human-centered AI. While deep neural embeddings dominate contemporary approaches, they often lack interpretability and limit expert-driven refinement. We propose a novel framework that uses Language Models (LMs) as semantic context conditioners over handcrafted affect descriptors to model changes in Valence and Arousal. Our approach begins with interpretable facial geometry and acoustic features derived from structured domain knowledge. These features are transformed into symbolic natural-language descriptions encoding their affective implications. A pretrained LM processes these descriptions to generate semantic context embeddings that act as high-level priors over affective dynamics. Unlike end-to-end black-box pipelines, our framework preserves feature transparency while leveraging the contextual abstraction capabilities of LMs. We evaluate the proposed method on the Aff-Wild2 and SEWA datasets for affect change prediction. Experimental results show consistent improvements in accuracy for both Valence and Arousal compared to handcrafted-only and deep-embedding baselines. Our findings demonstrate that semantic conditioning enables interpretable affect modelling without sacrificing predictive performance, offering a transparent and computationally efficient alternative to fully end-to-end architectures

item.title: FlowAdam: Implicit Regularization via Geometry-Aware Soft Momentum Injection
item.content:encoded: Adaptive moment methods such as Adam use a diagonal, coordinate-wise preconditioner based on exponential moving averages of squared gradients. This diagonal scaling is coordinate-system dependent and can struggle with dense or rotated parameter couplings, including those in matrix factorization, tensor decomposition, and graph neural networks, because it treats each parameter independently. We introduce FlowAdam, a hybrid optimizer that augments Adam with continuous gradient-flow integration via an ordinary differential equation (ODE). When EMA-based statistics detect landscape difficulty, FlowAdam switches to clipped ODE integration. Our central contribution is Soft Momentum Injection, which blends ODE velocity with Adam's momentum during mode transitions. This prevents the training collapse observed with naive hybrid approaches. Across coupled optimization benchmarks, the ODE integration provides implicit regularization, reducing held-out error by 10-22% on low-rank matrix/tensor recovery and 6% on Jester (real-world collaborative filtering), also surpassing tuned Lion and AdaBelief, while matching Adam on well-conditioned workloads (CIFAR-10). MovieLens-100K confirms benefits arise specifically from coupled parameter interactions rather than bias estimation. Ablation studies show that soft injection is essential, as hard replacement reduces accuracy from 100% to 82.5%.

item.title: 4D Vessel Reconstruction for Benchtop Thrombectomy Analysis
item.content:encoded: Introduction: Mechanical thrombectomy can cause vessel deformation and procedure-related injury. Benchtop models are widely used for device testing, but time-resolved, full-field 3D vessel-motion measurements remain limited. Methods: We developed a nine-camera, low-cost multi-view workflow for benchtop thrombectomy in silicone middle cerebral artery phantoms (2160p, 20 fps). Multi-view videos were calibrated, segmented, and reconstructed with 4D Gaussian Splatting. Reconstructed point clouds were converted to fixed-connectivity edge graphs for region-of-interest (ROI) displacement tracking and a relative surface-based stress proxy. Stress-proxy values were derived from edge stretch using a Neo-Hookean mapping and reported as comparative surface metrics. A synthetic Blender pipeline with known deformation provided geometric and temporal validation. Results: In synthetic bulk translation, the stress proxy remained near zero for most edges (median $\approx$ 0 MPa; 90th percentile 0.028 MPa), with sparse outliers. In synthetic pulling (1-5 mm), reconstruction showed close geometric and temporal agreement with ground truth, with symmetric Chamfer distance of 1.714-1.815 mm and precision of 0.964-0.972 at $τ= 1$ mm. In preliminary benchtop comparative trials (one trial per condition), cervical aspiration catheter placement showed higher max-median ROI displacement and stress-proxy values than internal carotid artery terminus placement. Conclusion: The proposed protocol provides standardized, time-resolved surface kinematics and comparative relative displacement and stress proxy measurements for thrombectomy benchtop studies. The framework supports condition-to-condition comparisons and methods validation, while remaining distinct from absolute wall-stress estimation. Implementation code and example data are available at https://ethanuser.github.io/vessel4D

item.title: Luwen Technical Report
item.content:encoded: Large language models have demonstrated remarkable capabilities across a wide range of natural language processing tasks, yet their application in the legal domain remains challenging due to the specialized terminology, complex reasoning requirements, and rapidly evolving legal knowledge involved. In this paper, we present Luwen, an open-source Chinese legal language model built upon the Baichuan foundation model through three key techniques: continual pre-training on a large-scale legal corpus, supervised fine-tuning with carefully curated legal instruction data, and retrieval-augmented generation integrated with a comprehensive legal knowledge base. We evaluate Luwen on five representative legal tasks spanning both prediction and generation settings, including legal judgment prediction, judicial examination, legal text summarization, law article question answering, and judicial decision reasoning. Experimental results show that Luwen outperforms several strong baselines, demonstrating the effectiveness of our approach in adapting general-purpose language models to the legal domain.

item.title: ClickGuard: A Trustworthy Adaptive Fusion Framework for Clickbait Detection
item.content:encoded: The widespread use of clickbait headlines, crafted to mislead and maximize engagement, poses a significant challenge to online credibility. These headlines employ sensationalism, misleading claims, and vague language, underscoring the need for effective detection to ensure trustworthy digital content. The paper introduces, ClickGuard: a trustworthy adaptive fusion framework for clickbait detection. It combines BERT embeddings and structural features using a Syntactic-Semantic Adaptive Fusion Block (SSAFB) for dynamic integration. The framework incorporates a hybrid CNN-BiLSTM to capture patterns and dependencies. The model achieved 96.93% testing accuracy, outperforming state-of-the-art approaches. The model's trustworthiness is evaluated using LIME and Permutation Feature Importance (PFI) for interpretability and perturbation analysis. These methods assess the model's robustness and sensitivity to feature changes by measuring the average prediction variation. Ablation studies validated the SSAFB's effectiveness in optimizing feature fusion. The model demonstrated robust performance across diverse datasets, providing a scalable, reliable solution for enhancing online content credibility by addressing syntactic-semantic modelling challenges. Code of the work is available at: https://github.com/palindromeRice/ClickBait_Detection_Architecture

item.title: A Noise Constrained Diffusion (NC-Diffusion) Framework for High Fidelity Image Compression
item.content:encoded: With the great success of diffusion models in image generation, diffusion-based image compression is attracting increasing interests. However, due to the random noise introduced in the diffusion learning, they usually produce reconstructions with deviation from the original images, leading to suboptimal compression results. To address this problem, in this paper, we propose a Noise Constrained Diffusion (NC-Diffusion) framework for high fidelity image compression. Unlike existing diffusion-based compression methods that add random Gaussian noise and direct the noise into the image space, the proposed NC-Diffusion formulates the quantization noise originally added in the learned image compression as the noise in the forward process of diffusion. Then a noise constrained diffusion process is constructed from the ground-truth image to the initial compression result generated with quantization noise. The NC-Diffusion overcomes the problem of noise mismatch between compression and diffusion, significantly improving the inference efficiency. In addition, an adaptive frequency-domain filtering module is developed to enhance the skip connections in the U-Net based diffusion architecture, in order to enhance high-frequency details. Moreover, a zero-shot sample-guided enhancement method is designed to further improve the fidelity of the image. Experiments on multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate that our method can achieve the best performance compared with existing methods.

item.title: Beyond the Mean: Modelling Annotation Distributions in Continuous Affect Prediction
item.content:encoded: Emotion annotation is inherently subjective and cognitively demanding, producing signals that reflect diverse perceptions across annotators rather than a single ground truth. In continuous affect prediction, this variability is typically collapsed into point estimates such as the mean or median, discarding valuable information about annotator disagreement and uncertainty. In this work, we propose a distribution-aware framework that models annotation consensus using the Beta distribution. Instead of predicting a single affect value, models estimate the mean and standard deviation of the annotation distribution, which are transformed into valid Beta parameters through moment matching. This formulation enables the recovery of higher-order distributional descriptors, including skewness, kurtosis, and quantiles, in closed form. As a result, the model captures not only the central tendency of emotional perception but also variability, asymmetry, and uncertainty in annotator responses. We evaluate the proposed approach on the SEWA and RECOLA datasets using multimodal features. Experimental results show that Beta-based modelling produces predictive distributions that closely match the empirical annotator distributions while achieving competitive performance with conventional regression approaches. These findings highlight the importance of modelling annotation uncertainty in affective computing and demonstrate the potential of distribution-aware learning for subjective signal analysis.

item.title: TC-AE: Unlocking Token Capacity for Deep Compression Autoencoders
item.content:encoded: We propose TC-AE, a ViT-based architecture for deep compression autoencoders. Existing methods commonly increase the channel number of latent representations to maintain reconstruction quality under high compression ratios. However, this strategy often leads to latent representation collapse, which degrades generative performance. Instead of relying on increasingly complex architectures or multi-stage training schemes, TC-AE addresses this challenge from the perspective of the token space, the key bridge between pixels and image latents, through two complementary innovations: Firstly, we study token number scaling by adjusting the patch size in ViT under a fixed latent budget, and identify aggressive token-to-latent compression as the key factor that limits effective scaling. To address this issue, we decompose token-to-latent compression into two stages, reducing structural information loss and enabling effective token number scaling for generation. Secondly, to further mitigate latent representation collapse, we enhance the semantic structure of image tokens via joint self-supervised training, leading to more generative-friendly latents. With these designs, TC-AE achieves substantially improved reconstruction and generative performance under deep compression. We hope our research will advance ViT-based tokenizer for visual generation.

item.title: Discourse Coherence and Response-Guided Context Rewriting for Multi-Party Dialogue Generation
item.content:encoded: Previous research on multi-party dialogue generation has predominantly leveraged structural information inherent in dialogues to directly inform the generation process. However, the prevalence of colloquial expressions and incomplete utterances in dialogues often impedes comprehension and weakens the fidelity of dialogue structure representations, which is particularly pronounced in multi-party dialogues. In this work, we propose a novel framework DRCR (Discourse coherence and Response-guided Context Rewriting) to improve multi-party dialogue generation through dialogue context rewriting. Specifically, DRCR employs two complementary feedback signals, discourse coherence and response quality, to construct preference data for both context rewriting and response generation. Moreover, we propose a dynamic self-evolution learning method that allows the rewriter and responder to continuously enhance their capabilities through mutual interaction in an iterative training loop. Comprehensive experiments conducted on four multi-party dialogue datasets substantiate the effectiveness of DRCR.

item.title: Joint Optimization of Reasoning and Dual-Memory for Self-Learning Diagnostic Agent
item.content:encoded: Clinical expertise improves not only by acquiring medical knowledge, but by accumulating experience that yields reusable diagnostic patterns. Recent LLMs-based diagnostic agents have shown promising progress in clinical reasoning for decision support. However, most approaches treat cases independently, limiting experience reuse and continual adaptation. We propose SEA, a self-learning diagnostic agent with cognitively inspired dual-memory module. We design a reinforcement training framework tailored to our designed agent for joint optimization of reasoning and memory management. We evaluate SEA in two complementary settings. On standard evaluation with MedCaseReasoning dataset, SEA achieves 92.46% accuracy, outperforming the strongest baseline by +19.6%, demonstrating the benefit of jointly optimizing reasoning and memory. On the long-horizon with ER-Reason dataset, SEA attains the best final accuracy (0.7214) and the largest improvement (+0.35 Acc@100), while baseline methods show limited or unstable gains. Expert evaluation further indicates that rules consolidated from SEA show strong clinical correctness, usefulness and trust, suggesting that the induced rules in dual-memory module are reliable and practically meaningful. Overall, SEA improves both diagnostic reasoning ability and continual learning by effectively transforming experience into reusable knowledge.

item.title: CASE: Cadence-Aware Set Encoding for Large-Scale Next Basket Repurchase Recommendation
item.content:encoded: Repurchase behavior is a primary signal in large-scale retail recommendation, particularly in categories with frequent replenishment: many items in a user's next basket were previously purchased and their timing follows stable, item-specific cadences. Yet most next basket repurchase recommendation models represent history as a sequence of discrete basket events indexed by visit order, which cannot explicitly model elapsed calendar time or update item rankings as days pass between purchases. We present CASE (Cadence-Aware Set Encoding for next basket repurchase recommendation), which decouples item-level cadence learning from cross-item interaction, enabling explicit calendar-time modeling while remaining production-scalable. CASE represents each item's purchase history as a calendar-time signal over a fixed horizon, applies shared multi-scale temporal convolutions to capture recurring rhythms, and uses induced set attention to model cross-item dependencies with sub-quadratic complexity, allowing efficient batch inference at scale. Across three public benchmarks and a proprietary dataset, CASE consistently improves Precision, Recall, and NDCG at multiple cutoffs compared to strong next basket prediction baselines. In a production-scale evaluation with tens of millions of users and a large item catalog, CASE achieves up to 8.6% relative Precision and 9.9% Recall lift at top-5, demonstrating that scalable cadence-aware modeling yields measurable gains in both benchmark and industrial settings.

item.title: Graph Neural ODE Digital Twins for Control-Oriented Reactor Thermal-Hydraulic Forecasting Under Partial Observability
item.content:encoded: Real-time supervisory control of advanced reactors requires accurate forecasting of plant-wide thermal-hydraulic states, including locations where physical sensors are unavailable. Meeting this need calls for surrogate models that combine predictive fidelity, millisecond-scale inference, and robustness to partial observability. In this work, we present a physics-informed message-passing Graph Neural Network coupled with a Neural Ordinary Differential Equation (GNN-ODE) to addresses all three requirements simultaneously. We represent the whole system as a directed sensor graph whose edges encode hydraulic connectivity through flow/heat transfer-aware message passing, and we advance the latent dynamics in continuous time via a controlled Neural ODE. A topology-guided missing-node initializer reconstructs uninstrumented states at rollout start; prediction then proceeds fully autoregressively. The GNN-ODE surrogate achieves satisfactory results for the system dynamics prediction. On held-out simulation transients, the surrogate achieves an average MAE of 0.91 K at 60 s and 2.18 K at 300 s for uninstrumented nodes, with $R^2$ up to 0.995 for missing-node state reconstruction. Inference runs at approximately 105 times faster than simulated time on a single GPU, enabling 64-member ensemble rollouts for uncertainty quantification. To assess sim-to-real transfer, we adapt the pretrained surrogate to experimental facility data using layerwise discriminative fine-tuning with only 30 training sequences. The learned flow-dependent heat-transfer scaling recovers a Reynolds-number exponent consistent with established correlations, indicating constitutive learning beyond trajectory fitting. The model tracks a steep power change transient and produces accurate trajectories at uninstrumented locations.

item.title: Syntax Is Easy, Semantics Is Hard: Evaluating LLMs for LTL Translation
item.content:encoded: Propositional Linear Temporal Logic (LTL) is a popular formalism for specifying desirable requirements and security and privacy policies for software, networks, and systems. Yet expressing such requirements and policies in LTL remains challenging because of its intricate semantics. Since many security and privacy analysis tools require LTL formulas as input, this difficulty places them out of reach for many developers and analysts. Large Language Models (LLMs) could broaden access to such tools by translating natural language fragments into LTL formulas. This paper evaluates that premise by assessing how effectively several representative LLMs translate assertive English sentences into LTL formulas. Using both human-generated and synthetic ground-truth data, we evaluate effectiveness along syntactic and semantic dimensions. The results reveal three findings: (1) in line with prior findings, LLMs perform better on syntactic aspects of LTL than on semantic ones; (2) they generally benefit from more detailed prompts; and (3) reformulating the task as a Python code-completion problem substantially improves overall performance. We also discuss challenges in conducting a fair evaluation on this task and conclude with recommendations for future work.

item.title: Reasoning Fails Where Step Flow Breaks
item.content:encoded: Large reasoning models (LRMs) that generate long chains of thought now perform well on multi-step math, science, and coding tasks. However, their behavior is still unstable and hard to interpret, and existing analysis tools struggle with such long, structured reasoning traces. We introduce Step-Saliency, which pools attention--gradient scores into step-to-step maps along the question--thinking--summary trajectory. Across several models, Step-Saliency reveals two recurring information-flow failures: Shallow Lock-in, where shallow layers over-focus on the current step and barely use earlier context, and Deep Decay, where deep layers gradually lose saliency on the thinking segment and the summary increasingly attends to itself and the last few steps. Motivated by these patterns, we propose StepFlow, a saliency-inspired test-time intervention that adjusts shallow saliency patterns measured by Step-Saliency via Odds-Equal Bridge and adds a small step-level residual in deep layers via Step Momentum Injection. StepFlow improves accuracy on math, science, and coding tasks across multiple LRMs without retraining, indicating that repairing information flow can recover part of their missing reasoning performance.

item.title: iTAG: Inverse Design for Natural Text Generation with Accurate Causal Graph Annotations
item.content:encoded: A fundamental obstacle to causal discovery from text is the lack of causally annotated text data for use as ground truth, due to high annotation costs. This motivates an important task of generating text with causal graph annotations. Early template-based generation methods sacrifice text naturalness in exchange for high causal graph annotation accuracy. Recent Large Language Model (LLM)-dependent methods directly generate natural text from target graphs through LLMs, but do not guarantee causal graph annotation accuracy. Therefore, we propose iTAG, which performs real-world concept assignment to nodes before converting causal graphs into text in existing LLM-dependent methods. iTAG frames this process as an inverse problem with the causal graph as the target, iteratively examining and refining concept selection through Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning so that the induced relations between concepts are as consistent as possible with the target causal relationships described by the causal graph. iTAG demonstrates both extremely high annotation accuracy and naturalness across extensive tests, and the results of testing text-based causal discovery algorithms with the generated data show high statistical correlation with real-world data. This suggests that iTAG-generated data can serve as a practical surrogate for scalable benchmarking of text-based causal discovery algorithms.

item.title: THIVLVC：基于检索和大语言模型的拉丁语依存句法解析系统
item.content:encoded:
THIVLVC通过检索和大模型提升拉丁语依存句法解析准确率。



摘要： 本文介绍了THIVLVC系统，用于EvaLatin 2026依存句法解析任务。该系统分为两阶段：首先根据句子长度和词性n-gram相似度从CIRCSE语料库中检索结构相似的句子，然后利用大语言模型结合UD标注规范对UDPipe的基线解析结果进行优化。系统提交了两种配置：一种不使用检索，另一种使用检索（RAG）。在诗歌（Seneca）数据上，THIVLVC相比UDPipe基线提升了17个CLAS分数；在散文（Thomas Aquinas）数据上，提升了1.5个CLAS。对300个分歧点的双盲分析显示，53.3%的标注一致情况下，THIVLVC更受认可，揭示了不同语料库间的标注不一致性。

item.title: 基于大模型的工业系统架构安全评估方法研究
item.content:encoded:
ASTRAL通过大模型实现不完整系统架构的安全评估。



摘要： 本文针对工业控制系统中因文档缺失或过时导致的安全评估困难问题，提出了一种基于大模型的架构中心安全威胁风险评估方法ASTRAL。该方法通过提示链、少量样本学习和架构推理，从分散数据源中提取并整合系统表示，支持动态威胁识别和量化风险评估。研究通过多个案例分析和专家评估验证了其有效性，结果表明ASTRAL能够提升网络安全风险管理决策的准确性。

item.title: JD-BP：一种联合竞价与定价的优化框架
item.content:encoded:
JD-BP提升广告竞价效率，增强收益与成本目标达成。



摘要： 本文提出JD-BP，一个联合生成决策框架用于竞价与定价优化。该框架通过输出竞价值和定价修正项，提升在KPI约束下的竞价策略效果。为减少历史约束违反的影响，设计了无记忆的Return-to-Go机制，并引入轨迹增强算法以提高部署效率。结合能量基直接偏好优化方法与交叉注意力模块，提升联合学习性能。实验表明，JD-BP在AuctionNet数据集上表现优异，实际测试中提升了广告收入和目标成本表现。

item.title: 基于本体知识的时序知识图谱外推方法
item.content:encoded:
利用本体知识提升时序知识图谱外推性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为OntoTKGE的框架，通过整合本体知识与时间知识，提升时序知识图谱（TKG）外推模型的性能。该方法利用本体知识中的概念层级关系，帮助实体继承其他同类实体的行为模式，从而缓解实体历史交互数据稀疏的问题。实验表明，OntoTKGE在多个数据集上显著提升了现有模型的性能，并优于多项先进方法。

item.title: 基于CVA架构的大型语言模型行为优化研究
item.content:encoded:
提升LLM行为真实性，缓解价值极化问题。



摘要： 本文探讨了大型语言模型（LLMs）在模拟人类行为时存在的行为僵化问题，并指出当前评估方法存在自我参照偏差。通过与真实数据对比，发现增强提示驱动推理反而加剧价值极化，导致群体多样性下降。为此，作者提出基于S-O-R模型和人类价值观理论的Context-Value-Action（CVA）架构，通过独立的价值验证器实现行为生成与认知推理的解耦，有效提升行为真实性与可解释性。实验结果表明，CVA在包含110万真实交互记录的CVABench数据集上优于现有基线方法。

item.title: AI机器身份治理的缺失与解决方案
item.content:encoded:
AI机器身份缺乏治理框架，导致重大安全风险。



摘要： 文章指出，AI系统使用的机器身份（如AI代理、API令牌等）在企业环境中数量远超人类身份，但目前缺乏统一的治理框架。这导致了严重的安全风险，例如2024年CrowdStrike事件中因未受监管的自动化代理造成54亿至100亿美元损失。文章提出四个原创贡献：AI-Identity Risk Taxonomy（AIRT）用于分类37种风险；Machine Identity Governance Taxonomy（MIGT）作为整合治理框架；针对国家行为者的威胁模型；以及跨司法管辖区的监管协调结构。最后，还提供了一个四阶段实施路线图，以推动企业实际应用。

item.title: Market-Bench：评估大语言模型经济能力的基准测试
item.content:encoded:
评估大语言模型在经济任务中的表现，揭示其资源管理能力差异。



摘要： 本文提出 Market-Bench，一个用于评估大语言模型（LLMs）在经济相关任务中表现的基准测试。通过构建多智能体供应链经济模型，LLMs 作为零售商代理参与采购和零售过程。在采购阶段，LLMs 在预算受限的拍卖中竞标库存；在零售阶段，LLMs 设定价格、生成营销标语，并通过角色注意力机制向买家提供。该基准记录完整的出价、价格、标语、销售和财务状态轨迹，支持经济、运营和语义指标的自动评估。对 20 个开源与闭源 LLM 代理的基准测试显示显著性能差异，仅少数模型能持续实现资本增值，多数则接近盈亏平衡点。

PRepair通过最小化修改提升代码修复精度。

item.title: 基于强化学习与大语言模型的交通信号控制框架CuraLight
item.content:encoded:
CuraLight提升交通信号控制效率，减少通行时间与排队长度。



摘要： 本文提出CuraLight，一个以大语言模型为中心的交通信号控制框架，结合强化学习进行环境探索并生成高质量交互轨迹，用于优化模型训练。通过多大语言模型的结构化辩论系统，进一步提升信号控制决策的准确性。实验在SUMO平台上进行，覆盖多个真实城市网络，结果表明CuraLight在平均通行时间、队列长度和等待时间方面均优于现有方法，显示出其在可扩展性和可解释性方面的优势。

item.title: 多轮对话中的语言模型校准方法研究
item.content:encoded:
研究多轮对话中模型置信度校准，提升交互可靠性。



摘要： 本文提出多轮校准任务，将模型置信度校准从静态属性转化为动态挑战。通过引入ECE@T指标评估多轮校准效果，发现用户反馈可能降低校准性能。为此，作者提出MTCal方法优化校准，并结合ConfChat策略提升多轮对话的准确性和一致性。实验表明，该方法在多轮场景中表现优异，有助于实现更安全、可靠的大型语言模型应用。

item.title: Character Error Vector：一种新的OCR评估方法
item.content:encoded:
提出CEV作为OCR质量评估的新方法，优于传统CER。



摘要： 本文介绍了Character Error Vector (CEV)，一种用于评估OCR质量的袋式字符评估方法。与传统的CER不同，CEV可以分解为解析、OCR和交互错误部分，从而帮助从业者更精准地定位影响文本提取质量的关键环节。研究展示了两种实现方式：SpACER和基于Jensen-Shannon距离的字符分布方法。通过与CER等其他指标的对比验证，CEV在页面级OCR评估中表现出色，尤其在处理复杂布局的档案报纸数据时，传统流程方法优于当前最先进的端到端模型。CEV需要字符级位置信息以达到最佳效果，但使用易获取的阈值即可预测主要错误来源，F1值达0.91。研究还提供了Python库以支持文档理解研究。

item.title: Paper Circle：基于多智能体的学术文献发现与分析系统
item.content:encoded:
Paper Circle 提供高效学术文献发现与分析工具。



摘要： 本文介绍了 Paper Circle，一个基于多智能体大型语言模型的学术文献发现与分析系统。该系统包含两个互补流程：发现流程整合了多源检索、多标准评分、多样性感知排序和结构化输出；分析流程将单篇论文转化为包含概念、方法、实验等类型节点的知识图谱，支持图感知问答和覆盖验证。系统在代码器 LLM 基础上实现，生成可复现的 JSON、CSV、BibTeX 等格式输出。文章描述了系统架构、代理角色、检索与评分方法、知识图谱模式及评估接口，并在论文检索与综述生成任务中进行了基准测试。

item.title: Action Images：基于可解释动作图像的统一世界动作模型
item.content:encoded:
提出Action Images，实现零样本策略学习与视频生成。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为Action Images的统一世界动作模型，将机器人控制转化为多视角视频生成任务。通过将7自由度机器人动作转化为基于像素的可解释动作图像，该模型无需单独策略头即可直接利用视频骨干网络进行策略学习。该方法不仅支持零样本策略执行，还能够进行视频-动作联合生成、动作条件视频生成和动作标注。实验表明，该模型在RLBench和真实世界任务中取得了最佳的零样本成功率，并提升了视频-动作联合生成的质量，验证了可解释动作图像在策略学习中的有效性。

item.title: 多步预测提升语言模型内部世界建模能力
item.content:encoded:
多步预测促进语言模型学习更结构化的表示，但存在结构幻觉问题。



摘要： 本文探讨了大型语言模型是否能够发展出连贯的内部世界模型，并分析了多步预测（MTP）在学习结构化表示方面的潜力。研究指出，MTP通过梯度耦合诱导表征收缩性，有助于模型收敛到内部信念状态，但标准MTP常出现结构幻觉，即离散标记监督导致潜在空间中违反环境约束的捷径。为此，作者提出LSE-MTP方法，通过锚定真实隐藏状态轨迹来改善预测。实验表明，该方法在合成图和曼哈顿出租车数据上有效提升了表征对齐度，减少了结构幻觉并增强了鲁棒性。

item.title: 基于外观分解的多遍历场景重建方法ADM-GS
item.content:encoded:
提出ADM-GS方法解决多遍历场景重建中的外观不一致问题。



摘要： 本文提出ADM-GS（Appearance Decomposition Gaussian Splatting for Multi-Traversal Reconstruction），一种用于多遍历场景重建的框架。该方法通过显式分解静态背景的外观，将外观分为与遍历无关的材质属性和与遍历相关的光照变化，以缓解不同遍历间的外观混淆。作者设计了一种神经光场，采用频率分离的混合编码策略，分别捕捉低频漫反射光照和高频镜面反射。在Argoverse 2和Waymo Open数据集上的定量评估表明，ADM-GS在多遍历实验中相比现有基线方法提升了0.98 dB的PSNR，并实现了更一致的外观表现。代码将在GitHub上公开。

item.title: 无标注皮肤病变分割框架BPC-Net的研究与应用
item.content:encoded:
提出BPC-Net解决无标注皮肤病变分割中的边界概率问题。



摘要： 本文针对无标注皮肤病变分割面临的三个挑战，提出BPC-Net框架，通过高斯概率平滑技术提升边界概率的准确性，无需人工标注即可实现高质量分割。该方法在ISIC-2017、ISIC-2018和PH2数据集上取得了最先进的无监督分割性能，接近有监督方法的效果。

item.title: 基于量子采样编码的高效量子机器学习方法
item.content:encoded:
SBQE提升量子机器学习效率，无需编码门即可实现高准确率。



摘要： 本文提出Shot-Based Quantum Encoding (SBQE) 方法，解决量子机器学习中数据加载效率低的问题。该方法通过将硬件资源shots按数据依赖的分布分配到多个初始量子态，构建混合态表示，其期望值与经典概率线性相关，可与非线性激活函数结合。实验表明，SBQE在Fashion MNIST和Semeion数据集上分别达到80.95%和89.1%的测试准确率，优于传统编码方式且无需数据编码门。

item.title: 基于神经符号方法的采购文件验证系统
item.content:encoded:
神经符号方法提升采购文件验证的可解释性与合规性。



摘要： 本文提出一种结合符号与非符号人工智能的神经符号方法，用于监管公共机构中采购文件的验证。通过语言模型提取信息，并利用逻辑张量网络进行可审计决策。该方法将领域知识与语言模型的语义理解相结合，使决策可通过谓词值、规则真值和对应文本片段进行解释，从而实现基于真实采购文档语料的规则检查。实验表明，该方法性能与现有模型相当，但具有更高的可解释性、模块化谓词提取和对可解释AI的支持。

item.title: 基于Σ-Voxfield的可扩展户外驾驶场景生成方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种3D生成框架，支持大规模户外场景生成与多视角渲染。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于Σ-Voxfield网格的3D生成框架，通过在每个占用体素中存储固定数量的着色表面样本，实现多视角一致的大规模户外场景生成。该方法利用语义条件扩散模型，在局部体素邻域内操作，并结合3D位置编码捕捉空间结构。通过渐进式空间扩展技术，实现对大范围区域的生成。最终通过延迟渲染模块生成逼真图像，无需逐场景优化。实验表明，该方法能够在合理计算成本下生成多样化的城市户外场景，并支持多种传感器配置和相机轨迹。

item.title: 提升扩散语言模型推理速度的自校正方法研究
item.content:encoded:
研究提出新框架提升扩散模型在少步采样中的性能。



摘要： 本文研究了连续扩散语言模型中自校正机制在少步采样时性能下降的问题，发现错误会随着去噪步骤累积，影响生成质量。为此，作者提出一种新的训练框架，通过扰动自校正信号来匹配推理噪声，增强对先前估计误差的鲁棒性。同时引入基于token的噪声感知机制，防止训练饱和，提升优化效果。实验表明该框架在条件生成任务中优于传统模型，并实现400倍的推理加速。

item.title: 探索MoE大模型中的领域专家及其应用
item.content:encoded:
研究发现MoE模型中存在领域专家，并提出DSMoE框架提升性能。



摘要： 在大型语言模型（LLM）时代，混合专家（MoE）架构被广泛用于提高计算效率。本文探讨了MoE模型中是否存在领域特定专家这一关键问题，并通过实验验证了其存在性。基于此，作者提出了Domain Steering Mixture of Experts（DSMoE）框架，在不增加推理成本的情况下，优于现有先进MoE模型和基线方法。实验结果表明，该方法在多个开源MoE模型上表现出色，具有良好的泛化能力，且无需额外训练。

item.title: 树决策图在布尔函数建模中的应用
item.content:encoded:
树决策图扩展了OBDD，具有更高效的表示和计算能力。



摘要： 文章介绍了树决策图（TDD）作为一种布尔函数的模型，它扩展了有序二叉决策图（OBDD）。TDD可以看作是结构化d-DNNF的一种限制形式，即遵循特定vtree的d-DNNF。研究表明，TDD在模型计数、枚举、条件化和应用等方面与OBDD具有相同的可处理性，并且更加简洁。特别是，文章指出，具有treewidth为k的CNF公式可以用FPT大小的TDD表示，而这是OBDD无法实现的。此外，研究还探讨了通过自底向上编译将CNF公式转换为确定性TDD的复杂性，并将其与Bova和Szeider提出的因子宽度概念相关联。

item.title: In-Place Test-Time Training：提升大语言模型持续学习能力的新框架
item.content:encoded:
In-Place TTT使大语言模型在推理时动态更新参数，提升适应新信息的能力。



摘要： 本文提出In-Place Test-Time Training（In-Place TTT），一种无需重新训练即可让大语言模型在推理时动态调整参数的框架。该方法利用MLP块的最终投影矩阵作为可变参数，实现高效、兼容的测试时训练。同时，通过针对自回归语言建模任务设计的目标函数，提升了模型在长上下文任务中的表现。实验表明，该框架在4B参数模型上表现出色，并优于现有TTT方法。研究为大语言模型的持续学习提供了新的方向。

item.title: 基于天气条件的多模态感知自适应路由3D目标检测方法
item.content:encoded:
提出一种自适应多模态融合方法提升恶劣天气下的3D目标检测性能。



摘要： 本文针对恶劣天气下3D目标检测的挑战，提出一种基于天气条件的多模态感知自适应路由框架。该框架通过维护三个并行的3D特征流（纯LiDAR、纯4D雷达和条件门控融合），结合轻量级路由器动态调整各模态权重，实现对环境变化的自适应响应。同时引入天气监督学习策略，防止分支坍塌并增强路由行为的多样性。实验表明，该方法在K-Radar基准上取得最佳性能，并提供可解释的模态偏好分析。

item.title: 指令调优模型的指令遵循机制研究
item.content:encoded:
指令调优模型依赖多样技能协调而非统一机制。



摘要： 本文研究了指令调优语言模型的指令遵循机制，通过九个不同任务的诊断探测发现，指令遵循并非基于统一机制，而是依赖于多样化的语言技能协调。实验表明，跨任务迁移能力较弱，且任务复杂度在模型层中呈现分层结构。此外，约束满足主要在生成过程中动态监控，而非预生成规划。这些结果表明，指令遵循更倾向于技能组合而非单一抽象过程。

item.title: 基于单点监督的红外小目标检测方法SPIRE
item.content:encoded:
提出SPIRE方法，提升红外小目标检测性能。



摘要： 本文针对红外小目标检测（IRSTD）中目标像素少、边界模糊的问题，提出一种基于单点监督的新型检测方法SPIRE。该方法将IRSTD重新定义为中心点回归任务，通过设计点响应先验监督（PRPS）和高分辨率概率编码器（HRPE），实现无需解码器的端到端回归。SPIRE有效提升了检测精度，降低了误报率和计算成本，并在多个基准数据集上验证了其优越性。

item.title: 面向长图像描述的细粒度幻觉检测基准研究
item.content:encoded:
提出DetailVerifyBench，用于评估长图像描述中的幻觉定位能力。



摘要： 本文针对多模态大语言模型生成的长图像描述中幻觉检测与定位的挑战，提出了一种名为DetailVerifyBench的新基准。该基准包含5个不同领域的1000张高质量图像，平均每段描述超过200词，并提供了密集的、基于token级别的多种幻觉类型标注，是目前最具有挑战性的长图像描述幻觉定位评估基准。该研究旨在提升图像描述系统的可靠性。

item.title: 基于广义贝叶斯推断的数据分布估值框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新型数据分布估值方法，提升数据评估效率。



摘要： 本文研究数据分布估值问题，旨在通过样本量化数据分布的价值。作者提出了一种名为广义贝叶斯估值的框架，利用基于可迁移性度量构建的损失函数进行广义贝叶斯推断。该框架统一解决了诸如标注者评估和数据增强等不同实际问题，并通过贝叶斯原理扩展到连续数据流场景，实验结果验证了其在多种现实场景中的有效性与高效性。

item.title: 融合符号回归与概率方法的动态系统建模框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种结合符号回归与高斯过程的框架，用于建模噪声环境下的动态系统。



摘要： 本文提出了一种混合符号回归与概率机器学习的方法，以在存在噪声的情况下准确推断动态系统的参数和动力学行为。该框架通过结合深度符号回归与基于高斯过程的最大似然估计，分别建模确定性动力学和噪声结构，无需预先假设其函数形式。研究验证了该方法在数值基准测试和实验生物振荡器系统中的有效性，表明其具有数据高效性和对噪声的鲁棒性，适用于需要同时理解系统结构和不确定性的领域。

item.title: 面向代理型AI系统的数字取证研究
item.content:encoded:
研究代理型AI系统在数字取证中的可追溯性与挑战。



摘要： 本文针对代理型AI系统（如OpenClaw）在数字取证中的表现进行实证研究。通过静态代码分析和差分取证方法，识别了代理交互过程中的可恢复痕迹，并对其调查价值进行了系统评估。研究提出了一个代理特征分类体系，揭示了代理执行带来的抽象层和非确定性对取证的挑战。结果表明，大语言模型、执行环境和动态上下文显著影响工具选择和状态转换，这与传统规则软件存在显著差异。该研究为代理型AI系统的系统化取证提供了初步基础，并对未来取证实践和研究具有重要启示。

item.title: Stories of Your Life as Others: A Round-Trip Evaluation of LLM-Generated Life Stories Conditioned on Rich Psychometric Profiles
item.content:encoded: Personality traits are richly encoded in natural language, and large language models (LLMs) trained on human text can simulate personality when conditioned on persona descriptions. However, existing evaluations rely predominantly on questionnaire self-report by the conditioned model, are limited in architectural diversity, and rarely use real human psychometric data. Without addressing these limitations, it remains unclear whether personality conditioning produces psychometrically informative representations of individual differences or merely superficial alignment with trait descriptors. To test how robustly LLMs can encode personality into extended text, we condition LLMs on real psychometric profiles from 290 participants to generate first-person life story narratives, and then task independent LLMs to recover personality scores from those narratives alone. We show that personality scores can be recovered from the generated narratives at levels approaching human test-retest reliability (mean r = 0.750, 85% of the human ceiling), and that recovery is robust across 10 LLM narrative generators and 3 LLM personality scorers spanning 6 providers. Decomposing systematic biases reveals that scoring models achieve their accuracy while counteracting alignment-induced defaults. Content analysis of the generated narratives shows that personality conditioning produces behaviourally differentiated text: nine of ten coded features correlate significantly with the same features in participants' real conversations, and personality-driven emotional reactivity patterns in narratives replicate in real conversational data. These findings provide evidence that the personality-language relationship captured during pretraining supports robust encoding and decoding of individual differences, including characteristic emotional variability patterns that replicate in real human behaviour.

item.title: On Dominant Manifolds in Reservoir Computing Networks
item.content:encoded: Understanding how training shapes the geometry of recurrent network dynamics is a central problem in time-series modeling. We study the emergence of low-dimensional dominant manifolds in the training of Reservoir Computing (RC) networks for temporal forecasting tasks. For a simplified linear and continuous-time reservoir model, we link the dimensionality and structure of the dominant modes directly to the intrinsic dimensionality and information content of the training data. In particular, for training data generated by an autonomous dynamical system, we relate the dominant modes of the trained reservoir to approximations of the Koopman eigenfunctions of the original system, illuminating an explicit connection between reservoir computing and the Dynamic Mode Decomposition algorithm. We illustrate the eigenvalue motion that generates the dominant manifolds during training in simulation, and discuss generalization to nonlinear RC via tangent dynamics and differential p-dominance.
